# linq2db XML Documentation Extract

Generated directly from the current package XML documentation. Use XML member ids for exact API lookup.

## LinqToDB.AnalyticFunctions.FunctionToken

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.AnalyticFunctions.FunctionToken`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Token name for analytic function. Used for resolving method chain.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.ContinueOnCapturedContext

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.ContinueOnCapturedContext`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Obsolete: All s are now awaited using ConfigureAwait(false). Please remove references to this property.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Data.BulkCopyUseConnectionCommandTimeout

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Data.BulkCopyUseConnectionCommandTimeout`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Controls behavior of bulk copy timeout if is not provided. - if - the currently set command timeout on the is used - if false - command timeout is infinite. Default value: false.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Data.ThrowOnDisposed

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Data.ThrowOnDisposed`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Enables throwing of when access disposed instance. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.IsStructIsScalarType

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.IsStructIsScalarType`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: If - structures (except primitive values including enums) will be treated as scalar types during mapping; otherwise they will be treated the same way as classes. Default value: false.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.LinqService.SerializeAssemblyQualifiedName

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.LinqService.SerializeAssemblyQualifiedName`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Controls format of type name, sent over remote context: - if - name from used; - if false - name from used. Default value: false.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.LinqService.ThrowUnresolvedTypeException

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.LinqService.ThrowUnresolvedTypeException`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Controls behavior of Linq To DB, when it cannot load by type name on query deserialization: - if - will be thrown; - if false - type load error will be ignored. Default value: false.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.OptimizeForSequentialAccess

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.OptimizeForSequentialAccess`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Enables mapping expression to be compatible with behavior. Note that it doesn't switch linq2db to use behavior for queries, so this optimization could be used for too. Default value: false.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.SqlServer.UseSchemaOnlyToGetSchema

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.SqlServer.UseSchemaOnlyToGetSchema`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: if set to , SchemaProvider uses to get metadata. Otherwise, the sp_describe_first_result_set sproc is used. Default value: false.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.UseEnumValueNameForStringColumns

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.UseEnumValueNameForStringColumns`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: If - Enum values are stored as by calling ToString(). Default value: .

- Summary: Gets value for option.

## LinqToDB.CompareNulls.LikeClr

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.CompareNulls.LikeClr`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: CLR semantics: nulls values are comparable/equal.

## LinqToDB.CompareNulls.LikeSql

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.CompareNulls.LikeSql`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: SQL semantics: nulls values compare as UNKNOWN (three-valued logic).

## LinqToDB.CompareNulls.LikeSqlExceptParameters

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.CompareNulls.LikeSqlExceptParameters`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: SQL semantics, except null parameters are treated like null constants (they compile to IS NULL).

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType.Default

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType.Default`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: LINQ To DB will select copy method based on current provider. Default method usually set at [PROVIDER_NAME_HERE]Tools.DefaultBulkCopyType.

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType.MultipleRows

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType.MultipleRows`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Data will be inserted into table as a batch insert using INSERT FROM SELECT or similar code. If method not supported, it will be downgraded to method.

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType.ProviderSpecific

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType.ProviderSpecific`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Data will be inserted using native bulk copy functionality if supported. If method not supported, it will be downgraded to method.

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType.RowByRow

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType.RowByRow`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Data will be inserted into table as a sequence of selects, row by row.

## LinqToDB.Data.ConflictAction.Default

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Data.ConflictAction.Default`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Default behavior - conflicts will be handled according to the database's normal behavior (typically will fail).

## LinqToDB.Data.ConflictAction.Ignore

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Data.ConflictAction.Ignore`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Ignore conflicts during bulk copy operation. Supported for following databases: MySql/MariaDB - uses INSERT IGNORE INTO statement (only works with )PostgreSQL - adds ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING clause to INSERT INTO statement (only works with )SQLite - uses INSERT OR IGNORE INTO statement (only works with )

## LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.WriteTraceLine

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.WriteTraceLine`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Trace function. By Default use class for logging, but could be replaced to log e.g. to your log file. will be ignored if is called on builder First parameter contains trace message.Second parameter contains trace message category ().Third parameter contains trace level for message ().Should only not use to write trace lines, only use .

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdIdentifierQuoteMode.Auto

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdIdentifierQuoteMode.Auto`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Quote identifiers if needed. LINQ To DB will quote identifiers, if they are not valid without quotation. This includes: - use of reserved words; - use of any characters except latin letters, digits, _ and $; - use digit, _ or $ as first character. This is default mode. Note that if you need to preserve casing of identifiers, you should use mode. Quoted identifiers not supported by SQL Dialect < 1.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdIdentifierQuoteMode.None

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdIdentifierQuoteMode.None`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Do not quote identifiers. LINQ To DB will not check identifiers for validity (spaces, reserved words) is this mode. This mode should be used only for SQL Dialect < 1 and it is developer's responsibility to ensure that there is no identifiers in use that require quotation.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdIdentifierQuoteMode.Quote

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdIdentifierQuoteMode.Quote`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Always quote identifiers. LINQ To DB will quote all identifiers, even if it is not required. Select this mode, if you need to preserve identifiers casing. Quoted identifiers not supported by SQL Dialect < 1.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.AlternativeBulkCopy.InsertAll

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.AlternativeBulkCopy.InsertAll`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: This mode generates INSERT ALL statement. Note that INSERT ALL doesn't support sequences and will use single generated value for all rows. INSERT ALL INTO target_table VALUES(/*row data*/) ... INTO target_table VALUES(/*row data*/)

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.AlternativeBulkCopy.InsertDual

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.AlternativeBulkCopy.InsertDual`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: This mode generates INSERT ... SELECT statement. INSERT INTO target_table(/*columns*/) SELECT /*row data*/ FROM DUAL UNION ALL ... UNION ALL SELECT /*row data*/ FROM DUAL

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.AlternativeBulkCopy.InsertInto

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.AlternativeBulkCopy.InsertInto`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: This mode performs regular INSERT INTO query with array of values for each column. INSERT INTO target_table(/*columns*/) VALUES(:column1ArrayParameter, ..., :columnXArrayParameter)

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.DistanceMetric.Cosine

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.DistanceMetric.Cosine`
- Kind: Field

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.DistanceMetric.Dot

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.DistanceMetric.Dot`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Dot product-based indication of distance, obtained by calculating the negative dot product. [-∞, +∞] Smaller numbers indicate more similar vectors

- Kind: Field

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.NormType.Norm1`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: The 1-norm, which is the sum of the absolute values of the vector components.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.NormType.Norm2

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.NormType.Norm2`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: The 2-norm, also known as the Euclidean Norm, which is the square root of the sum of the squares of the vector components.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.NormType.NormInf

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.NormType.NormInf`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: The infinity norm, which is the maximum of the absolute values of the vector components.

## LinqToDB.DataType.DateTime2

- Kind: Field

## LinqToDB.DataType.DateTime2Tz

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.DataType.DateTime2Tz`

## LinqToDB.DataType.DateTimeTz

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.DataType.Int64`

## LinqToDB.DataType.Money

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.DataType.Money`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: A currency value ranging from -2 63 (or -9,223,372,036,854,775,808) to 2 63 -1 (or +9,223,372,036,854,775,807) with an accuracy to a ten-thousandth of a currency unit.

## LinqToDB.DataType.NChar

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.DataType.NChar`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: A fixed-length stream of Unicode characters ranging between 1 and 4,000 characters.

## LinqToDB.DataType.NText

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.DataType.NText`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: A variable-length stream of Unicode data with a maximum length of 2 30 - 1 (or 1,073,741,823) characters.

## LinqToDB.DataType.NVarChar

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.DataType.NVarChar`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: A variable-length stream of Unicode characters ranging between 1 and 4,000 characters. Implicit conversion fails if the string is greater than 4,000 characters. Oracle: We need NVarChar2 in order to insert UTF8 string values. The default Odp VarChar2 dbtype doesnt work with UTF8 values. Note : Microsoft oracle client uses NVarChar value by default. Same as VARCHAR2 except that the column stores values in the National CS , ie you can store values in Bangla if your National CS is BN8BSCII .If the National CS is of fixed width CS (all characters are represented by a fixed byte ,say 2 bytes for JA16EUCFIXED) , then NVARCHAR2(30) stores 30 Characters. Varchar2 works with 8 bit characters where as Nvarchar2 works ith 16 bit characters. If you have to store data other than english prefer Nvarchar2 or viceversa. NCHAR and NVARCHAR2 are Unicode datatypes that store Unicode character data. The character set of NCHAR and NVARCHAR2 datatypes can only be either AL16UTF16 or UTF8 and is specified at database creation time as the national character set. AL16UTF16 and UTF8 are both Unicode encoding. The NCHAR datatype stores fixed-length character strings that correspond to the national character set.The NVARCHAR2 datatype stores variable length character strings. When you create a table with an NCHAR or NVARCHAR2 column, the maximum size specified is always in character length semantics. Character length semantics is the default and only length semantics for NCHAR or NVARCHAR2. For example, if national character set is UTF8, then the following statement defines the maximum byte length of 90 bytes: CREATE TABLE tab1 (col1 NCHAR(30)); This statement creates a column with maximum character length of 30. The maximum byte length is the multiple of the maximum character length and the maximum number of bytes in each character. The maximum length of an NVARCHAR2 column is 4000 bytes. It can hold up to 4000 characters. The actual data is subject to the maximum byte limit of 4000. The two size constraints must be satisfied simultaneously at run time.

## LinqToDB.DataType.SByte

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.DataType.SByte`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: An integral type representing signed 8-bit integers with values between -128 and 127.

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.DataType.Timestamp`

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.DataTypeInfo.CreateFormat

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.DataTypeInfo.CreateFormat`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Gets or sets SQL type name template - type name and, optionally, parameters. This template could be used to define column or variable of specific type. E.g. DECIMAL({0}, {1}).

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.DataTypeInfo.CreateParameters

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.DataTypeInfo.CreateParameters`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Gets or sets comma-separated positional list of parameters. E.g. "precision,scale". Order of parameters must match order in .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.DataTypeInfo.DataType

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.DataTypeInfo.DataType`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Gets or sets .NET type name, used by provider for current type.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.DataTypeInfo.ProviderDbType

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.DataTypeInfo.ProviderDbType`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Gets or sets provider-specific type identifier to use for query parameters of this type. Corresponds to some provider's enumeration, e.g. SqlDbType, OracleType, etc.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.DataTypeInfo.ProviderSpecific

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.DataTypeInfo.ProviderSpecific`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Marks provider-specific types.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.DataTypeInfo.TypeName

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.DataTypeInfo.TypeName`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Gets or sets SQL name of data type.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureInfo.CatalogName

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureInfo.CatalogName`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Gets or sets database name for procedure.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureInfo.Description

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureInfo.Description`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Procedure or function description.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureInfo.IsAggregateFunction

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureInfo.IsAggregateFunction`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Gets or sets flag to distinguish aggregate function from other functions.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureInfo.IsDefaultSchema

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureInfo.IsDefaultSchema`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Get or sets flag, indicating that procedure belongs to default schema/owner.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureInfo.IsFunction

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureInfo.IsFunction`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Gets or sets flag to distinguish function from procedure.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureInfo.IsResultDynamic

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureInfo.IsResultDynamic`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Get or sets flag, indicating that procedure returns dynamic (generic) result.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureInfo.IsTableFunction

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureInfo.IsTableFunction`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Gets or sets flag to distinguish table function from other functions.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureInfo.IsWindowFunction

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureInfo.IsWindowFunction`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Gets or sets flag to distinguish window function from other functions.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureInfo.PackageName

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureInfo.PackageName`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Gets or sets package/module/library name for procedure.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureInfo.ProcedureDefinition

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureInfo.ProcedureDefinition`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Gets or sets procedure source code.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureInfo.ProcedureID

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureInfo.ProcedureID`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Gets or sets unique procedure identifier. NOTE: this is not fully-qualified procedure name (even if it used right now for some providers as procedure identifier).

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureInfo.ProcedureName

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureInfo.ProcedureName`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Gets or sets procedure name.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureInfo.SchemaName

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureInfo.SchemaName`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Gets or sets schema/owner name for procedure.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureParameterInfo.DataType

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureParameterInfo.DataType`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Get or sets database type for parameter.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureParameterInfo.DataTypeExact

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureParameterInfo.DataTypeExact`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Get or sets exact database type for parameter.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureParameterInfo.Description

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureParameterInfo.Description`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Parameter's description.

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureParameterInfo.IsNullable`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Gets flag indicating that it is nullable parameter.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureParameterInfo.IsOut

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureParameterInfo.IsOut`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Gets or sets output or input-output parameter flag.

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureParameterInfo.IsResult`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Gets or sets return value parameter flag.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureParameterInfo.Length

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureParameterInfo.Length`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Gets or sets parameter type length attribute.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureParameterInfo.Ordinal

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureParameterInfo.Ordinal`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Gets or sets parameter position.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureParameterInfo.ParameterName

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureParameterInfo.ParameterName`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Gets or sets parameter name.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureParameterInfo.Precision

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureParameterInfo.Precision`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Gets or sets parameter type precision attribute.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureParameterInfo.ProcedureID

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureParameterInfo.ProcedureID`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Gets or sets unique procedure identifier. NOTE: this is not fully-qualified procedure name (even if it used right now for some providers as procedure identifier).

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureParameterInfo.Scale

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureParameterInfo.Scale`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Gets or sets parameter type scale attribute.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureParameterInfo.UDTCatalog

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SchemaProvider.ProcedureParameterInfo.UDTCatalog`
- Kind: Field

- Kind: Field

- Kind: Field
- Summary: Parameter's user-defined type(UDT) schema/owner.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.ConcatBuildStyle.Function

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.ConcatBuildStyle.Function`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: CONCAT(a, b, c) — MySQL, ClickHouse.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.ConcatBuildStyle.Pipes

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.ConcatBuildStyle.Pipes`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: a || b || c — ANSI-SQL standard; PostgreSQL, Oracle, SQLite, DB2, Firebird, Informix, SAP HANA, DuckDB, Sybase ASE, SQL Server 2025+.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.ConcatBuildStyle.Plus

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.ConcatBuildStyle.Plus`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: a + b + c — SQL Server pre-2025, SqlCe, Access.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.ExceptionToErrorMessage

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.ExceptionToErrorMessage`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Gets error message from a native exception. For example: SqlException -> SqlException.Message, OleDbException -> OleDbException.Errors[0].Message

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.ExceptionToErrorNumber

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.ExceptionToErrorNumber`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Gets error number from a native exception. For example: SqlException -> SqlException.Number, OleDbException -> OleDbException.Errors[0].NativeError

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.NameToCommandParameter

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.NameToCommandParameter`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Provided name should be converted to command parameter name. For example: firstName -> @firstName for the following query: db.Parameter("@firstName") = "John"; ^ here

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.NameToCteName

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.NameToCteName`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Provided name should be converted to CTE name. For example: Cte -> [Cte] for the following query: WITH [Cte] AS (....) SELECT * FROM [Cte] ^ add ^

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.NameToDatabase

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.NameToDatabase`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Provided name should be converted to query database. For example: MyDatabase -> [MyDatabase] for the following query: SELECT * FROM [MyDatabase]..[Person] ^ add ^

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.NameToPackage

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.NameToPackage`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Provided name should be converted to package/module/library name.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.NameToProcedure

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.NameToProcedure`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Provided name should be converted to function/procedure name.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.NameToQueryField

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.NameToQueryField`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Provided name should be converted to query field name. For example: FirstName -> [FirstName] for the following query: SELECT [FirstName] FROM Person WHERE ID = 1 ^ add ^

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.NameToQueryFieldAlias

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.NameToQueryFieldAlias`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Provided name should be converted to query field alias. For example: ID -> "ID" for the following query: SELECT "ID" as "ID" FROM Person WHERE "ID" = 1 ^ ^ here

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.NameToQueryParameter

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.NameToQueryParameter`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Provided name should be converted to query parameter name. For example: firstName -> @firstName for the following query: SELECT * FROM Person WHERE FirstName = @firstName ^ here

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.NameToQueryTable

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.NameToQueryTable`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Provided name should be converted to query table name. For example: Person -> [Person] for the following query: SELECT * FROM [Person] ^ add ^

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.NameToQueryTableAlias

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.NameToQueryTableAlias`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Provided name should be converted to query table alias. For example: table1 -> [table1] for the following query: SELECT * FROM [Person] [table1] ^ add ^

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.NameToSchema

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.NameToSchema`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Provided name should be converted to query schema/owner. For example: dbo -> [dbo] for the following query: SELECT * FROM [ dbo ].[Person] ^ add ^

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.NameToServer

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.NameToServer`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Provided name should be converted to linked server name. For example: host name\named instance -> [host name\named instance] for the following query: SELECT * FROM [host name\named instance]..[Person] ^ add ^

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.NameToSprocParameter

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.NameToSprocParameter`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Provided name should be converted to stored procedure parameter name. For example: firstName -> @firstName for the following query: db.Parameter("@firstName") = "John"; ^ here

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.SequenceName

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.SequenceName`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Provided name should be converted to sequence name.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.SprocParameterToName

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.SprocParameterToName`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Provided stored procedure parameter name should be converted to name. For example: @firstName -> firstName for the following query: db.Parameter("@firstName") = "John"; ^ '@' has to be removed

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.TriggerName

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.ConvertType.TriggerName`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Provided name should be converted to trigger name.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.RowFeature.Between

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.RowFeature.Between`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Provider supports BETWEEN operator for tuples.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.RowFeature.CompareToSelect

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.RowFeature.CompareToSelect`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Provider supports subqueries in tuple constructor: (SELECT 1, 2).

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.RowFeature.Comparisons

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.RowFeature.Comparisons`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Provider supports comparison operators for tuples: >, >=, <<=.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.RowFeature.Equality

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.RowFeature.Equality`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Provider supports equality (=, <>) operators with tuples.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.RowFeature.In

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.RowFeature.In`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Provider supports tuples with IN operator: (1, 2) IN ((1, 2), (3, 4)).

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.RowFeature.IsNull

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.RowFeature.IsNull`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Provider supports for IS NULL operator: (1, 2) IS NULL.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.RowFeature.Overlaps

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.RowFeature.Overlaps`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Provider supports OVERLAPS operator.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.RowFeature.Update

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.RowFeature.Update`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Provider supports tuples in SET clause with non-literal rvalue: UPDATE T SET (COL1, COL2) = (SELECT 1, 2).

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.RowFeature.UpdateLiteral

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.RowFeature.UpdateLiteral`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Provider supports tuples in SET clause with rvalue literal: UPDATE T SET (COL1, COL2) = (1, 2).

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.CteAnnotationNames.Materialized

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.CteAnnotationNames.Materialized`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Boolean. When emits AS MATERIALIZED, when emits AS NOT MATERIALIZED. Currently recognized by PostgreSQL 12+, SQLite 3.35+, and ClickHouse 26.3+.
- Remarks: Expected value type is . Annotation values participate in both query-cache keying (via and the -marked ) and LinqService round-trips, so values must hash stably and serialize via the standard SerializationConverter. Non-primitive values are not supported today.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.JoinType.Auto

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.JoinType.Auto`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Don't use this type. It defines intermediate join type calculated by SelectManyBuilder and shouldn't be used outside of this class.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.JoinType.Cross

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.JoinType.Cross`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: CROSS JOIN.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.JoinType.CrossApply

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.JoinType.CrossApply`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: CROSS APPLY.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.JoinType.Full

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.JoinType.Full`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: FULL (OUTER) JOIN.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.JoinType.FullApply

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.JoinType.FullApply`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Intermediate fake JOIN type, added by SelectManyBuilder and replaced with real type in query optimizer.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.JoinType.Inner

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.JoinType.Inner`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: INNER JOIN. Also represents CROSS JOIN when join conditions not set.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.JoinType.Left

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.JoinType.Left`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: LEFT (OUTER) JOIN.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.JoinType.OuterApply

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.JoinType.OuterApply`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: OUTER APPLY.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.JoinType.Right

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.JoinType.Right`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: RIGHT (OUTER) JOIN.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.JoinType.RightApply

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.JoinType.RightApply`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Intermediate fake JOIN type, added by SelectManyBuilder and replaced with real type in query optimizer.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.NullsDefaultOrdering.AlwaysFirst

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.NullsDefaultOrdering.AlwaysFirst`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: NULL always sorts first, regardless of sort direction.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.NullsDefaultOrdering.AlwaysLast

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.NullsDefaultOrdering.AlwaysLast`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: NULL always sorts last, regardless of sort direction (e.g. ClickHouse).

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.NullsDefaultOrdering.Largest

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.NullsDefaultOrdering.Largest`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: NULL sorts as the largest value: ascending ⇒ NULLS LAST, descending ⇒ NULLS FIRST (e.g. Oracle, PostgreSQL, DB2).

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.NullsDefaultOrdering.Smallest

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.NullsDefaultOrdering.Smallest`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: NULL sorts as the smallest value: ascending ⇒ NULLS FIRST, descending ⇒ NULLS LAST (e.g. SQL Server, MySQL, SQLite, Firebird 2.0+).

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.NullsDefaultOrdering.Unknown

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.NullsDefaultOrdering.Unknown`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: The natural placement is unknown — a requested is always honored and never elided.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.ParametersNullabilityType.IfAllParametersNullable

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.ParametersNullabilityType.IfAllParametersNullable`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Expression could return NULL if all parameters of expression could contain NULL.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.ParametersNullabilityType.IfAnyParameterNullable

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.ParametersNullabilityType.IfAnyParameterNullable`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Expression could return NULL if at least one parameter of expression could contain NULL.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.ParametersNullabilityType.NotNullable

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.ParametersNullabilityType.NotNullable`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Expression never returns NULL.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.ParametersNullabilityType.Nullable

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.ParametersNullabilityType.Nullable`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Expression could always return NULL.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.ParametersNullabilityType.SameAsFirstOrSecondParameter

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.ParametersNullabilityType.SameAsFirstOrSecondParameter`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Expression could return NULL if first or second parameters of expression could contain NULL.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.ParametersNullabilityType.SameAsFirstParameter

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.ParametersNullabilityType.SameAsFirstParameter`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Expression could return NULL if first parameter of expression could contain NULL.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.ParametersNullabilityType.SameAsLastParameter

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.ParametersNullabilityType.SameAsLastParameter`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Expression could return NULL if last parameter of expression could contain NULL.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.ParametersNullabilityType.SameAsSecondParameter

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.ParametersNullabilityType.SameAsSecondParameter`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Expression could return NULL if second parameter of expression could contain NULL.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.ParametersNullabilityType.SameAsThirdParameter

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.ParametersNullabilityType.SameAsThirdParameter`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Expression could return NULL if third parameter of expression could contain NULL.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.ParametersNullabilityType.Undefined

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.ParametersNullabilityType.Undefined`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Nullability not specified, and other sources (like or return type) will be used to identify nullability.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.PseudoFunctions.CONVERT_FORMAT

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.PseudoFunctions.CONVERT_FORMAT`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Function to convert value from one type to another: CONVERT(to_type, from_type, value) { CanBeNull = value.CanBeNull }

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.PseudoFunctions.LENGTH

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.PseudoFunctions.LENGTH`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Function for calculating length of string: LENGTH(value)

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.PseudoFunctions.REMOVE_CONVERT

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.PseudoFunctions.REMOVE_CONVERT`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Function to suppress conversion SQL generation for provided value: REMOVE_CONVERT(value, resultType)

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.PseudoFunctions.REPLACE

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.PseudoFunctions.REPLACE`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Function to replace one text fragment with another in string: REPLACE(value, oldSubstring, newSubstring)

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.PseudoFunctions.TO_LOWER

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.PseudoFunctions.TO_LOWER`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Function to convert text parameter to lowercased form: TO_LOWER(string)

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.PseudoFunctions.TO_UPPER

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.PseudoFunctions.TO_UPPER`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Function to convert text parameter to uppercased form: TO_UPPER(string)

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.PseudoFunctions.TRY_CONVERT

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.PseudoFunctions.TRY_CONVERT`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Function to convert value from one type to another: TRY_CONVERT(to_type, from_type, value) { CanBeNull = true }. Returns NULL on conversion failure.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.PseudoFunctions.TRY_CONVERT_OR_DEFAULT

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.PseudoFunctions.TRY_CONVERT_OR_DEFAULT`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Function to convert value from one type to another: TRY_CONVERT_OR_DEFAULT(to_type, from_type, value, defaultValue) { CanBeNull = value.CanBeNull || defaultValue.CanBeNull }. Returns provided default value on conversion failure.

- Kind: Field

## LinqToDB.SchemaProvider.GetSchemaOptions.GetAssociationMemberName

- Kind: Field
- Summary: Optional custom name generation logic for association property.

## LinqToDB.SchemaProvider.GetSchemaOptions.GetForeignKeys

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.SchemaProvider.GetSchemaOptions.GetForeignKeys`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Enable or disable read of foreign keys. Default - enabled (). Disabe could be useful at least for Access, as it could crash on some database files.

## LinqToDB.SchemaProvider.GetSchemaOptions.GetProcedures

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.SchemaProvider.GetSchemaOptions.GetProcedures`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Enable or disable read of procedures and functions metadata. Default - enabled ().

## LinqToDB.SchemaProvider.GetSchemaOptions.GetTables

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.SchemaProvider.GetSchemaOptions.GetTables`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Enable or disable read of table schema. Default - enabled ().

## LinqToDB.SchemaProvider.GetSchemaOptions.IgnoreSystemHistoryTables

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.SchemaProvider.GetSchemaOptions.IgnoreSystemHistoryTables`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Only for SQL Server. Doesn't return history table schema for temporal tables.

## LinqToDB.SchemaProvider.GetSchemaOptions.IncludedCatalogs

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.SchemaProvider.GetSchemaOptions.IncludedCatalogs`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: List of allowed databases/catalogs.

## LinqToDB.SchemaProvider.GetSchemaOptions.IncludedSchemas

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.SchemaProvider.GetSchemaOptions.IncludedSchemas`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: List of allowed schemas/owners.

## LinqToDB.SchemaProvider.GetSchemaOptions.LoadProcedure

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.SchemaProvider.GetSchemaOptions.LoadProcedure`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Optional procedure metadata load filter. By default all procedures loaded.

## LinqToDB.SchemaProvider.GetSchemaOptions.LoadTable

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.SchemaProvider.GetSchemaOptions.LoadTable`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Optinal callback to filter loaded tables. receives object with table details and return boolean flag to indicate that table should be loaded () or skipped (false).

## LinqToDB.SchemaProvider.GetSchemaOptions.PreferProviderSpecificTypes

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.SchemaProvider.GetSchemaOptions.PreferProviderSpecificTypes`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: When set to true, will prefer generation of provider-specific types instead of general types.

## LinqToDB.SchemaProvider.GetSchemaOptions.ProcedureLoadingProgress

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.SchemaProvider.GetSchemaOptions.ProcedureLoadingProgress`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Optional callback to report procedure metadata load progress. First parameter contains total number of discovered procedures. Second parameter provides position of currently loaded procedure.

## LinqToDB.SchemaProvider.GetSchemaOptions.StringComparer

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.SchemaProvider.GetSchemaOptions.StringComparer`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: String comparison logic for , , and values. Default is .

## LinqToDB.SchemaProvider.GetSchemaOptions.UseSchemaOnly

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.SchemaProvider.GetSchemaOptions.UseSchemaOnly`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: if set to true, SchemaProvider uses to get SqlServer metadata. Otherwise the sp_describe_first_result_set sproc is used.

## LinqToDB.Sql.DateParts.Week

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Sql.DateParts.Week`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: This date part behavior depends on used database and also depends on where if calculated - in C# code or in database. Eeach database could have own week numbering logic, see notes below. Current implementation uses following schemas per-provider: C# evaluation: CultureInfo.CurrentCulture.Calendar.GetWeekOfYear(date.Value, CalendarWeekRule.FirstDay, DayOfWeek.Sunday) Databases: US numbering schema used by: MS AccessSQL CESQL ServerSAP/Sybase ASEInformixYdbUS 0-based numbering schema used by MySQL databaseISO numbering schema with incorrect numbering of first week used by SAP HANA databaseISO numbering schema with proper numbering of first week used by: FirebirdPostgreSQLClickHousePrimitive (each 7 days counted as week) numbering schema: DB2OracleSQLite numbering logic cannot be classified by human being

## LinqToDB.Sql.IsNullableType.IfAnyParameterNullable

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Sql.IsNullableType.IfAnyParameterNullable`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Expression could return NULL if at least one parameter of expression could contain NULL.

## LinqToDB.Sql.IsNullableType.NotNullable

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Sql.IsNullableType.NotNullable`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Expression never returns NULL.

## LinqToDB.Sql.IsNullableType.Nullable

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Sql.IsNullableType.Nullable`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Expression could always return NULL.

## LinqToDB.Sql.IsNullableType.SameAsFirstOrSecondParameter

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Sql.IsNullableType.SameAsFirstOrSecondParameter`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Expression could return NULL if first or second parameters of expression could contain NULL.

## LinqToDB.Sql.IsNullableType.SameAsFirstParameter

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Sql.IsNullableType.SameAsFirstParameter`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Expression could return NULL if first parameter of expression could contain NULL.

## LinqToDB.Sql.IsNullableType.SameAsLastParameter

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Sql.IsNullableType.SameAsLastParameter`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Expression could return NULL if last parameter of expression could contain NULL.

## LinqToDB.Sql.IsNullableType.SameAsSecondParameter

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Sql.IsNullableType.SameAsSecondParameter`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Expression could return NULL if second parameter of expression could contain NULL.

## LinqToDB.Sql.IsNullableType.SameAsThirdParameter

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Sql.IsNullableType.SameAsThirdParameter`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Expression could return NULL if third parameter of expression could contain NULL.

## LinqToDB.Sql.IsNullableType.Undefined

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Sql.IsNullableType.Undefined`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Nullability not specified, and other sources (like or return type) will be used to identify nullability.

## LinqToDB.Sql.QueryExtensionScope.IndexHint

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Sql.QueryExtensionScope.IndexHint`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Extension will be applied to table as index hint.

## LinqToDB.Sql.QueryExtensionScope.JoinHint

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Sql.QueryExtensionScope.JoinHint`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Extension will be applied to join as join hint/extension.

## LinqToDB.Sql.QueryExtensionScope.None

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Sql.QueryExtensionScope.None`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: No scope. Could be used with builder to skip extension processing by Linq To DB for specific configuration.

## LinqToDB.Sql.QueryExtensionScope.QueryHint

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Sql.QueryExtensionScope.QueryHint`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Extension will be applied to query as query hint.

## LinqToDB.Sql.QueryExtensionScope.SubQueryHint

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Sql.QueryExtensionScope.SubQueryHint`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Extension will be applied to subquery as subquery hint.

## LinqToDB.Sql.QueryExtensionScope.TableHint

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Sql.QueryExtensionScope.TableHint`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Extension will be applied to table as table hint.

## LinqToDB.Sql.QueryExtensionScope.TableNameHint

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Sql.QueryExtensionScope.TableNameHint`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Extension will be applied to table name (e.g. temporal table modifiers).

## LinqToDB.Sql.QueryExtensionScope.TablesInScopeHint

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.Sql.QueryExtensionScope.TablesInScopeHint`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Extension will be applied to all tables in current (sub)query as table hint.

- Summary: Resolve the generated SQL table specification/path for the table source.

## LinqToDB.SqlQuery.DefaultNullable.None

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.SqlQuery.DefaultNullable.None`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: No defaults available, both NULL and NOT NULL should be generated always.

## LinqToDB.SqlQuery.DefaultNullable.NotNull

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.SqlQuery.DefaultNullable.NotNull`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Generate NULL only, NOT NULL is default behavior.

## LinqToDB.SqlQuery.DefaultNullable.Null

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.SqlQuery.DefaultNullable.Null`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Generate NOT NULL only, NULL is default behavior.

## LinqToDB.SqlQuery.MultiInsertType.All

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.SqlQuery.MultiInsertType.All`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: INSERT ALL operation with WHEN conditions.

## LinqToDB.SqlQuery.MultiInsertType.First

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.SqlQuery.MultiInsertType.First`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: INSERT FIRST operation.

## LinqToDB.SqlQuery.MultiInsertType.Unconditional

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.SqlQuery.MultiInsertType.Unconditional`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: INSERT ALL operation without WHEN conditions.

## LinqToDB.SqlQuery.Precedence.Concatenate

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.SqlQuery.Precedence.Concatenate`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Conservative low-binding precedence for SQL concat (|| / CONCAT(...)). Real per-provider || precedence varies (e.g. SQLite documents it between unary and multiplicative; Oracle places it at additive level). Setting it below every other operator forces parentheses around concat chains whenever they're nested in another operator — defensive over that variance.

## LinqToDB.TableOptions.CheckExistence

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.TableOptions.CheckExistence`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Convenience flag: | .

## LinqToDB.TableOptions.CreateIfNotExists

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.TableOptions.CreateIfNotExists`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Use CREATE ... IF NOT EXISTS when supported by the provider.
- Remarks: Supported by: DB2, Firebird, Informix, MySql, Oracle, PostgreSQL, SQLite, SQL Server, Sybase ASE.

## LinqToDB.TableOptions.DropIfExists

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.TableOptions.DropIfExists`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Use DROP ... IF EXISTS when supported by the provider.
- Remarks: Supported by: DB2, Firebird, Informix, MySql, Oracle, PostgreSQL, SQLite, SQL Server, Sybase ASE.

## LinqToDB.TableOptions.IsGlobalTemporaryData

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.TableOptions.IsGlobalTemporaryData`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Temporary table with data visibility semantics defined as "global" in the provider abstraction.
- Remarks: The exact meaning of "global" data visibility is provider-specific. In some databases (e.g., Oracle), data in global temporary tables remains session- or transaction-scoped despite globally visible structure. Supported by: DB2, Firebird, Oracle, SAP Hana, SQL Server, Sybase ASE.

## LinqToDB.TableOptions.IsGlobalTemporaryStructure

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.TableOptions.IsGlobalTemporaryStructure`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Temporary table with globally visible structure (DDL-level visibility), when supported by the provider.
- Remarks: Structure visibility does not imply shared data visibility. Actual data scope is provider-specific. Supported by: DB2, Firebird, Oracle, SAP Hana, SQL Server, Sybase ASE.

## LinqToDB.TableOptions.IsLocalTemporaryData

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.TableOptions.IsLocalTemporaryData`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Temporary table with session-scoped data visibility, when supported by the provider.
- Remarks: Data is not visible to other sessions. Supported by: DB2, Informix, MySql, PostgreSQL, SQLite, SAP Hana, SQL Server, Sybase ASE.

## LinqToDB.TableOptions.IsLocalTemporaryStructure

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.TableOptions.IsLocalTemporaryStructure`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Temporary table with session-scoped structure (DDL-level visibility limited to the session), when supported by the provider.
- Remarks: Structure visibility does not define data visibility semantics. Supported by: DB2, Informix, MySql, PostgreSQL, SQLite, SAP Hana, SQL Server, Sybase ASE.

## LinqToDB.TableOptions.IsTemporary

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.TableOptions.IsTemporary`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Source is a temporary table (not visible to other sessions) when supported.
- Remarks: If a provider supports both global and local temporary tables, a local temporary table is used. Supported by: DB2, Firebird, Informix, MySql, Oracle, PostgreSQL, SQLite, SQL Server, SAP Hana, Sybase ASE.

## LinqToDB.TableOptions.IsTemporaryOptionSet

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.TableOptions.IsTemporaryOptionSet`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Convenience flag: any temporary-related option is set.

## LinqToDB.TableOptions.IsTransactionTemporaryData

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.TableOptions.IsTransactionTemporaryData`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Temporary table with transaction-scoped data visibility, when supported by the provider.
- Remarks: Rows are visible only within the current transaction. Supported by: Firebird, Oracle, PostgreSQL.

## LinqToDB.TableOptions.None

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.TableOptions.None`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Explicitly specifies that no special table options apply.
- Remarks: Means "specified as none": disables option-driven table behavior. The effective SQL is still provider- and mapping-dependent, but without additional behavior implied by flags.

## LinqToDB.TableOptions.NotSet

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.TableOptions.NotSet`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: No option value specified: do not override mapped or operation-level table options. Providers interpret the resulting concrete flags; they do not replace this value with provider defaults.
- Remarks: Means "unspecified": the current table option value is not overridden. Effective behavior is determined by mapping or API-level defaults for the specific operation. Providers interpret the resulting concrete flags; they do not replace with provider defaults.

## LinqToDB.TakeHints.Percent

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.TakeHints.Percent`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: SELECT TOP 10 PERCENT.

## LinqToDB.TakeHints.WithTies

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.TakeHints.WithTies`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: SELECT TOP 10 WITH TIES.

## LinqToDB.TempTable<T>.TotalCopied

- XML member: `F:LinqToDB.TempTable`1.TotalCopied`
- Kind: Field
- Summary: Gets total number of records, inserted into table using BulkCopy.

## LinqToDB.Async.AsyncExtensions.AsAsyncEnumerable<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.Async.AsyncExtensions.AsAsyncEnumerable``1(System.Linq.IQueryable{``0})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Returns an that can be enumerated asynchronously.
- Type parameters:
  - `TSource`: Source sequence element type.
- Parameters:
  - `source`: Source sequence.
- Returns: A query that can be enumerated asynchronously.

## LinqToDB.Async.AsyncExtensions.ForEachAsync<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.Async.AsyncExtensions.ForEachAsync``1(System.Linq.IQueryable{``0},System.Action{``0},System.Threading.CancellationToken)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Asynchronously apply provided action to each element in source sequence. Sequence elements processed sequentially.
- Type parameters:
  - `TSource`: Source sequence element type.
- Parameters:
  - `source`: Source sequence.
  - `action`: Action to apply to each sequence element.
  - `token`: Optional asynchronous operation cancellation token.
- Returns: Asynchronous operation completion task.

## LinqToDB.Async.AsyncExtensions.ForEachUntilAsync<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.Async.AsyncExtensions.ForEachUntilAsync``1(System.Linq.IQueryable{``0},System.Func{``0,System.Boolean},System.Threading.CancellationToken)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Asynchronously apply provided function to each element in source sequence sequentially. Sequence enumeration stops if function returns .
- Type parameters:
  - `TSource`: Source sequence element type.
- Parameters:
  - `source`: Source sequence.
  - `func`: Function to apply to each sequence element. Returning from function will stop numeration.
  - `token`: Optional asynchronous operation cancellation token.
- Returns: Asynchronous operation completion task.

## LinqToDB.Async.AsyncExtensions.ToArrayAsync<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.Async.AsyncExtensions.ToArrayAsync``1(System.Linq.IQueryable{``0},System.Threading.CancellationToken)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Asynchronously loads data from query to an array.
- Type parameters:
  - `TSource`: Query element type.
- Parameters:
  - `source`: Source query.
  - `token`: Optional asynchronous operation cancellation token.
- Returns: Array with query results.

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.Async.AsyncExtensions.ToDictionaryAsync``3(System.Linq.IQueryable{``0},System.Func{``0,``1},System.Func{``0,``2},System.Threading.CancellationToken)`

## LinqToDB.Async.AsyncExtensions.ToListAsync<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.Async.AsyncExtensions.ToListAsync``1(System.Linq.IQueryable{``0},System.Threading.CancellationToken)`

## LinqToDB.Async.AsyncExtensions.ToLookupAsync<T1,T2>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.Async.AsyncExtensions.ToLookupAsync``2(System.Linq.IQueryable{``0},System.Func{``0,``1},System.Collections.Generic.IEqualityComparer{``1},System.Threading.CancellationToken)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Asynchronously creates a from an according to a specified key selector function.
- Type parameters:
  - `TSource`: The type of the elements of source.
  - `TKey`: The type of the key returned by keySelector.
- Parameters:
  - `source`: The to create a from.
  - `keySelector`: A function to extract a key from each element.
  - `comparer`: An to compare keys.
  - `token`: Optional asynchronous operation cancellation token.
- Returns: A that contains keys and values. The values within each group are in the same order as in source.

## LinqToDB.Async.AsyncExtensions.ToLookupAsync<T1,T2>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.Async.AsyncExtensions.ToLookupAsync``2(System.Linq.IQueryable{``0},System.Func{``0,``1},System.Threading.CancellationToken)`
- Kind: Method

- Type parameters:
  - `TSource`: The type of the elements of source.
  - `TKey`: The type of the key returned by keySelector.
- Parameters:
  - `source`: The to create a from.
  - `keySelector`: A function to extract a key from each element.
  - `token`: Optional asynchronous operation cancellation token.
- Returns: A that contains keys and values. The values within each group are in the same order as in source.

## LinqToDB.Concurrency.ConcurrencyExtensions.DeleteOptimistic<T>

  - `obj`: Entity instance to delete.
- Returns: Number of deleted records.

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.Concurrency.ConcurrencyExtensions.WhereKeyOptimistic``1(System.Linq.IQueryable{``0},``0)`
- Kind: Method

  - `obj`: Entity instance to take current lock field value from.
- Returns: Query with filter over lock field.

## LinqToDB.CreateTableOptions.Constructor

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.CreateTableOptions.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,LinqToDB.TableOptions,LinqToDB.SqlQuery.DefaultNullable)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Options for creating a table.
- Parameters:
  - `TableName`: Optional name of table. If not specified, value from mapping will be used.
  - `DatabaseName`: Optional name of table's database. If not specified, value from mapping will be used.
  - `SchemaName`: Optional name of table schema/owner. If not specified, value from mapping will be used.
  - `ServerName`: Optional name of linked server. If not specified, value from mapping will be used.
  - `StatementHeader`: Replacement for "CREATE TABLE table_name" header. Header is a template with {0} parameter for table name.
  - `StatementFooter`: SQL appended to the generated create table statement.
  - `TableOptions`: Optional Table options. Default is .
  - `DefaultNullable`: Defines how columns nullability flag should be generated: - - generate only NOT NULL for non-nullable fields. Missing nullability information treated as NULL by database. - - generate only NULL for nullable fields. Missing nullability information treated as NOT NULL by database. - - explicitly generate NULL and NOT NULL for all columns. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.CreateTempTableOptions.Constructor

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.CreateTempTableOptions.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,LinqToDB.TableOptions,LinqToDB.SqlQuery.DefaultNullable)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Options for creating a temporary table.
- Parameters:
  - `TableName`: Optional name of temporary table. If not specified, value from mapping will be used.
  - `DatabaseName`: Optional name of table's database. If not specified, value from mapping will be used.
  - `SchemaName`: Optional name of table schema/owner. If not specified, value from mapping will be used.
  - `ServerName`: Optional name of linked server. If not specified, value from mapping will be used.
  - `StatementHeader`: Replacement for "CREATE TABLE table_name" header. Header is a template with {0} parameter for table name.
  - `StatementFooter`: SQL appended to the generated create table statement.
  - `TableOptions`: Optional Table options. Default is .
  - `DefaultNullable`: Defines how columns nullability flag should be generated: - - generate only NOT NULL for non-nullable fields. Missing nullability information treated as NULL by database. - - generate only NULL for nullable fields. Missing nullability information treated as NOT NULL by database. - - explicitly generate NULL and NOT NULL for all columns. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.CteBuilderExtensions.IsMaterialized

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.CteBuilderExtensions.IsMaterialized(LinqToDB.ICteBuilder,System.Boolean)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Sets the MATERIALIZED / NOT MATERIALIZED CTE hint. Recognized by PostgreSQL 12+, SQLite 3.35+, and ClickHouse 26.3+. Other providers silently drop the hint without tracing — if you rely on the hint for performance, be aware it is a no-op on those providers.
- Parameters:
  - `cteBuilder`: Builder provided by the AsCte callback.
  - `materialized`: to emit AS MATERIALIZED, to emit AS NOT MATERIALIZED.
- Returns: The same builder for chaining.

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.Constructor

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.#ctor(System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32},LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType,System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,LinqToDB.TableOptions,System.Int32,System.Action{LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyRowsCopied},System.Boolean,System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Boolean,LinqToDB.Data.ConflictAction)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Defines behavior of method.
- Remarks: Pass an instance to the BulkCopy / BulkCopyAsync overloads to control batch size, timeouts, identity handling, conflict resolution, and provider-specific features. The parameter selects the insert strategy: — provider chooses the most efficient path. — uses the provider's native bulk API when available. — generates multi-row INSERT … VALUES statements. — inserts one row per statement (slowest; most compatible). Many parameters (e.g., , , ) are only honoured for mode on supporting providers. See individual parameter documentation for provider support details.
- Parameters:
  - `MaxBatchSize`: Number of rows in each batch. At the end of each batch, the rows in the batch are sent to the server. Returns an integer value or zero if no value has been set.
  - `BulkCopyTimeout`: Number of seconds for the operation to complete before it times out. For native bulk copy this option works only for providers that support it: DB2InformixMySql/MariaDB using MySqlConnector providerOraclePostgreSQLSAP HANASQL ServerSAP/Sybase ASE
  - `BulkCopyType`: Default bulk copy mode, used by methods, if mode is not specified explicitly.
  - `CheckConstraints`: Enables database constrains enforcement during bulk copy operation. Supported with bulk copy mode for following databases: OracleSQL ServerSAP/Sybase ASE
  - `KeepIdentity`: If this option set to true, bulk copy will use values of columns, marked with IsIdentity flag. SkipOnInsert flag in this case will be ignored. Otherwise those columns will be skipped and values will be generated by server. Not compatible with mode.
  - `TableLock`: Applies table lock during bulk copy operation. Supported with bulk copy mode for following databases: DB2Informix (using DB2 provider)SQL ServerSAP/Sybase ASE
  - `KeepNulls`: Enables instert of NULL values instead of values from colum default constraint during bulk copy operation. Supported with bulk copy mode for following databases: SQL ServerSAP/Sybase ASE
  - `FireTriggers`: Enables insert triggers during bulk copy operation. Supported with bulk copy mode for following databases: OracleSQL ServerSAP/Sybase ASE
  - `UseInternalTransaction`: Enables automatic transaction creation during bulk copy operation. Supported with bulk copy mode for following databases: OracleSQL ServerSAP/Sybase ASE
  - `ServerName`: Gets or sets explicit name of target server instead of one, configured for copied entity in mapping schema. See method for support information per provider. Also note that it is not supported by provider-specific insert method.
  - `DatabaseName`: Gets or sets explicit name of target database instead of one, configured for copied entity in mapping schema. See method for support information per provider.
  - `SchemaName`: Gets or sets explicit name of target schema/owner instead of one, configured for copied entity in mapping schema. See method for support information per provider.
  - `TableName`: Gets or sets explicit name of target table instead of one, configured for copied entity in mapping schema.
  - `TableOptions`: Gets or sets flags overrides instead of configured for copied entity in mapping schema. See method for support information per provider.
  - `NotifyAfter`: Gets or sets counter after how many copied records should be called. E.g. if you set it to 10, callback will be called after each 10 copied records. To disable callback, set this option to 0 (default value).
  - `RowsCopiedCallback`: Gets or sets callback method that will be called by BulkCopy operation after each rows copied. This callback will not be used if set to 0.
  - `UseParameters`: Gets or sets whether to always use Parameters for MultipleRowsCopy. Default is false. If True, provider-specific parameter limit per batch will be used to determine the maximum number of rows per insert, Unless overridden by .
  - `MaxParametersForBatch`: If set, will set the maximum parameters per batch statement. Also see .
  - `MaxDegreeOfParallelism`: Implemented only by ClickHouse.Driver provider. Defines number of connections, used for parallel insert in mode.
  - `WithoutSession`: Implemented only by ClickHouse.Driver provider. When set, provider-specific bulk copy will use session-less connection even if called over connection with session. Note that session-less connections cannot be used with session-bound functionality like temporary tables.
  - `ConflictAction`: Specifies the action to take when conflicts occur during bulk copy operation. See for more details on supported databases and compatibility.
- AI metadata: Group=Configuration; Affects=Configuration; Pipeline=BulkInsert; Provider=ProviderDefined;

## LinqToDB.Data.CommandInfo.Constructor

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.Data.CommandInfo.#ctor(LinqToDB.IDataContext,System.String,LinqToDB.Data.DataParameter)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Creates database command instance using provided database connection, command text and single parameter.
- Parameters:
  - `dataContext`: Database connection instance.
  - `commandText`: Command text.
  - `parameter`: Command parameter.

## LinqToDB.Data.CommandInfo.Constructor

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.Data.CommandInfo.#ctor(LinqToDB.IDataContext,System.String,LinqToDB.Data.DataParameter[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Creates database command instance using provided database connection, command text and parameters.
- Parameters:
  - `dataContext`: Database connection instance.
  - `commandText`: Command text.
  - `parameters`: List of command parameters.

## LinqToDB.Data.CommandInfo.Constructor

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.Data.CommandInfo.#ctor(LinqToDB.IDataContext,System.String,System.Object)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Creates database command instance using provided database connection, command text and parameters.
- Parameters:
  - `dataContext`: Database connection instance.
  - `commandText`: Command text.
  - `parameters`: Command parameters. Supported values: - for command without parameters; - single instance; - array of parameters; - mapping class entity.Last case will convert all mapped columns to instances using following logic: - if column is of type, column value will be used. If parameter name () is not set, column name will be used; - if converter from column type to is defined in mapping schema, it will be used to create parameter with colum name passed to converter; - otherwise column value will be converted to using column name as parameter name and column value will be converted to parameter value using conversion, defined by mapping schema.

## LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.Constructor

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.#ctor(System.String,System.String,LinqToDB.DataProvider.IDataProvider,System.String,LinqToDB.Mapping.MappingSchema,System.Data.Common.DbConnection,System.Data.Common.DbTransaction,System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Func{LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Data.Common.DbConnection},System.Func{LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions,LinqToDB.DataProvider.IDataProvider},LinqToDB.Interceptors.ConnectionOptionsConnectionInterceptor,System.Action{LinqToDB.Mapping.MappingSchema,LinqToDB.Mapping.IEntityChangeDescriptor})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Defines provider, connection, transaction, mapping schema, and connection-lifetime settings used to create or configure a data context.
- Remarks: Compose connection settings through and reuse the resulting options for short-lived or instances. When options are changed on an existing context through , only context-reapplicable connection settings can be changed; connection string, provider, and similar creation-time settings are not reconfigured on an already-created context.
- Parameters:
  - `ConfigurationString`: Gets configuration string name to use with instance.
  - `ConnectionString`: The connection string, or if a was used instead of a connection string.
  - `DataProvider`: Gets optional implementation to use with connection.
  - `ProviderName`: Gets optional provider name to use with instance.
  - `MappingSchema`: Gets optional instance to use with instance.
  - `DbConnection`: Gets optional instance to use with instance.
  - `DbTransaction`: Gets optional instance to use with instance.
  - `DisposeConnection`: Gets ownership status for instance. If , will dispose provided/created connection on own dispose. If , will dispose connection on own dispose only if it created by instance.
  - `ConnectionFactory`: Gets connection factory to use with instance. Accepts current context settings.
  - `DataProviderFactory`: Gets factory to use with instance.
  - `ConnectionInterceptor`: Connection interceptor to support connection configuration before or right after connection opened.
  - `OnEntityDescriptorCreated`: Action, called on entity descriptor creation. Allows descriptor modification. When not specified, application-wide callback called.
- AI metadata: Group=Configuration; Affects=ConnectionConfiguration; Pipeline=Connection,Execution; Provider=ProviderDefined;

## LinqToDB.Data.DataContextExtensions.BulkCopy<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.Data.DataContextExtensions.BulkCopy``1(LinqToDB.IDataContext,LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Performs bulk insert operation.
- Remarks: Uses the most efficient insert path available for the configured provider (native bulk API or multi-row INSERT fallback, controlled by ). Execution is immediate; does not use the LINQ translation pipeline.
- Type parameters:
  - `T`: Mapping type of inserted record.
- Parameters:
  - `dataContext`: Database connection.
  - `options`: Operation options.
  - `source`: Records to insert.
- Returns: Bulk insert operation status.
- AI metadata: Group=DML; Execution=Immediate; Composability=Terminal; Affects=Data; Pipeline=BulkInsert;

## LinqToDB.Data.DataContextExtensions.BulkCopy<T>

- Type parameters:
  - `T`: Mapping type of inserted record.
- Parameters:
  - `dataContext`: Database connection.
  - `source`: Records to insert.
- Returns: Bulk insert operation status.

- Summary: Performs bulk insert operation.
- Type parameters:
  - `T`: Mapping type of inserted record.
- Parameters:
  - `dataContext`: Database connection.
  - `maxBatchSize`: Number of rows in each batch. At the end of each batch, the rows in the batch are sent to the server.

## LinqToDB.Data.DataContextExtensions.Execute

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.Data.DataContextExtensions.Execute(LinqToDB.IDataContext,System.String,LinqToDB.Data.DataParameter[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Executes command and returns number of affected records.
- Parameters:
  - `dataContext`: Database connection.
  - `sql`: Command text.
  - `parameters`: Command parameters.
- Returns: Number of records, affected by command execution.

## LinqToDB.Data.DataContextExtensions.Execute

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.Data.DataContextExtensions.Execute(LinqToDB.IDataContext,System.String,System.Object)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Executes command and returns number of affected records.
- Parameters:
  - `dataContext`: Database connection.
  - `sql`: Command text.
  - `parameters`: Command parameters. Supported values: - for command without parameters; - single instance; - array of parameters; - mapping class entity.Last case will convert all mapped columns to instances using following logic: - if column is of type, column value will be used. If parameter name () is not set, column name will be used; - if converter from column type to is defined in mapping schema, it will be used to create parameter with column name passed to converter; - otherwise column value will be converted to using column name as parameter name and column value will be converted to parameter value using conversion, defined by mapping schema.
- Returns: Number of records, affected by command execution.

## LinqToDB.Data.DataContextExtensions.ExecuteReader

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.Data.DataContextExtensions.ExecuteReader(LinqToDB.IDataContext,System.String,System.Object)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Executes command and returns data reader instance.
- Parameters:
  - `dataContext`: Database connection.
  - `sql`: Command text.
  - `parameters`: Command parameters. Supported values: - for command without parameters; - single instance; - array of parameters; - mapping class entity.Last case will convert all mapped columns to instances using following logic: - if column is of type, column value will be used. If parameter name () is not set, column name will be used; - if converter from column type to is defined in mapping schema, it will be used to create parameter with column name passed to converter; - otherwise column value will be converted to using column name as parameter name and column value will be converted to parameter value using conversion, defined by mapping schema.
- Returns: Data reader object.

## LinqToDB.Data.DataContextExtensions.QueryMultiple<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.Data.DataContextExtensions.QueryMultiple``1(LinqToDB.IDataContext,System.String,LinqToDB.Data.DataParameter[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Executes command and returns a result containing multiple result sets.
- Remarks: - type should have default constructor. - if at least one property or field has , then properties that are not marked with will be ignored. - if there is missing index in properties that are marked with , then result set under missing index will be ignored. - if there is no , then all non readonly fields or properties with setter will read from multiple result set. Order is based on their appearance in class.
- Type parameters:
  - `T`: Result set type.
- Parameters:
  - `dataContext`: Database connection.
  - `sql`: Command text.
  - `parameters`: Command parameters.
- Returns: Returns result.

## LinqToDB.Data.DataContextExtensions.QueryMultipleAsync<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.Data.DataContextExtensions.QueryMultipleAsync``1(LinqToDB.IDataContext,System.String,LinqToDB.Data.DataParameter[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Executes command asynchronously and returns a result containing multiple result sets.
- Remarks: - type should have default constructor. - if at least one property or field has , then properties that are not marked with will be ignored. - if there is missing index in properties that are marked with , then result set under missing index will be ignored. - if there is no , then all non readonly fields or properties with setter will read from multiple result set. Order is based on their appearance in class.
- Type parameters:
  - `T`: Result set type.
- Parameters:
  - `dataContext`: Database connection.
  - `sql`: Command text.
  - `parameters`: Command parameters.
- Returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result contains object with multiply result sets.

## LinqToDB.Data.DataContextExtensions.QueryProc<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.Data.DataContextExtensions.QueryProc``1(LinqToDB.IDataContext,``0,System.String,System.Object)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Executes stored procedure and returns results as collection of values of specified type.
- Type parameters:
  - `T`: Result record type.
- Parameters:
  - `dataContext`: Database connection.
  - `template`: This value used only for parameter type inference, which makes this method usable with anonymous types.
  - `sql`: Command text.
  - `parameters`: Command parameters. Supported values: - for command without parameters; - single instance; - array of parameters; - mapping class entity.Last case will convert all mapped columns to instances using following logic: - if column is of type, column value will be used. If parameter name () is not set, column name will be used; - if converter from column type to is defined in mapping schema, it will be used to create parameter with column name passed to converter; - otherwise column value will be converted to using column name as parameter name and column value will be converted to parameter value using conversion, defined by mapping schema.
- Returns: Returns collection of query result records.

## LinqToDB.Data.DataContextExtensions.QueryProc<T>

- Type parameters:

- Parameters:
  - `dataContext`: Database connection.
  - `objectReader`: Record mapping function from data reader.
  - `sql`: Command text. This is caller's responsibility to properly escape procedure name.
  - `parameters`: Command parameters.
- Returns: Returns collection of query result records.

## LinqToDB.Data.DataContextExtensions.QueryProcMultiple<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.Data.DataContextExtensions.QueryProcMultiple``1(LinqToDB.IDataContext,System.String,LinqToDB.Data.DataParameter[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Executes command using command type and returns a result containing multiple result sets. Sets result values for output and reference parameters to corresponding parameters in .
- Remarks: - type should have default constructor. - if at least one property or field has , then properties that are not marked with will be ignored. - if there is missing index in properties that are marked with , then result set under missing index will be ignored. - if there is no , then all non readonly fields or properties with setter will read from multiple result set. Order is based on their appearance in class.
- Type parameters:
  - `T`: Result set type.
- Parameters:
  - `dataContext`: Database connection.
  - `sql`: Command text. This is caller's responsibility to properly escape procedure name.
  - `parameters`: Command parameters.
- Returns: Returns result.

## LinqToDB.Data.DataContextExtensions.SetCommand

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.Data.DataContextExtensions.SetCommand(LinqToDB.IDataContext,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Creates command wrapper for current connection with provided command text.
- Remarks: Entry point for the raw SQL fluent builder pattern. Chain Query, Execute, or ExecuteReader (and their Async variants) on the returned to materialize results or run DML.
- Parameters:
  - `dataContext`: Database connection.
  - `commandText`: Command text.
- Returns: Database command wrapper.
- AI metadata: Group=RawSQL; Execution=Deferred; Composability=Composable; Affects=CommandBuilder; Pipeline=SqlText;
