- Summary: Sets DbConnection option.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithDbTransaction

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithDbTransaction(LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions,System.Data.Common.DbTransaction)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Sets DbTransaction option.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithDefaultNullsPosition

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithDefaultNullsPosition(LinqToDB.SqlOptions,LinqToDB.Sql.NullsPosition)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Default position of NULL values in an ORDER BY clause for ordering keys that do not specify a explicitly. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithDisableQueryCache

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithDisableQueryCache(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Used to disable LINQ expressions caching for queries. This cache reduces time, required for query parsing but have several side-effects: - cached LINQ expressions could contain references to external objects as parameters, which could lead to memory leaks if those objects are not used anymore by other code - cache access synchronization could lead to bigger latencies than it saves. Default value: . It is not recommended to enable this option as it could lead to severe slowdown. Better approach will be to use scope around queries, that produce severe memory leaks you need to fix. More details.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithDisposeConnection

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithDisposeConnection(LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions,System.Nullable{System.Boolean})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Sets DisposeConnection option.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithDoNotClearOrderBys

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithDoNotClearOrderBys(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Controls behavior, when LINQ query chain contains multiple or calls: - if - non-first OrderBy* call will be treated as ThenBy* call; - if - OrderBy* call will discard sort specifications, added by previous OrderBy* and ThenBy* calls. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithEnableConstantExpressionInOrderBy

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithEnableConstantExpressionInOrderBy(LinqToDB.SqlOptions,System.Boolean)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: If , linq2db will allow any constant expressions in ORDER BY clause. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithEnableContextSchemaEdit

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithEnableContextSchemaEdit(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: If , user could add new mappings to context mapping schems (). Otherwise, will be generated on locked mapping schema edit attempt. It is not recommended to enable this option as it has performance implications. Proper approach is to create single instance once, configure mappings for it and use this instance for all context instances.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithExponentialBase

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithExponentialBase(LinqToDB.Data.RetryPolicy.RetryPolicyOptions,System.Double)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: The base for the exponential function used to compute the delay between retries, must not be lesser than 1. Default value: 2.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithFactory

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithFactory(LinqToDB.Data.RetryPolicy.RetryPolicyOptions,System.Func{LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection,LinqToDB.Data.RetryPolicy.IRetryPolicy})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Retry policy factory method, used to create retry policy for new instance. If factory method is not set, retry policy is not used. Not set by default.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithFireTriggers

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithFireTriggers(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions,System.Nullable{System.Boolean})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: When specified, causes the server to fire the insert triggers for the rows being inserted into the database.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithGenerateExpressionTest

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithGenerateExpressionTest(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Enables generation of test class for each LINQ query, executed while this option is enabled. This option could be useful for issue reporting, when you need to provide reproducible case. Test file will be placed to linq2db subfolder of temp folder and exact file path will be logged to data connection tracing infrastructure. See for more details. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithGenerateFinalAliases

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithGenerateFinalAliases(LinqToDB.SqlOptions,System.Boolean)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Indicates whether SQL Builder should generate aliases for final projection. It is not required for correct query processing but simplifies SQL analysis. Default value: . For the query var query = from child in db.Child select new { TrackId = child.ChildID, }; When property is SELECT [child].[ChildID] as [TrackId] FROM [Child] [child] Otherwise alias will be removed SELECT [child].[ChildID] FROM [Child] [child]

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithGuardGrouping

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithGuardGrouping(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Controls behavior of LINQ query, which ends with GroupBy call. - if - will be thrown for such queries; - if - eager loading used. Default value: .
- Remarks: More details.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithIgnoreEmptyUpdate

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithIgnoreEmptyUpdate(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Controls behavior of linq2db when there is no updateable fields in Update query: - if - query not executed and Update operation returns 0 as number of affected records; - if - will be thrown. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithKeepDistinctOrdered

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithKeepDistinctOrdered(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Allows SQL generation to automatically transform SELECT DISTINCT value FROM Table ORDER BY date Into GROUP BY equivalent if syntax is not supported Default value: .

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithKeepIdentity

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithKeepIdentity(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions,System.Nullable{System.Boolean})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: If this option set to true, bulk copy will use values of columns, marked with IsIdentity flag. SkipOnInsert flag in this case will be ignored. Otherwise, those columns will be skipped and values will be generated by server. Not compatible with mode.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithKeepNulls

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithKeepNulls(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions,System.Nullable{System.Boolean})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Preserves null values in the destination table regardless of the settings for default values.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithMappingSchema

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithMappingSchema(LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions,LinqToDB.Mapping.MappingSchema)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Sets MappingSchema option.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithMaxBatchSize

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithMaxBatchSize(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions,System.Nullable{System.Int32})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Number of rows in each batch. At the end of each batch, the rows in the batch are sent to the server. Returns an integer value or zero if no value has been set.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithMaxDegreeOfParallelism

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithMaxDegreeOfParallelism(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions,System.Nullable{System.Int32})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Implemented only by ClickHouse.Driver provider. Defines number of connections, used for parallel insert in mode.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithMaxDelay

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithMaxDelay(LinqToDB.Data.RetryPolicy.RetryPolicyOptions,System.TimeSpan)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: The maximum time delay between retries, must be nonnegative. Default value: 30 seconds.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithMaxParametersForBatch

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithMaxParametersForBatch(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions,System.Nullable{System.Int32})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: If set, will set the maximum parameters per batch statement. Also see .

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithMaxRetryCount

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithMaxRetryCount(LinqToDB.Data.RetryPolicy.RetryPolicyOptions,System.Int32)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: The number of retry attempts. Default value: 5.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithNotifyAfter

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithNotifyAfter(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions,System.Int32)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Gets or sets counter after how many copied records should be called. E.g. if you set it to 10, callback will be called after each 10 copied records. To disable callback, set this option to 0 (default value).

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithOnEntityDescriptorCreated

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithOnEntityDescriptorCreated(LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions,System.Action{LinqToDB.Mapping.MappingSchema,LinqToDB.Mapping.IEntityChangeDescriptor})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Sets OnEntityDescriptorCreated option.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithOnTrace

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithOnTrace(LinqToDB.Data.QueryTraceOptions,System.Action{LinqToDB.Data.TraceInfo})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Configure the database to use the specified callback for logging or tracing.
- Parameters:
  - `onTrace`: Callback, may not be called depending on the trace level.
- Returns: The builder instance so calls can be chained.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithOptimizeJoins

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithOptimizeJoins(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: If enabled, linq2db will try to reduce number of generated SQL JOINs for LINQ query. Attempted optimizations: - removes duplicate joins by unique target table key; - removes self-joins by unique key; - removes left joins if joined table is not used in query. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithParameterizeTakeSkip

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithParameterizeTakeSkip(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Enables Take/Skip parameterization. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithPreferApply

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithPreferApply(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Used to generate CROSS APPLY or OUTER APPLY if possible. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithPreferExistsForScalar

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithPreferExistsForScalar(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Depending on this option linq2db generates different SQL for sequence.Contains(value). - EXISTS (SELECT * FROM sequence WHERE sequence.key = value). - value IN (SELECT sequence.key FROM sequence). Default value: .

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithPreloadGroups

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithPreloadGroups(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Controls how group data for LINQ queries ended with GroupBy will be loaded: - if - group data will be loaded together with main query, resulting in 1 + N queries, where N - number of groups; - if - group data will be loaded when you call enumerator for specific group . Default value: .

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithProviderName

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithProviderName(LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Sets ProviderName option.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithRandomFactor

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithRandomFactor(LinqToDB.Data.RetryPolicy.RetryPolicyOptions,System.Double)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: The maximum random factor, must not be lesser than 1. Default value: 1.1.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithRetryPolicy

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithRetryPolicy(LinqToDB.Data.RetryPolicy.RetryPolicyOptions,LinqToDB.Data.RetryPolicy.IRetryPolicy)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Uses retry policy

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithRowsCopiedCallback

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithRowsCopiedCallback(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions,System.Action{LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyRowsCopied})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Gets or sets callback method that will be called by BulkCopy operation after each rows copied. This callback will not be used if set to 0.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithSchemaName

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithSchemaName(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Gets or sets explicit name of target schema/owner instead of one, configured for copied entity in mapping schema. See method for support information per provider.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithServerName

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithServerName(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Gets or sets explicit name of target server instead of one, configured for copied entity in mapping schema. See method for support information per provider. Also note that it is not supported by provider-specific insert method.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithTableLock

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithTableLock(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions,System.Nullable{System.Boolean})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Obtains a bulk update lock for the duration of the bulk copy operation.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithTableName

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithTableName(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Gets or sets explicit name of target table instead of one, configured for copied entity in mapping schema.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithTableOptions

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithTableOptions(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions,LinqToDB.TableOptions)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Gets or sets flags overrides instead of configured for copied entity in mapping schema. See method for support information per provider.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithTraceLevel

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithTraceLevel(LinqToDB.Data.QueryTraceOptions,System.Diagnostics.TraceLevel)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Configure the database to use specified trace level.
- Returns: The builder instance so calls can be chained.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithTraceMapperExpression

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithTraceMapperExpression(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Enables logging of generated mapping expression to data connection tracing infrastructure. See for more details. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithUseInternalTransaction

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithUseInternalTransaction(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions,System.Nullable{System.Boolean})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: When specified, each batch of the bulk-copy operation will occur within a transaction.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithUseParameters

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithUseParameters(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions,System.Boolean)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Gets or sets whether to always use Parameters for MultipleRowsCopy. Default is false. If True, provider-specific parameter limit per batch will be used to determine the maximum number of rows per insert, Unless overridden by .

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithWithoutSession

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithWithoutSession(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions,System.Boolean)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Implemented only by ClickHouse.Driver provider. When set, provider-specific bulk copy will use session-less connection even if called over connection with session. Note that session-less connections cannot be used with session-bound functionality like temporary tables.

## LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithWriteTrace

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithWriteTrace(LinqToDB.Data.QueryTraceOptions,System.Action{System.String,System.String,System.Diagnostics.TraceLevel})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Configure the database to use the specified a string trace callback.
- Parameters:
  - `write`: Callback, may not be called depending on the trace level.
- Returns: The builder instance so calls can be chained.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.Access.AccessHints.SubQueryHint<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Access.AccessHints.SubQueryHint``1(LinqToDB.DataProvider.Access.IAccessSpecificQueryable{``0},System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Adds a query hint to a generated query.
- Type parameters:
  - `TSource`: Table record mapping class.
- Parameters:
  - `source`: Query source.
  - `hint`: SQL text, added as a database specific hint to generated query.
- Returns: Query source with join hints.
- AI metadata: Group=Hints; HintType=SubQuery; Execution=Deferred; Composability=Composable; Affects=SqlSemantics; Pipeline=ExpressionTree,SqlAST,SqlText; Provider=ProviderDefined;

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Access.AccessHints.WithOwnerAccessOption``1(LinqToDB.DataProvider.Access.IAccessSpecificQueryable{``0})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Adds an Access WITH OWNERACCESS OPTION subquery hint.
- AI metadata: Group=Hints; HintType=SubQuery; Execution=Deferred; Composability=Composable; Affects=SqlSemantics; Pipeline=ExpressionTree,SqlAST,SqlText; Provider=ProviderDefined;

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.Access.AccessOptions.Constructor

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Access.AccessOptions.#ctor(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType)`
- Kind: Method
- Parameters:
  - `BulkCopyType`: Default bulk copy mode, used for Access by methods, if mode is not specified explicitly. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.Access.AccessTools.CreateDatabase

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Access.AccessTools.CreateDatabase(System.String,System.Boolean,LinqToDB.DataProvider.Access.AccessVersion)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Creates new Access database file. Requires Access OLE DB provider (JET or ACE) and ADOX.
- Parameters:
  - `databaseName`: Name of database to create.
  - `deleteIfExists`: If , existing database will be removed before create.
  - `version`: Access engine to use to create database. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.Access.AccessTools.CreateDatabase

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Access.AccessTools.CreateDatabase(System.String,System.Boolean,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Creates new Access database file. Requires Access OLE DB provider (JET or ACE) and ADOX.
- Remarks: Provider value examples: Microsoft.Jet.OLEDB.4.0 (for JET database), Microsoft.ACE.OLEDB.12.0, Microsoft.ACE.OLEDB.15.0 (for ACE database).
- Parameters:
  - `databaseName`: Name of database to create.
  - `deleteIfExists`: If , existing database will be removed before create.
  - `provider`: Name of OleDb provider to use to create database. Default value: "Microsoft.Jet.OLEDB.4.0".

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.Access.AccessTools.DropDatabase

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Access.AccessTools.DropDatabase(System.String,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Removes database file by database name.
- Parameters:
  - `databaseName`: Name of database to remove.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.Access.AccessTools.GetDataProvider

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Access.AccessTools.GetDataProvider(LinqToDB.DataProvider.Access.AccessVersion,LinqToDB.DataProvider.Access.AccessProvider,System.String,System.Data.Common.DbConnection,System.Data.Common.DbTransaction)`

- Summary: Returns instance of Access database provider.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.ClickHouse.ClickHouseHints.QueryHint<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.ClickHouse.ClickHouseHints.QueryHint``1(LinqToDB.DataProvider.ClickHouse.IClickHouseSpecificQueryable{``0},System.String,System.String,System.Object[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Adds a query hint to the generated query.
- Type parameters:
  - `TSource`: Table record mapping class.
- Parameters:
  - `source`: Query source.
  - `hint`: SQL text, added as a database specific hint to generated query.
  - `hintParameters`: Table hint parameters.
- Returns: Table-like query source with table hints.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.ClickHouse.ClickHouseHints.SettingsHint<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.ClickHouse.ClickHouseHints.SettingsHint``1(LinqToDB.DataProvider.ClickHouse.IClickHouseSpecificQueryable{``0},System.String,System.Object[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Adds a ClickHouse SETTINGS clause using provider-specific setting text.
- Remarks: ClickHouse exposes a large and evolving set of query-level settings through the SETTINGS clause. LinqToDB intentionally exposes SettingsHint as the general settings API instead of providing typed helpers for every ClickHouse setting. Use typed ClickHouse hint helpers when they exist for a concrete SQL feature; use this method for ClickHouse settings that do not have a dedicated typed helper. The hintParameters argument can include Sql.TableAlias, Sql.TableName, or Sql.TableSpec values for table sources marked with TableID. This lets settings that refer to tables use the exact identifiers emitted by the SQL builder.
- AI metadata: Group=Hints; HintType=Query; Execution=Deferred; Composability=Composable; Affects=SqlSemantics; Pipeline=ExpressionTree,SqlAST,SqlText; Provider=ProviderDefined;

- Summary: Adds a subquery hint to the generated query.
- Type parameters:

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.ClickHouse.ClickHouseOptions.Constructor

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.ClickHouse.ClickHouseOptions.#ctor(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType,System.Boolean)`
- Kind: Method
- Parameters:
  - `BulkCopyType`: Default bulk copy mode. Default value: .
  - `UseStandardCompatibleAggregates`: Enables -OrNull combinator for Min, Max, Sum and Avg aggregation functions to support SQL standard-compatible behavior. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.ClickHouse.ClickHouseRetryPolicy.Constructor

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.ClickHouse.ClickHouseRetryPolicy.#ctor`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Creates a new instance of .

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.ClickHouse.ClickHouseRetryPolicy.Constructor

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.ClickHouse.ClickHouseRetryPolicy.#ctor(System.Int32,System.TimeSpan,System.Double,System.Double,System.TimeSpan,System.Collections.Generic.ICollection{System.Int32})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Creates a new instance of .
- Parameters:
  - `maxRetryCount`: The maximum number of retry attempts.
  - `maxRetryDelay`: The maximum delay in milliseconds between retries.
  - `randomFactor`: The maximum random factor.
  - `exponentialBase`: The base for the exponential function used to compute the delay between retries.
  - `coefficient`: The coefficient for the exponential function used to compute the delay between retries.
  - `errorNumbersToAdd`: Additional SQL error numbers that should be considered transient.

  - `maxRetryCount`: The maximum number of retry attempts.

- Kind: Method
- Parameters:
  - `BulkCopyType`: Default bulk copy mode, used for DB2 by methods, if mode is not specified explicitly. Default value: .
  - `IdentifierQuoteMode`: Identifier quotation logic for SQL generation. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.DuckDB.DuckDBOptions.Constructor

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.DuckDB.DuckDBOptions.#ctor(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: DuckDB data provider configuration options.
- Parameters:
  - `BulkCopyType`: Default bulk copy mode for DuckDB. Default value: . Uses native DuckDB Appender for best performance with automatic fallback to MultipleRows when the table has unmapped columns or identity columns with nextval() defaults.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdOptions.Constructor

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdOptions.#ctor(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType,LinqToDB.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdIdentifierQuoteMode,System.Boolean)`
- Kind: Method
- Parameters:
  - `BulkCopyType`: Default bulk copy mode, used for Firebird by methods, if mode is not specified explicitly. Default value: .
  - `IdentifierQuoteMode`: Specifies how identifiers like table and field names should be quoted. Default value: .
  - `IsLiteralEncodingSupported`: Specifies that Firebird supports literal encoding. Available from version 2.5 and could be used for Dialect 1 databases.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.IDataProvider.ExecuteScope

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.IDataProvider.ExecuteScope(LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Returns scoped context object to wrap calls of Execute* methods. Using this, provider could e.g. change thread culture during Execute* calls. Following calls wrapped right now: DataConnection.ExecuteNonQuery DataConnection.ExecuteReader.
- Parameters:
  - `dataConnection`: Data connection instance used with scope.
- Returns: Returns disposable scope object. Can be .

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.IDataProvider.GetQueryParameterNormalizer

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.IDataProvider.GetQueryParameterNormalizer`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Returns instance of , which implements normalization logic for parameters of single query. E.g. it could include: trimming names that are too longremoving/replacing unsupported charactersname deduplication for parameters with same name. For implementation without state it is recommended to return static instance. E.g. this could be done for providers with positional parameters that ignore names.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.IDataProvider.InitCommand

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.IDataProvider.InitCommand(LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection,System.Data.Common.DbCommand,System.Data.CommandType,System.String,LinqToDB.Data.DataParameter[],System.Boolean)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Initializes command object.
- Parameters:
  - `dataConnection`: Data connection instance to initialize with new command.
  - `command`: Command instance to initialize.
  - `commandType`: Type of command.
  - `commandText`: Command SQL.
  - `parameters`: Optional list of parameters to add to initialized command.
  - `withParameters`: Flag to indicate that command has parameters. Used to configure parameters support when method called without parameters and parameters added later to command.
- Returns: Initialized command instance.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.Informix.InformixOptions.Constructor

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Informix.InformixOptions.#ctor(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType,System.Boolean)`
- Kind: Method
- Parameters:
  - `BulkCopyType`: Default bulk copy mode, used for Informix by methods, if mode is not specified explicitly. Default value: .
  - `ExplicitFractionalSecondsSeparator`: Enables use of explicit fractional seconds separator in datetime values. Must be enabled for Informix starting from v11.70.xC8 and v12.10.xC2. More details at: https://www.ibm.com/support/knowledgecenter/SSGU8G_12.1.0/com.ibm.po.doc/new_features_ce.htm#newxc2__xc2_datetime

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlExtensions.Match

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlExtensions.Match(LinqToDB.DataProvider.MySql.IMySqlExtensions,LinqToDB.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlExtensions.MatchModifier,System.String,System.Object[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Applies full-text search condition using MATCH AGAINST predicate against specified full-text columns using specified search modifier. Example: MATCH(col1, col2) AGAINST('search query' MODIFIER).
- Parameters:
  - `ext`: Extension point.
  - `modifier`: Search modifier.
  - `search`: Full-text search condition.
  - `columns`: Full-text columns that should be queried.
- Returns: Returns if full-text search found matching records.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlExtensions.Match

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlExtensions.Match(LinqToDB.DataProvider.MySql.IMySqlExtensions,System.String,System.Object[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Applies full-text search condition using MATCH AGAINST predicate against specified full-text columns using default mode (IN NATURAL LANGUAGE MODE). Example: MATCH(col1, col2) AGAINST('search query').
- Parameters:
  - `ext`: Extension point.
  - `search`: Full-text search condition.
  - `columns`: Full-text columns that should be queried.
- Returns: Returns if full-text search found matching records.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlExtensions.MatchRelevance

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlExtensions.MatchRelevance(LinqToDB.DataProvider.MySql.IMySqlExtensions,LinqToDB.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlExtensions.MatchModifier,System.String,System.Object[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Calculates relevance of full-text search for current record using MATCH AGAINST predicate against specified full-text columns using specified search modifier. Example: MATCH(col1, col2) AGAINST('search query' MODIFIER).
- Parameters:
  - `ext`: Extension point.
  - `modifier`: Search modifier.
  - `search`: Full-text search condition.
  - `columns`: Full-text columns that should be queried.
- Returns: Returns full-text search relevance value for current record.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlExtensions.MatchRelevance

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlExtensions.MatchRelevance(LinqToDB.DataProvider.MySql.IMySqlExtensions,System.String,System.Object[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Calculates relevance of full-text search for current record using MATCH AGAINST predicate against specified full-text columns using default mode (IN NATURAL LANGUAGE MODE). Example: MATCH(col1, col2) AGAINST('search query').
- Parameters:
  - `ext`: Extension point.
  - `search`: Full-text search condition.
  - `columns`: Full-text columns that should be queried.
- Returns: Returns full-text search relevance value for current record.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlHints.QueryBlockHint<T1,T2>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlHints.QueryBlockHint``2(LinqToDB.DataProvider.MySql.IMySqlSpecificQueryable{``0},System.String,``1[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Adds a query hint to the generated query.
- Type parameters:
  - `TSource`: Table record mapping class.
  - `TParam`: Table hint parameter type.
- Parameters:

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlHints.SubQueryTableHint<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlHints.SubQueryTableHint``1(LinqToDB.DataProvider.MySql.IMySqlSpecificQueryable{``0},System.String,LinqToDB.Sql.SqlID[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Adds a MySQL subquery row-locking hint to a generated query.
- Remarks: The tableIDs values are created with Sql.TableAlias, Sql.TableName, or Sql.TableSpec for table sources marked with TableID. They resolve to the generated SQL identifiers for the selected table sources.
- Type parameters:
  - `TSource`: Table record mapping class.
- Parameters:
  - `source`: Query source.
  - `hint`: MySQL row-locking hint, e.g. FOR UPDATE or FOR SHARE.
  - `tableIDs`: Optional table identifiers for the OF clause.
- Returns: Query source with subquery hint.
- AI metadata: Group=Hints; HintType=SubQuery; Execution=Deferred; Composability=Composable; Affects=SqlSemantics; Pipeline=ExpressionTree,SqlAST,SqlText; Provider=ProviderDefined;

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlHints.SubQueryTableHint<T>

- Type parameters:
  - `TSource`: Table record mapping class.
- Parameters:
  - `source`: Query source.
  - `hint`: MySQL row-locking hint, e.g. FOR UPDATE or FOR SHARE.
  - `hint2`: NOWAIT | SKIP LOCKED
  - `tableIDs`: Optional table identifiers for the OF clause.

- AI metadata: Group=Hints; HintType=SubQuery; Execution=Deferred; Composability=Composable; Affects=SqlSemantics; Pipeline=ExpressionTree,SqlAST,SqlText; Provider=ProviderDefined;

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.OracleHints.OptParamHint<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.OracleHints.OptParamHint``1(LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.IOracleSpecificQueryable{``0},System.String[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Adds an Oracle OPT_PARAM query hint.
- Remarks: Oracle defines the optimizer parameter names and values accepted by OPT_PARAM. LinqToDB exposes OptParamHint as the provider-specific family API for that Oracle hint instead of enumerating every possible optimizer parameter value.
- AI metadata: Group=Hints; HintType=Query; Execution=Deferred; Composability=Composable; Affects=SqlSemantics; Pipeline=ExpressionTree,SqlAST,SqlText; Provider=ProviderDefined;

- Kind: Method
- Summary: Adds a query hint to a generated query. // will produce following SQL code in generated query: INNER LOOP JOIN var tableWithHint = db.Table.JoinHint("LOOP");
- Type parameters:
  - `TSource`: Table record mapping class.
- Parameters:
  - `source`: Query source.
  - `hint`: SQL text, added as a database specific hint to generated query.
- Returns: Query source with join hints.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.OracleOptions.Constructor

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.OracleOptions.#ctor(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType,LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.AlternativeBulkCopy,System.Boolean)`
- Kind: Method
- Parameters:
  - `BulkCopyType`: Default bulk copy mode, used for oracle by methods, if mode is not specified explicitly. Default value: .
  - `AlternativeBulkCopy`: Defines type of multi-row INSERT operation to generate for bulk copy mode.
  - `DontEscapeLowercaseIdentifiers`: Gets or sets flag to tell LinqToDB to quote identifiers, if they contain lowercase letters. Default value: . This flag is added for backward compatibility and not recommended for use with new applications.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLHints.SubQueryTableHint<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLHints.SubQueryTableHint``1(LinqToDB.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.IPostgreSQLSpecificQueryable{``0},System.String,LinqToDB.Sql.SqlID[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Adds a PostgreSQL subquery row-locking hint to a generated query.
- Remarks: The tableIDs values are created with Sql.TableAlias, Sql.TableName, or Sql.TableSpec for table sources marked with TableID. They resolve to the generated SQL identifiers for the selected table sources.
- Type parameters:
  - `TSource`: Table record mapping class.
- Parameters:
  - `source`: Query source.
  - `hint`: PostgreSQL row-locking hint, e.g. FOR UPDATE or FOR SHARE.
  - `tableIDs`: Optional table identifiers for the OF clause.
- Returns: Query source with subquery hint.
- AI metadata: Group=Hints; HintType=SubQuery; Execution=Deferred; Composability=Composable; Affects=SqlSemantics; Pipeline=ExpressionTree,SqlAST,SqlText; Provider=ProviderDefined;

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLHints.SubQueryTableHint<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLHints.SubQueryTableHint``1(LinqToDB.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.IPostgreSQLSpecificQueryable{``0},System.String,System.String,LinqToDB.Sql.SqlID[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Adds a PostgreSQL subquery row-locking hint to a generated query.
- Remarks: The tableIDs values are created with Sql.TableAlias, Sql.TableName, or Sql.TableSpec for table sources marked with TableID. They resolve to the generated SQL identifiers for the selected table sources.
- Type parameters:
  - `TSource`: Table record mapping class.
- Parameters:
  - `source`: Query source.
  - `hint`: PostgreSQL row-locking hint, e.g. FOR UPDATE or FOR SHARE.
  - `hint2`: NOWAIT | SKIP LOCKED
  - `tableIDs`: Optional table identifiers for the OF clause.
- Returns: Query source with subquery hint.
- AI metadata: Group=Hints; HintType=SubQuery; Execution=Deferred; Composability=Composable; Affects=SqlSemantics; Pipeline=ExpressionTree,SqlAST,SqlText; Provider=ProviderDefined;

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLOptions.Constructor

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLOptions.#ctor(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType,System.Boolean,LinqToDB.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLIdentifierQuoteMode)`
- Kind: Method
- Parameters:
  - `BulkCopyType`: Default bulk copy mode, used for PostgreSQL by methods, if mode is not specified explicitly. Default value: .
  - `NormalizeTimestampData`: Enables normalization of and data, passed to query as parameter or passed to APIs, to comform with Npgsql 6 requerements: convert value to UTC value with zero Use for timestamptz valuesUse for timestamp values with kind Default value: .
  - `IdentifierQuoteMode`: Specify identifiers quotation logic for SQL generation. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlCe.SqlCeOptions.Constructor

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlCe.SqlCeOptions.#ctor(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType,System.Boolean)`
- Kind: Method
- Parameters:
  - `BulkCopyType`: Default bulk copy mode, used for SqlCe by methods, if mode is not specified explicitly. Default value: .
  - `InlineFunctionParameters`: Enables force inlining of function parameters to support SQL CE 3.0. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SQLite.SQLiteExtensions.FTS3Snippet<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SQLite.SQLiteExtensions.FTS3Snippet``1(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SQLite.ISQLiteExtensions,``0,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: FTS3/4 snippet(fts_table, startMatch, endMatch, ellipses, columnIndex, tokensNumber) function. Example: "snippet(table, 'startMatch', 'endMatch', 'ellipses', columnIndex, tokensNumber)".
- Remarks: FTS Support: FTS3/4.
- Type parameters:
  - `TEntity`: Full-text search table mapping class.
- Parameters:
  - `ext`: Extension point.
  - `entity`: Full-text search table.
  - `startMatch`: Start match wrap text.
  - `endMatch`: End match wrap text.
  - `ellipses`: Ellipses text.
  - `columnIndex`: Index of column to extract snippet from. -1 matches all columns.
  - `tokensNumber`: Manages how many tokens should be returned (check function documentation).
- Returns: Check documentation of SQLite site.

- Kind: Method
- Summary: Executes FTS5 'optimize' command for specific table. Example: "INSERT INTO table(table) VALUES('optimize')".
- Type parameters:
  - `TEntity`: Table mapping class.
- Parameters:
  - `dc`: instance.
  - `table`: FTS table.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SQLite.SQLiteExtensions.FTS5Snippet<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SQLite.SQLiteExtensions.FTS5Snippet``1(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SQLite.ISQLiteExtensions,``0,System.Int32,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Int32)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: FTS5 snippet(fts_table, columnIndex, startMatch, endMatch, ellipses, tokensNumber) function. Example: "snippet(table, columnIndex, 'startMatch', 'endMatch', 'ellipses', tokensNumber)".
- Remarks: FTS Support: FTS5.
- Type parameters:
  - `TEntity`: Full-text search table mapping class.
- Parameters:
  - `ext`: Extension point.
  - `entity`: Full-text search table.
  - `columnIndex`: Index of column to extract snippet from.
  - `startMatch`: Start match wrap text.
  - `endMatch`: End match wrap text.
  - `ellipses`: Ellipses text.
  - `tokensNumber`: Manages how many tokens should be returned (check function documentation).
- Returns: Check documentation of SQLite site.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SQLite.SQLiteExtensions.Match

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SQLite.SQLiteExtensions.Match(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SQLite.ISQLiteExtensions,System.Object,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Applies full-text search condition using MATCH predicate against whole FTS table or specific column. Examples: "table MATCH 'search query'"/"table.column MATCH 'search query'".
- Remarks: FTS Support: FTS3/4, FTS5.
- Parameters:
  - `ext`: Extension point.
  - `entityOrColumn`: Table or column to perform full-text search against.
  - `match`: Full-text search condition.
- Returns: Returns if full-text search found matching records.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SQLite.SQLiteExtensions.MatchTable<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SQLite.SQLiteExtensions.MatchTable``1(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SQLite.ISQLiteExtensions,LinqToDB.ITable{``0},System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Performs full-text search query against specified table and returns search results. Example: "table('search query')".
- Remarks: FTS Support: FTS5.
- Type parameters:
  - `TEntity`: Queried table mapping class.
- Parameters:
  - `ext`: Extension point.
  - `table`: Table to perform full-text search against.
  - `match`: Full-text search condition.
- Returns: Returns table, filtered using specified search condition, applied to whole table.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SQLite.SQLiteExtensions.Rank<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SQLite.SQLiteExtensions.Rank``1(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SQLite.ISQLiteExtensions,``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Provides access to FTS5 rank hidden column. Example: "table.rank".
- Remarks: FTS Support: FTS5.
- Type parameters:
  - `TEntity`: Type of table mapping class.
- Parameters:
  - `ext`: Extension point.
  - `entity`: Table record instance.
- Returns: Returns rank column value.

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SQLite.SQLiteExtensions.RowId``1(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SQLite.ISQLiteExtensions,``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Provides access to rowid hidden column. Example: "table.rowid".
- Type parameters:
  - `TEntity`: Type of table mapping class.
- Parameters:
  - `ext`: Extension point.
  - `entity`: Table record instance.
- Returns: Returns rowid column value.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SQLite.SQLiteOptions.Constructor

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SQLite.SQLiteOptions.#ctor(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType,System.Boolean)`
- Kind: Method
- Parameters:
  - `BulkCopyType`: Default bulk copy mode, used for SQLite by methods, if mode is not specified explicitly. Default value: .
  - `AlwaysCheckDbNull`: Enables null-value checks during database data mapping even if SQLite reports that column cannot be NULL to avoid on mapping when database reports nullability incorrectly. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SQLite.SQLiteTools.ClearAllPools

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SQLite.SQLiteTools.ClearAllPools(System.Nullable{LinqToDB.DataProvider.SQLite.SQLiteProvider})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Invokes ClearAllPools() method for specified provider.
- Parameters:
  - `provider`: For which provider ClearAllPools should be called. If value passed - call method for all providers.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$A03CA024753CE7A3E7327C9BB5C169A6.CosineVectorDistance

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$A03CA024753CE7A3E7327C9BB5C169A6.CosineVectorDistance(System.Single[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: The VECTOR_DISTANCE function calculates the distance between two vectors using the cosine distance metric.
- Parameters:
  - `vector2`: An expression that evaluates to vector data type.
- Returns: The function returns a scalar float value that represents the distance between the two vectors using the specified distance metric.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$A03CA024753CE7A3E7327C9BB5C169A6.DotVectorDistance

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$A03CA024753CE7A3E7327C9BB5C169A6.DotVectorDistance(System.Single[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: The VECTOR_DISTANCE function calculates the distance between two vectors using the dot product-based indication of distance metric.
- Parameters:
  - `vector2`: An expression that evaluates to vector data type.
- Returns: The function returns a scalar float value that represents the distance between the two vectors using the specified distance metric.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$A03CA024753CE7A3E7327C9BB5C169A6.EuclideanVectorDistance

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$A03CA024753CE7A3E7327C9BB5C169A6.EuclideanVectorDistance(System.Single[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: The VECTOR_DISTANCE function calculates the distance between two vectors using the Euclidean distance metric.
- Parameters:
  - `vector2`: An expression that evaluates to vector data type.
- Returns: The function returns a scalar float value that represents the distance between the two vectors using the specified distance metric.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$A03CA024753CE7A3E7327C9BB5C169A6.VectorBaseTypeProperty

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$A03CA024753CE7A3E7327C9BB5C169A6.VectorBaseTypeProperty`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: The VECTORPROPERTY function returns BaseType of a given vector. The function requires two arguments: the vector itself and the property to be retrieved.
- Returns: sysname with the name of the data type.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$A03CA024753CE7A3E7327C9BB5C169A6.VectorDimensionsProperty

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$A03CA024753CE7A3E7327C9BB5C169A6.VectorDimensionsProperty`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: The VECTORPROPERTY function returns Dimensions of a given vector. The function requires two arguments: the vector itself and the property to be retrieved.
- Returns: Integer value with dimension count.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$A03CA024753CE7A3E7327C9BB5C169A6.VectorDistance

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$A03CA024753CE7A3E7327C9BB5C169A6.VectorDistance(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.DistanceMetric,System.Single[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: The VECTOR_DISTANCE function calculates the distance between two vectors using a specified distance metric.
- Parameters:
  - `distanceMetric`: A string with the name of the distance metric to use to calculate the distance between the two given vectors. The following distance metrics are supported: cosineCosine distanceeuclideanEuclidean distancedot(Negative) Dot product
  - `vector2`: An expression that evaluates to vector data type.
- Returns: The function returns a scalar float value that represents the distance between the two vectors using the specified distance metric.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$A03CA024753CE7A3E7327C9BB5C169A6.VectorNorm

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$A03CA024753CE7A3E7327C9BB5C169A6.VectorNorm(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.NormType)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Use VECTOR_NORM to take a vector as an input and return the norm of the vector (which is a measure of its length or magnitude) in a given norm type.
- Parameters:
  - `normType`: A string with the name of the norm type to use to calculate the norm of the given vector. The following norm types are supported: norm1The 1-norm, which is the sum of the absolute values of the vector components.norm2The 2-norm, also known as the Euclidean Norm, which is the square root of the sum of the squares of the vector components.norminfThe infinity norm, which is the maximum of the absolute values of the vector components.
- Returns: The function returns a float value that represents the norm of the vector using the specified norm type. An error is returned if norm_type isn't a valid norm type and if the vector isn't of the vector data type.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$A03CA024753CE7A3E7327C9BB5C169A6.VectorNorm1

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$A03CA024753CE7A3E7327C9BB5C169A6.VectorNorm1`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Use VECTOR_NORM to take a vector as an input and return the norm of the vector (which is a measure of its length or magnitude) in the norm1 type.
- Returns: The function returns a float value that represents the norm of the vector using the specified norm type. An error is returned if norm_type isn't a valid norm type and if the vector isn't of the vector data type.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$A03CA024753CE7A3E7327C9BB5C169A6.VectorNorm2

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$A03CA024753CE7A3E7327C9BB5C169A6.VectorNorm2`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Use VECTOR_NORM to take a vector as an input and return the norm of the vector (which is a measure of its length or magnitude) in the norm2 type.
- Returns: The function returns a float value that represents the norm of the vector using the specified norm type. An error is returned if norm_type isn't a valid norm type and if the vector isn't of the vector data type.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$A03CA024753CE7A3E7327C9BB5C169A6.VectorNormalize

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$A03CA024753CE7A3E7327C9BB5C169A6.VectorNormalize(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.NormType)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Use VECTOR_NORMALIZE to take a vector as an input and return the normalized vector scaled to have a length of 1 in a given norm type.
- Parameters:
  - `normType`: A string with the name of the norm type to use to calculate the norm of the given vector. The following norm types are supported: norm1The 1-norm, which is the sum of the absolute values of the vector components.norm2The 2-norm, also known as the Euclidean Norm, which is the square root of the sum of the squares of the vector components.norminfThe infinity norm, which is the maximum of the absolute values of the vector components.
- Returns: The result is a vector with the same direction as the input vector but with a length of 1 according to the given norm. If the input is NULL, the returned result is also NULL. An error is returned if norm_type isn't a valid norm type and if the vector isn't of the vector data type.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$A03CA024753CE7A3E7327C9BB5C169A6.VectorNormalize1

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$A03CA024753CE7A3E7327C9BB5C169A6.VectorNormalize1`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Use VECTOR_NORMALIZE to take a vector as an input and return the normalized vector scaled to have a length of 1 in the norm1 type.
- Returns: The result is a vector with the same direction as the input vector but with a length of 1 according to the given norm. If the input is NULL, the returned result is also NULL. An error is returned if norm_type isn't a valid norm type and if the vector isn't of the vector data type.

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$A03CA024753CE7A3E7327C9BB5C169A6.VectorNormalize2`
- Kind: Method

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$A03CA024753CE7A3E7327C9BB5C169A6.VectorNormInf`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Use VECTOR_NORM to take a vector as an input and return the norm of the vector (which is a measure of its length or magnitude) in the norminf type.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$A03CA024753CE7A3E7327C9BB5C169A6.VectorProperty

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$A03CA024753CE7A3E7327C9BB5C169A6.VectorProperty(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.VectorPropertyType)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: The VECTORPROPERTY function returns specific properties of a given vector. The function requires two arguments: the vector itself and the property to be retrieved.
- Parameters:
  - `property`: An expression specifying the name of the database property to return. DimensionsReturn vector's dimensions count.BaseTypeReturn vector's base type.
- Returns: The function returns the specific properties of a given vector based on the property selected. For example: If the property is Dimensions, the function returns an integer value representing the dimension count of the vector.If the property is BaseType, the function returns the name of the data type(sysname).

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$BAC44226FEFE1ED1B549A4B5F35748C7`1.VectorNorm(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.NormType)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Use VECTOR_NORM to take a vector as an input and return the norm of the vector (which is a measure of its length or magnitude) in a given norm type.
- Parameters:
  - `normType`: A string with the name of the norm type to use to calculate the norm of the given vector. The following norm types are supported: norm1The 1-norm, which is the sum of the absolute values of the vector components.norm2The 2-norm, also known as the Euclidean Norm, which is the square root of the sum of the squares of the vector components.norminfThe infinity norm, which is the maximum of the absolute values of the vector components.
- Returns: The function returns a float value that represents the norm of the vector using the specified norm type. An error is returned if norm_type isn't a valid norm type and if the vector isn't of the vector data type.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$BAC44226FEFE1ED1B549A4B5F35748C7<T>.VectorNorm1

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$BAC44226FEFE1ED1B549A4B5F35748C7`1.VectorNorm1`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Use VECTOR_NORM to take a vector as an input and return the norm of the vector (which is a measure of its length or magnitude) in the norm1 type.
- Returns: The function returns a float value that represents the norm of the vector using the specified norm type. An error is returned if norm_type isn't a valid norm type and if the vector isn't of the vector data type.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$BAC44226FEFE1ED1B549A4B5F35748C7<T>.VectorNorm2

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$BAC44226FEFE1ED1B549A4B5F35748C7`1.VectorNorm2`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Use VECTOR_NORM to take a vector as an input and return the norm of the vector (which is a measure of its length or magnitude) in the norm2 type.
- Returns: The function returns a float value that represents the norm of the vector using the specified norm type. An error is returned if norm_type isn't a valid norm type and if the vector isn't of the vector data type.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$BAC44226FEFE1ED1B549A4B5F35748C7<T>.VectorNormalize

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$BAC44226FEFE1ED1B549A4B5F35748C7`1.VectorNormalize(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.NormType)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Use VECTOR_NORMALIZE to take a vector as an input and return the normalized vector scaled to have a length of 1 in a given norm type.
- Parameters:
  - `normType`: A string with the name of the norm type to use to calculate the norm of the given vector. The following norm types are supported: norm1The 1-norm, which is the sum of the absolute values of the vector components.norm2The 2-norm, also known as the Euclidean Norm, which is the square root of the sum of the squares of the vector components.norminfThe infinity norm, which is the maximum of the absolute values of the vector components.
- Returns: The result is a vector with the same direction as the input vector but with a length of 1 according to the given norm. If the input is NULL, the returned result is also NULL. An error is returned if norm_type isn't a valid norm type and if the vector isn't of the vector data type.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$BAC44226FEFE1ED1B549A4B5F35748C7<T>.VectorNormalize1

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$BAC44226FEFE1ED1B549A4B5F35748C7`1.VectorNormalize1`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Use VECTOR_NORMALIZE to take a vector as an input and return the normalized vector scaled to have a length of 1 in the norm1 type.
- Returns: The result is a vector with the same direction as the input vector but with a length of 1 according to the given norm. If the input is NULL, the returned result is also NULL. An error is returned if norm_type isn't a valid norm type and if the vector isn't of the vector data type.

- Kind: Method
- Summary: Use VECTOR_NORMALIZE to take a vector as an input and return the normalized vector scaled to have a length of 1 in the norminf type.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$BAC44226FEFE1ED1B549A4B5F35748C7<T>.VectorNormInf

- Kind: Method
- Summary: Use VECTOR_NORM to take a vector as an input and return the norm of the vector (which is a measure of its length or magnitude) in the norminf type.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$BAC44226FEFE1ED1B549A4B5F35748C7<T>.VectorProperty

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.<G>$BAC44226FEFE1ED1B549A4B5F35748C7`1.VectorProperty(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.VectorPropertyType)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: The VECTORPROPERTY function returns specific properties of a given vector. The function requires two arguments: the vector itself and the property to be retrieved.
- Parameters:
  - `property`: An expression specifying the name of the database property to return. DimensionsReturn vector's dimensions count.BaseTypeReturn vector's base type.
- Returns: The function returns the specific properties of a given vector based on the property selected. For example: If the property is Dimensions, the function returns an integer value representing the dimension count of the vector.If the property is BaseType, the function returns the name of the data type(sysname).

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Abs<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Abs``1(``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: ABS (Transact-SQL)A mathematical function that returns the absolute (positive) value of the specified numeric expression. (ABS changes negative values to positive values. ABS has no effect on zero or positive values.)
- Parameters:
  - `numeric_expression`: An expression of the exact numeric or approximate numeric data type category.
- Returns: Returns the same type as numeric_expression.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Acos<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Acos``1(``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: ACOS (Transact-SQL)A function that returns the angle, in radians, whose cosine is the specified float expression. This is also called arccosine.
- Parameters:
  - `float_expression`: An expression of either type float or of a type that can implicitly convert to float. Only a value ranging from -1.00 to 1.00 is valid. For values outside this range, no value is returned, and ACOS will report a domain error.
- Returns: float

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.AppName

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.AppName`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: APP_NAME (Transact-SQL)This function returns the application name for the current session, if the application sets that name value.
- Returns: nvarchar(128)

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Ascii

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Ascii(System.Char)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: ASCII (Transact-SQL)Returns the ASCII code value of the leftmost character of a character expression.
- Parameters:
  - `character_expression`: An expression of type char or varchar.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Ascii

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Ascii(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: ASCII (Transact-SQL)Returns the ASCII code value of the leftmost character of a character expression.
- Parameters:
  - `character_expression`: An expression of type char or varchar.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Asin<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Asin``1(``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: ASIN (Transact-SQL)A function that returns the angle, in radians, whose sine is the specified float expression. This is also called arcsine.
- Parameters:
  - `float_expression`: An expression of either type float or of a type that can implicitly convert to float. Only a value ranging from -1.00 to 1.00 is valid. For values outside this range, no value is returned, and ASIN will report a domain error.
- Returns: float

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Atan<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Atan``1(``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: ATAN (Transact-SQL)A function that returns the angle, in radians, whose tangent is a specified float expression. This is also called arctangent.
- Parameters:
  - `float_expression`: An expression of either type float or of a type that implicitly convert to float.
- Returns: float

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Atn2<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Atn2``1(``0,``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: ATN2 (Transact-SQL)Returns the angle, in radians, between the positive x-axis and the ray from the origin to the point (y, x), where x and y are the values of the two specified float expressions.
- Parameters:
  - `float_expression`: An expression of type float.
- Returns: float

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.BinaryCheckSum

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.BinaryCheckSum`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: BINARY_CHECKSUM (Transact-SQL)Returns the binary checksum value computed over a row of a table or over a list of expressions.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.BinaryCheckSum

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.BinaryCheckSum(System.Object[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: BINARY_CHECKSUM (Transact-SQL)Returns the binary checksum value computed over a row of a table or over a list of expressions.
- Parameters:
  - `expressions`: An expression of any type. BINARY_CHECKSUM ignores expressions of noncomparable data types in its computation.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Cast<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Cast``1(System.Object,LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlType{``0})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: CAST and CONVERT (Transact-SQL)These functions convert an expression of one data type to another.
- Parameters:
  - `expression`: Any valid expression.
  - `data_type`: The target data type. This includes xml, bigint, and sql_variant. Alias data types cannot be used.
- Returns: Returns expression, translated to data_type.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Ceiling<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Ceiling``1(``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: CEILING (Transact-SQL)This function returns the smallest integer greater than, or equal to, the specified numeric expression.
- Parameters:
  - `numeric_expression`: An expression of the exact numeric or approximate numeric data type category. For this function, the bit data type is invalid.
- Returns: Return values have the same type as numeric_expression.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Char

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Char(System.Nullable{System.Int32})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: CHAR (Transact-SQL)Returns the ASCII code value of the leftmost character of a character expression.
- Parameters:
  - `integer_expression`: An integer from 0 through 255. CHAR returns a NULL value for integer expressions outside this input range or not representing a complete character. CHAR also returns a NULL value when the character exceeds the length of the return type. Many common character sets share ASCII as a sub-set and will return the same character for integer values in the range 0 through 127.
- Returns: char(1)

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.CharIndex

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.CharIndex(System.String,System.String,System.Nullable{System.Int32})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: CHARINDEX (Transact-SQL)This function searches for one character expression inside a second character expression, returning the starting position of the first expression if found.
- Parameters:
  - `expressionToFind`: A character expression containing the sequence to find. expressionToFind has an 8000 character limit.
  - `expressionToSearch`: A character expression to search.
  - `start_location`: An integer or bigint expression at which the search starts. If start_location is not specified, has a negative value, or has a zero (0) value, the search starts at the beginning of expressionToSearch.
- Returns: bigint if expressionToSearch has an nvarchar(max), varbinary(max), or varchar(max) data type; int otherwise.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.CharIndex

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.CharIndex(System.String,System.String,System.Nullable{System.Int64})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: CHARINDEX (Transact-SQL)This function searches for one character expression inside a second character expression, returning the starting position of the first expression if found.
- Parameters:
  - `expressionToFind`: A character expression containing the sequence to find. expressionToFind has an 8000 character limit.
  - `expressionToSearch`: A character expression to search.
  - `start_location`: An integer or bigint expression at which the search starts. If start_location is not specified, has a negative value, or has a zero (0) value, the search starts at the beginning of expressionToSearch.
- Returns: bigint if expressionToSearch has an nvarchar(max), varbinary(max), or varchar(max) data type; int otherwise.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.CheckSum

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.CheckSum`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: CHECKSUM (Transact-SQL)The CHECKSUM function returns the checksum value computed over a table row, or over an expression list. Use CHECKSUM to build hash indexes.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.CheckSum

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.CheckSum(System.Object[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: CHECKSUM (Transact-SQL)The CHECKSUM function returns the checksum value computed over a table row, or over an expression list. Use CHECKSUM to build hash indexes.
- Parameters:
  - `expressions`: An expression of any type, except a noncomparable data type.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Choose<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Choose``1(System.Nullable{System.Int32},``0[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: CHOOSE (Transact-SQL)Returns the last identity value generated for a specified table or view. The last identity value generated can be for any session and any scope.
- Parameters:
  - `index`: Is an integer expression that represents a 1-based index into the list of the items following it.
  - `values`: List of comma separated values of any data type.
- Returns: Returns the data type with the highest precedence from the set of types passed to the function.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Collate

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Collate(System.String,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: COLLATE (Transact-SQL)Returns a character expression after converting uppercase character data to lowercase.Windows_collation_name is the collation name for a Windows Collation Name.Collation and Unicode support
- Parameters:
  - `collation_name`: Is the name of the collation to be applied to the expression, column definition, or database definition. collation_name can be only a specified Windows_collation_name or a SQL_collation_name. collation_name must be a literal value. collation_name cannot be represented by a variable or expression.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ColumnLength

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ColumnLength(System.String,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: COL_LENGTH (Transact-SQL)This function returns the defined length of a column, in bytes.
- Parameters:
  - `table`: The name of the table whose column length information we want to determine. table is an expression of type nvarchar.
  - `column`: The column name whose length we want to determine. column is an expression of type nvarchar.
- Returns: smallint

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ColumnName

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ColumnName(System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Int32)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: COL_NAME (Transact-SQL)This function returns the name of a table column, based on the table identification number and column identification number values of that table column.
- Parameters:
  - `table_id`: The identification number of the table containing that column. The table_id argument has an int data type.
  - `column_id`: The identification number of the column. The column_id argument has an int data type.
- Returns: sysname

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ColumnProperty

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ColumnProperty(System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.String,LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ColumnPropertyName)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: COLUMNPROPERTY (Transact-SQL)This function returns column or parameter information.
- Parameters:
  - `id`: An expression containing the identifier (ID) of the table or procedure.
  - `column`: An expression containing the name of the column or parameter.
  - `property`: For the id argument, the property argument specifies the information type that the COLUMNPROPERTY function will return.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Compress

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Compress(System.Byte[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: COMPRESS (Transact-SQL)This function compresses the input expression, using the GZIP algorithm. The function returns a byte array of type varbinary(max).
- Parameters:
  - `expression`: A binary(n)char(n)nchar(n)nvarchar(max)nvarchar(n)varbinary(max)varbinary(n)varchar(max) or varchar(n) expression.
- Returns: varbinary(max) representing the compressed content of the input.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Compress

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Compress(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: COMPRESS (Transact-SQL)This function compresses the input expression, using the GZIP algorithm. The function returns a byte array of type varbinary(max).
- Parameters:
  - `expression`: A binary(n)char(n)nchar(n)nvarchar(max)nvarchar(n)varbinary(max)varbinary(n)varchar(max) or varchar(n) expression.
- Returns: varbinary(max) representing the compressed content of the input.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Concat

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Concat(System.String[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: CONCAT (Transact-SQL)This function returns a string resulting from the concatenation, or joining, of two or more string values in an end-to-end manner.
- Parameters:
  - `string_value`: A string value to concatenate to the other values. The CONCAT function requires at least two string_value arguments, and no more than 254 string_value arguments.
- Returns: string_value

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ConcatWithSeparator

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ConcatWithSeparator(System.String,System.String[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: CONCAT_WS (Transact-SQL)This function returns a string resulting from the concatenation, or joining, of two or more string values in an end-to-end manner. It separates those concatenated string values with the delimiter specified in the first function argument. (CONCAT_WS indicates concatenate with separator.)
- Parameters:
  - `separator`: An expression of any character type (char, nchar, nvarchar, or varchar).
  - `arguments`: An expression of any type. The CONCAT_WS function requires at least two arguments, and no more than 254 arguments.
- Returns: A string value whose length and type depend on the input.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ConnectionProperty

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ConnectionProperty(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ConnectionPropertyName)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: CONNECTIONPROPERTY (Transact-SQL)For a request that comes in to the server, this function returns information about the connection properties of the unique connection which supports that request.
- Parameters:
  - `property`: The property of the connection.
- Returns: int

- Kind: Method
- Summary: CAST and CONVERT (Transact-SQL)These functions convert an expression of one data type to another.
- Parameters:
  - `expression`: Any valid expression.
- Returns: Returns expression, translated to data_type.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Cos<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Cos``1(``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: COS (Transact-SQL)A mathematical function that returns the trigonometric cosine of the specified angle - measured in radians - in the specified expression.
- Parameters:
  - `float_expression`: An expression of type float.
- Returns: float

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Cot<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Cot``1(``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: COT (Transact-SQL)A mathematical function that returns the trigonometric cotangent of the specified angle - in radians - in the specified float expression.
- Parameters:
  - `float_expression`: An expression of type float, or of a type that can implicitly convert to float.
- Returns: float

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.CurrentRequestID

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.CurrentRequestID`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: CURRENT_REQUEST_ID (Transact-SQL)This function returns the ID of the current request within the current session.
- Returns: smallint

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.CurrentTimezone

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.CurrentTimezone`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: CURRENT_TIMEZONE (Transact-SQL)This function returns the name of the time zone observed by a server or an instance. For SQL Managed Instance, return value is based on the time zone of the instance itself assigned during instance creation, not the time zone of the underlying operating system.
- Returns: varchar

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.CurrentTimezoneID

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.CurrentTimezoneID`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: CURRENT_TIMEZONE_ID (Transact-SQL)This function returns the ID of the time zone observed by a server or an instance. For Azure SQL Managed Instance, return value is based on the time zone of the instance itself assigned during instance creation, not the time zone of the underlying operating system.
- Returns: varchar

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.CurrentTransactionID

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.CurrentTransactionID`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: CURRENT_TRANSACTION_ID (Transact-SQL)This function returns the transaction ID of the current transaction in the current session.
- Returns: bigint

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.DatabasePropertyEx

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.DatabasePropertyEx(System.String,LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.DatabasePropertyName)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: DATABASEPROPERTYEX (Transact-SQL)For a specified database in SQL Server, this function returns the current setting of the specified database option or property.
- Parameters:
  - `database`: An expression specifying the name of the database for which DATABASEPROPERTYEX will return the named property information. database has an nvarchar(128) data type.
  - `property`: An expression specifying the name of the database property to return. property has a varchar(128) data type
- Returns: sql_variant

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.DataLength<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.DataLength``1(``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: DATALENGTH (Transact-SQL)This function returns the number of bytes used to represent any expression.DATALENGTH becomes really helpful when used with data types that can store variable-length data, such as: ntextnvarchartextvarbinaryvarchar For a NULL value, DATALENGTH returns NULL. Use the LEN to return the number of characters encoded into a given string expression, and DATALENGTH to return the size in bytes for a given string expression. These outputs may differ depending on the data type and type of encoding used in the column. For more information on storage differences between different encoding types, see Collation and Unicode Support.
- Type parameters:
  - `T`: An expression of any data type.
- Parameters:
  - `expression`: An expression of any data type.
- Returns: bigint if expression has an nvarchar(max), varbinary(max), or varchar(max) data type; otherwise int.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.DataLengthBig<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.DataLengthBig``1(``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: DATALENGTH (Transact-SQL)This function returns the number of bytes used to represent any expression.DATALENGTH becomes really helpful when used with data types that can store variable-length data, such as: ntextnvarchartextvarbinaryvarchar For a NULL value, DATALENGTH returns NULL. Use the LEN to return the number of characters encoded into a given string expression, and DATALENGTH to return the size in bytes for a given string expression. These outputs may differ depending on the data type and type of encoding used in the column. For more information on storage differences between different encoding types, see Collation and Unicode Support.
- Type parameters:
  - `T`: An expression of any data type.
- Parameters:
  - `expression`: An expression of any data type.
- Returns: bigint if expression has an nvarchar(max), varbinary(max), or varchar(max) data type; otherwise int.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.DateAdd

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.DateAdd(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.DateParts,System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.DateTime})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: DATEADD (Transact-SQL)This function adds a number (a signed integer) to a datepart of an input date, and returns a modified date/time value.
- Parameters:
  - `datepart`: The part of date to which DATEADD adds an integer number. This table lists all valid datepart arguments.
  - `number`: An expression that can resolve to an int that DATEADD adds to a datepart of date. DATEADD accepts user-defined variable values for number. DATEADD will truncate a specified number value that has a decimal fraction. It will not round the number value in this situation.
- Returns: varchar

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.DateAdd

- Returns: varchar

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Decompress

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Decompress(System.Byte[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: DECOMPRESS (Transact-SQL)This function will decompress an input expression value, using the GZIP algorithm. DECOMPRESS will return a byte array (VARBINARY(MAX) type).
- Parameters:
  - `expression`: A varbinary(n), varbinary(max), or binary(n) value.
- Returns: varbinary(max) representing the compressed content of the input.

- Returns: Return values have the same type as numeric_expression.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.EndOfMonth

- Kind: Method
- Summary: EOMONTH (Transact-SQL)This function returns the last day of the month containing a specified date, with an optional offset.
- Parameters:

  - `month_to_add`: An optional integer expression that specifies the number of months to add to start_date.
- Returns: date
