- Kind: Method
- Summary: EOMONTH (Transact-SQL)This function returns the last day of the month containing a specified date, with an optional offset.
- Parameters:
  - `start_date`: A date expression that specifies the date for which to return the last day of the month.
  - `month_to_add`: An optional integer expression that specifies the number of months to add to start_date.
- Returns: date

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.EndOfMonth

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.EndOfMonth(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: EOMONTH (Transact-SQL)This function returns the last day of the month containing a specified date, with an optional offset.
- Parameters:
  - `start_date`: A date expression that specifies the date for which to return the last day of the month.
- Returns: date

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.EuclideanVectorDistance

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.EuclideanVectorDistance(System.Single[],System.Single[])`
- Kind: Method

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.EuclideanVectorDistance<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.EuclideanVectorDistance``1(``0,``0)`
- Kind: Method

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Exp<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Exp``1(``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: EXP (Transact-SQL)Returns the exponential value of the specified float expression.
- Parameters:
  - `float_expression`: Is an expression of type float or of a type that can be implicitly converted to float.
- Returns: float

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FileGroupID

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FileGroupID(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: FILEGROUP_ID (Transact-SQL)This function returns the filegroup identification (ID) number for a specified filegroup name.
- Parameters:
  - `filegroup_name`: An expression of type sysname, representing the filegroup name whose filegroup ID FILEGROUP_ID will return.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FileGroupName

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FileGroupName(System.Nullable{System.Int16})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: FILEGROUP_NAME (Transact-SQL)This function returns the filegroup name for the specified filegroup identification (ID) number.
- Parameters:
  - `filegroup_id`: The filegroup ID number whose filegroup name FILEGROUP_NAME will return. filegroup_id has a smallint data type.
- Returns: nvarchar(128)

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FileGroupProperty

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FileGroupProperty(System.String,LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FileGroupPropertyName)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: FILEGROUPPROPERTY (Transact-SQL)This function returns the filegroup property value for a specified name and filegroup value.
- Parameters:
  - `filegroup_name`: An expression of type sysname that represents the filegroup name for which FILEGROUPPROPERTY returns the named property information.
  - `property`: An expression of type varchar(128) that returns the name of the filegroup property.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FileID

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FileID(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: FILE_ID (Transact-SQL)For the given logical name for a component file of the current database, this function returns the file identification (ID) number.
- Parameters:
  - `file_name`: An expression of type sysname, representing the logical name of the file whose file ID value FILE_ID will return.
- Returns: smallint

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FileIDEx

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FileIDEx(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: FILE_IDEX (Transact-SQL)This function returns the file identification (ID) number for the specified logical name of a data, log, or full-text file of the current database.
- Parameters:
  - `file_name`: An expression of type sysname that returns the file ID value 'FILE_IDEX' for the name of the file.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FileName

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FileName(System.Nullable{System.Int32})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: FILE_NAME (Transact-SQL)This function returns the logical file name for a given file identification (ID) number.
- Parameters:
  - `file_id`: The file identification number whose file name FILE_NAME will return. file_id has an int data type.
- Returns: nvarchar(128)

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FileProperty

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FileProperty(System.String,LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FilePropertyName)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: FILEPROPERTY (Transact-SQL)Returns the specified file name property value when a file name in the current database and a property name are specified. Returns NULL for files that are not in the current database.
- Parameters:
  - `file_name`: Is an expression that contains the name of the file associated with the current database for which to return property information. file_name is nchar(128).
  - `property`: Is an expression that contains the name of the file property to return. property is varchar(128), and can be one of the following values.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FilePropertyEx

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FilePropertyEx(System.String,LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FilePropertyExName)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: FILEPROPERTYEX (Transact-SQL)Returns the specified extended file property value when a file name in the current database and a property name are specified. Returns NULL for files that are not in the current database or for extended file properties that do not exist. Currently, extended file properties only apply to databases that are in Azure Blob storage.
- Parameters:
  - `file_name`: Is an expression that contains the name of the file associated with the current database for which to return property information. file_name is nchar(128).
  - `property`: Is an expression that contains the name of the file property to return. property is varchar(128), and can be one of the following values.
- Returns: sql_variant

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Floor<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Floor``1(``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: FLOOR (Transact-SQL)Returns the largest integer less than or equal to the specified numeric expression.
- Parameters:
  - `numeric_expression`: An expression of the exact numeric or approximate numeric data type category, except for the bit data type.
- Returns: Return values have the same type as numeric_expression.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Format

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Format(System.Object,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: FORMAT (Transact-SQL)Returns a value formatted with the specified format and optional culture. Use the FORMAT function for locale-aware formatting of date/time and number values as strings. For general data type conversions, use CAST or CONVERT.
- Parameters:
  - `value`: Expression of a supported data type to format. For a list of valid types, see the table in the following Remarks section.
- Returns: nvarchar or null

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FormatMessage

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FormatMessage(System.Int32,System.Object[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: FORMATMESSAGE (Transact-SQL)Constructs a message from an existing message in sys.messages or from a provided string. The functionality of FORMATMESSAGE resembles that of the RAISERROR statement. However, RAISERROR prints the message immediately, while FORMATMESSAGE returns the formatted message for further processing.
- Parameters:
  - `msg_number`: Is the ID of the message stored in sys.messages. If msg_number is <= 13000, or if the message does not exist in sys.messages, NULL is returned.
  - `param_values`: Is a parameter value for use in the message. Can be more than one parameter value. The values must be specified in the order in which the placeholder variables appear in the message. The maximum number of values is 20.
- Returns: nvarchar

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FormatMessage

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FormatMessage(System.String,System.Object[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: FORMATMESSAGE (Transact-SQL)Constructs a message from an existing message in sys.messages or from a provided string. The functionality of FORMATMESSAGE resembles that of the RAISERROR statement. However, RAISERROR prints the message immediately, while FORMATMESSAGE returns the formatted message for further processing.
- Parameters:
  - `msg_string`: Is a string enclosed in single quotes and containing parameter value placeholders. The error message can have a maximum of 2,047 characters. If the message contains 2,048 or more characters, only the first 2,044 are displayed and an ellipsis is added to indicate that the message has been truncated. Note that substitution parameters consume more characters than the output shows because of internal storage behavior.
  - `param_values`: Is a parameter value for use in the message. Can be more than one parameter value. The values must be specified in the order in which the placeholder variables appear in the message. The maximum number of values is 20.
- Returns: nvarchar

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FullTextCatalogProperty

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FullTextCatalogProperty(System.String,LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FullTextCatalogPropertyName)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: FULLTEXTCATALOGPROPERTY (Transact-SQL)Returns information about full-text catalog properties in SQL Server.
- Parameters:
  - `catalog_name`: Is an expression containing the name of the full-text catalog.
  - `property`: Is an expression containing the name of the full-text catalog property. The table lists the properties and provides descriptions of the information returned.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FullTextServiceProperty

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FullTextServiceProperty(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.FullTextServicePropertyName)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: FULLTEXTSERVICEPROPERTY (Transact-SQL)Returns information related to the properties of the Full-Text Engine. These properties can be set and retrieved by using sp_fulltext_service.
- Parameters:
  - `property`: Is an expression containing the name of the full-text service-level property. The table lists the properties and provides descriptions of the information returned.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.GetAnsiNull

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.GetAnsiNull`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: GETANSINULL (Transact-SQL)Returns the default nullability for the database for this session.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.GetAnsiNull

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.GetAnsiNull(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: GETANSINULL (Transact-SQL)Returns the default nullability for the database for this session.
- Parameters:
  - `database`: Is the name of the database for which to return nullability information. database is either char or nchar. If char, database is implicitly converted to nchar.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.GetDate

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.GetDate`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: GETDATE (Transact-SQL)Returns the current database system timestamp as a datetime value without the database time zone offset.
- Returns: datetime

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.GetUtcDate

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.GetUtcDate`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: GETUTCDATE (Transact-SQL)Returns the current database system timestamp as a datetime value. The database time zone offset is not included. This value represents the current UTC time (Coordinated Universal Time). This value is derived from the operating system of the computer on which the instance of SQL Server is running.
- Returns: datetime

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.HostID

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.HostID`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: HOST_ID (Transact-SQL)Returns the workstation identification number. The workstation identification number is the process ID (PID) of the application on the client computer that is connecting to SQL Server.
- Returns: char(10)

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.HostName

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.HostName`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: HOST_NAME (Transact-SQL)Returns the workstation identification number. The workstation identification number is the process ID (PID) of the application on the client computer that is connecting to SQL Server.
- Returns: nvarchar(128)

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.IdentityCurrent

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.IdentityCurrent(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: IDENT_CURRENT (Transact-SQL)Returns the last identity value generated for a specified table or view. The last identity value generated can be for any session and any scope.
- Parameters:
  - `table_or_view`: Is the name of the table or view whose identity value is returned. table_or_view is varchar, with no default.
- Returns: numeric(@@MAXPRECISION, 0))

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.IdentityIncrement

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.IdentityIncrement(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: IDENT_INCR (Transact-SQL)Returns the increment value specified when creating a table or view's identity column.
- Parameters:
  - `table_or_view`: Is an expression specifying the table or view to check for a valid identity increment value. table_or_view can be a character string constant enclosed in quotation marks. It can also be a variable, a function, or a column name. table_or_view is char, nchar, varchar, or nvarchar.
- Returns: numeric(@@MAXPRECISION, 0))

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.IdentitySeed

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.IdentitySeed(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: IDENT_SEED (Transact-SQL)Returns the original seed value specified when creating an identity column in a table or a view. Changing the current value of an identity column by using DBCC CHECKIDENT doesn't change the value returned by this function.
- Parameters:
  - `table_or_view`: Is an expression specifying the table or view to check for a valid identity increment value. table_or_view can be a character string constant enclosed in quotation marks. It can also be a variable, a function, or a column name. table_or_view is char, nchar, varchar, or nvarchar.
- Returns: numeric(@@MAXPRECISION, 0))

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Iif<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Iif``1(System.Nullable{System.Boolean},``0,``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: IIF (Transact-SQL)Returns one of two values, depending on whether the Boolean expression evaluates to true or false in SQL Server.
- Parameters:
  - `boolean_expression`: A valid Boolean expression.
  - `true_value`: Value to return if boolean_expression evaluates to true.
  - `false_value`: Value to return if boolean_expression evaluates to false.
- Returns: Returns the data type with the highest precedence from the types in true_value and false_value.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.IndexColumn

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.IndexColumn(System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: INDEX_COL (Transact-SQL)Returns the indexed column name. Returns NULL for XML indexes.
- Parameters:
  - `table_or_view`: Is the name of the table or indexed view. table_or_view_name must be delimited by single quotation marks and can be fully qualified by database name and schema name.
  - `index_id`: Is the ID of the index. index_ID is int.
  - `key_id`: Is the index key column position. key_ID is int.
- Returns: nvarchar(128)

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.IndexKeyProperty

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.IndexKeyProperty(System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32},LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.IndexKeyPropertyName)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: INDEXKEY_PROPERTY (Transact-SQL)Returns information about the index key. Returns NULL for XML indexes.
- Parameters:
  - `object_ID`: Is the object identification number of the table or indexed view. object_ID is int.
  - `index_ID`: Is the index identification number. index_ID is int.
  - `key_ID`: Is the index key column position. key_ID is int.
  - `property`: Is the name of the property for which information will be returned. property is a character string and can be one of the following values.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.IndexProperty

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.IndexProperty(System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.String,LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.IndexPropertyName)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: INDEXPROPERTY (Transact-SQL)Returns information about the index key. Returns NULL for XML indexes.
- Parameters:
  - `object_ID`: Is an expression that contains the object identification number of the table or indexed view for which to provide index property information. object_ID is int.
  - `index_or_statistics_name`: Is an expression that contains the name of the index or statistics for which to return property information. index_or_statistics_name is nvarchar(128).
  - `property`: Is an expression that contains the name of the database property to return. property is varchar(128), and can be one of these values.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.IsDate

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.IsDate(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: ISDATE (Transact-SQL)Returns 1 if the expression is a valid datetime value; otherwise, 0. ISDATE returns 0 if the expression is a datetime2 value.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.IsJson

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.IsJson(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: ISJSON (Transact-SQL)Tests whether a string contains valid JSON.
- Parameters:
  - `expression`: The string to test.
- Returns: Returns 1 if the string contains valid JSON; otherwise, returns 0. Returns null if expression is null.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.IsNull<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.IsNull``1(``0,``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: ISNULL (Transact-SQL)Replaces NULL with the specified replacement value.
- Parameters:
  - `check_expression`: Is the expression to be checked for NULL. check_expression can be of any type.
  - `replacement_value`: Is the expression to be returned if check_expression is NULL. replacement_value must be of a type that is implicitly convertible to the type of check_expression.
- Returns: Returns the same type as check_expression. If a literal NULL is provided as check_expression, returns the datatype of the replacement_value. If a literal NULL is provided as check_expression and no replacement_value is provided, returns an int.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.IsNumeric<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.IsNumeric``1(``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: ISNUMERIC (Transact-SQL)Determines whether an expression is a valid numeric type.
- Parameters:
  - `expression`: Is the expression to be evaluated.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.JsonModify

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.JsonModify(System.String,System.String,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: JSON_MODIFY (Transact-SQL)Updates the value of a property in a JSON string and returns the updated JSON string.
- Parameters:
  - `expression`: An expression. Typically the name of a variable or a column that contains JSON text.
  - `path`: A JSON path expression that specifies the property to update.
  - `newValue`: The new value for the property specified by path. The new value must be a [n]varchar or text.
- Returns: Returns the updated value of expression as properly formatted JSON text.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.JsonQuery

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.JsonQuery(System.String,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: JSON_QUERY (Transact-SQL)Extracts an object or an array from a JSON string.
- Parameters:
  - `expression`: An expression. Typically the name of a variable or a column that contains JSON text.
  - `path`: A JSON path that specifies the property to extract.
- Returns: Returns a JSON fragment of type nvarchar(max). The collation of the returned value is the same as the collation of the input expression.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.JsonValue

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.JsonValue(System.String,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: JSON_VALUE (Transact-SQL)Extracts a scalar value from a JSON string.
- Parameters:
  - `expression`: An expression. Typically the name of a variable or a column that contains JSON text.
  - `path`: A JSON path that specifies the property to extract.
- Returns: Returns a single text value of type nvarchar(4000). The collation of the returned value is the same as the collation of the input expression.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Left

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Left(System.String,System.Nullable{System.Int32})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: LEFT (Transact-SQL)Returns the left part of a character string with the specified number of characters.
- Parameters:
  - `character_expression`: Is an expression of character or binary data. character_expression can be a constant, variable, or column. character_expression can be of any data type, except text or ntext, that can be implicitly converted to varchar or nvarchar. Otherwise, use the CAST function to explicitly convert character_expression.
  - `integer_expression`: Is a positive integer that specifies how many characters of the character_expression will be returned. If integer_expression is negative, an error is returned. If integer_expression is type bigint and contains a large value, character_expression must be of a large data type such as varchar(max). The integer_expression parameter counts a UTF-16 surrogate character as one character.
- Returns: Returns varchar when character_expression is a non-Unicode character data type. Returns nvarchar when character_expression is a Unicode character data type.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.LeftTrim

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.LeftTrim(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: LTRIM (Transact-SQL)Returns a character expression after it removes leading blanks.
- Parameters:
  - `character_expression`: Is the string expression of character or binary data. character_expression can be a constant, variable, or column. character_expression must be of a data type that is implicitly convertible to varchar.
- Returns: varchar or nvarchar

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Len

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Len(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: LEN (Transact-SQL)Returns the number of characters of the specified string expression, excluding trailing spaces.
- Parameters:
  - `character_expression`: Is the string expression to be evaluated. character_expression can be a constant, variable, or column of either character or binary data.
- Returns: bigint if expression is of the varchar(max), nvarchar(max) or varbinary(max) data types; otherwise, int.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.LenBig

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.LenBig(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: LEN (Transact-SQL)Returns the number of characters of the specified string expression, excluding trailing spaces.
- Parameters:
  - `character_expression`: Is the string expression to be evaluated. character_expression can be a constant, variable, or column of either character or binary data.
- Returns: bigint if expression is of the varchar(max), nvarchar(max) or varbinary(max) data types; otherwise, int.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Log<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Log``1(``0,System.Int32)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: LOG (Transact-SQL)Returns the natural logarithm of the specified float expression in SQL Server.
- Parameters:
  - `float_expression`: Is an expression of type float or of a type that can be implicitly converted to float.
  - `base`: Optional integer argument that sets the base for the logarithm.
- Returns: float

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Log<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Log``1(``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: LOG (Transact-SQL)Returns the natural logarithm of the specified float expression in SQL Server.
- Parameters:
  - `float_expression`: Is an expression of type float or of a type that can be implicitly converted to float.
- Returns: float

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Log10<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Log10``1(``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: LOG10 (Transact-SQL)Returns the base-10 logarithm of the specified float expression.
- Parameters:
  - `float_expression`: Is an expression of type float or of a type that can be implicitly converted to float.
- Returns: float

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Lower

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Lower(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: LOWER (Transact-SQL)Returns a character expression after converting uppercase character data to lowercase.
- Parameters:
  - `character_expression`: Is the string expression of character or binary data. character_expression can be a constant, variable, or column. character_expression must be of a data type that is implicitly convertible to varchar.
- Returns: varchar or nvarchar

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.MinActiveRowVersion

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.MinActiveRowVersion`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: MIN_ACTIVE_ROWVERSION (Transact-SQL)Returns the workstation identification number. The workstation identification number is the process ID (PID) of the application on the client computer that is connecting to SQL Server.
- Returns: Returns a binary(8) value.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Month

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Month(System.Nullable{System.DateTime})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: MONTH (Transact-SQL)This function returns an integer that represents the day (day of the month) of the specified date.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Month

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Month(System.Nullable{System.DateTimeOffset})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: MONTH (Transact-SQL)This function returns an integer that represents the day (day of the month) of the specified date.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Month

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Month(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: MONTH (Transact-SQL)Returns an integer that represents the month of the specified date.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.NChar

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.NChar(System.Nullable{System.Int32})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: NCHAR (Transact-SQL)Returns the Unicode character with the specified integer code, as defined by the Unicode standard.
- Parameters:
  - `integer_expression`: When the collation of the database does not contain the Supplementary Character (SC) flag, this is a positive integer from 0 through 65535 (0 through 0xFFFF). If a value outside this range is specified, NULL is returned.
- Returns: nchar(1) when the default database collation does not support supplementary characters. nvarchar(2) when the default database collation supports supplementary characters.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.NewID

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.NewID`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: NEWID (Transact-SQL)Creates a unique value of type uniqueidentifier.
- Returns: uniqueidentifier

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.NextValueFor

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.NextValueFor(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: NEXT VALUE FOR (Transact-SQL)Generates a sequence number from the specified sequence object.
- Parameters:
  - `sequence_name`: The name of the sequence object that generates the number.
- Returns: Returns a number using the type of the sequence.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.NextValueForOver

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.NextValueForOver(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: NEXT VALUE FOR (Transact-SQL)Generates a sequence number from the specified sequence object.
- Parameters:
  - `sequence_name`: The name of the sequence object that generates the number.
- Returns: Returns a number using the type of the sequence.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ObjectDefinition

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ObjectDefinition(System.Nullable{System.Int32})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: OBJECT_DEFINITION (Transact-SQL)Returns the Transact-SQL source text of the definition of a specified object.
- Parameters:
  - `object_id`: Is the ID of the object to be used. object_id is int, and assumed to represent an object in the current database context.
- Returns: nvarchar(max)

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ObjectID

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ObjectID(System.String,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: OBJECT_ID (Transact-SQL)Returns the database object identification number of a schema-scoped object.
- Parameters:
  - `object_name`: Is the object to be used. object_name is either varchar or nvarchar. If object_name is varchar, it is implicitly converted to nvarchar. Specifying the database and schema names is optional.
  - `object_type`: Is the schema-scoped object type. object_type is either varchar or nvarchar. If object_type is varchar, it is implicitly converted to nvarchar. For a list of object types, see the type column in sys.objects (Transact-SQL).
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ObjectID

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ObjectID(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: OBJECT_ID (Transact-SQL)Returns the database object identification number of a schema-scoped object.
- Parameters:
  - `object_name`: Is the object to be used. object_name is either varchar or nvarchar. If object_name is varchar, it is implicitly converted to nvarchar. Specifying the database and schema names is optional.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ObjectName

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ObjectName(System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: OBJECT_NAME (Transact-SQL)Returns the database object name for schema-scoped objects. For a list of schema-scoped objects, see sys.objects (Transact-SQL).
- Parameters:
  - `object_id`: Is the ID of the object to be used. object_id is int and is assumed to be a schema-scoped object in the specified database, or in the current database context.
  - `database_id`: Is the ID of the database where the object is to be looked up. database_id is int.
- Returns: sysname

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ObjectName

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ObjectName(System.Nullable{System.Int32})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: OBJECT_NAME (Transact-SQL)Returns the database object name for schema-scoped objects. For a list of schema-scoped objects, see sys.objects (Transact-SQL).
- Parameters:
  - `object_id`: Is the ID of the object to be used. object_id is int and is assumed to be a schema-scoped object in the specified database, or in the current database context.
- Returns: sysname

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ObjectProperty

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ObjectProperty(System.Nullable{System.Int32},LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ObjectPropertyName)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: OBJECTPROPERTY (Transact-SQL)Returns information about schema-scoped objects in the current database. For a list of schema-scoped objects, see sys.objects (Transact-SQL). This function cannot be used for objects that are not schema-scoped, such as data definition language (DDL) triggers and event notifications.
- Parameters:
  - `id`: Is an expression that represents the ID of the object in the current database. id is int and is assumed to be a schema-scoped object in the current database context.
  - `property`: Is an expression that represents the information to be returned for the object specified by id. property can be one of the following values.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ObjectPropertyEx

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ObjectPropertyEx(System.Nullable{System.Int32},LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ObjectPropertyExName)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: OBJECTPROPERTYEX (Transact-SQL)Returns information about schema-scoped objects in the current database. For a list of these objects, see sys.objects (Transact-SQL). OBJECTPROPERTYEX cannot be used for objects that are not schema-scoped, such as data definition language (DDL) triggers and event notifications.
- Parameters:
  - `id`: Is an expression that represents the ID of the object in the current database. id is int and is assumed to be a schema-scoped object in the current database context.
  - `property`: Is an expression that contains the information to be returned for the object specified by id. The return type is sql_variant. The following table shows the base data type for each property value.
- Returns: sql_variant

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ObjectSchemaName

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ObjectSchemaName(System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: OBJECT_SCHEMA_NAME (Transact-SQL)Returns the database schema name for schema-scoped objects. For a list of schema-scoped objects, see sys.objects (Transact-SQL).
- Parameters:
  - `object_id`: Is the ID of the object to be used. object_id is int and is assumed to be a schema-scoped object in the specified database, or in the current database context.
  - `database_id`: Is the ID of the database where the object is to be looked up. database_id is int.
- Returns: sysname

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ObjectSchemaName

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ObjectSchemaName(System.Nullable{System.Int32})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: OBJECT_SCHEMA_NAME (Transact-SQL)Returns the database schema name for schema-scoped objects. For a list of schema-scoped objects, see sys.objects (Transact-SQL).
- Parameters:
  - `object_id`: Is the ID of the object to be used. object_id is int and is assumed to be a schema-scoped object in the specified database, or in the current database context.
- Returns: sysname

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.OpenJson

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.OpenJson(LinqToDB.IDataContext,System.String,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: OPENJSON (Transact-SQL)A table-valued function that parses JSON text and returns objects and properties from the JSON input as rows and columns.
- Remarks: Only available on SQL Server 2017 or later, and compatibility mode for the database must be set to 140 or higher
- Parameters:
  - `json`: An expression. Typically the name of a variable or a column that contains JSON text.
  - `path`: A JSON path expression that specifies the property to query.
- Returns: Returns a rowset view over the elements of a JSON object or array.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.OpenJson

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.OpenJson(LinqToDB.IDataContext,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: OPENJSON (Transact-SQL)A table-valued function that parses JSON text and returns objects and properties from the JSON input as rows and columns.
- Remarks: Only available on SQL Server 2016 or later, and compatibility mode for the database must be set to 130 or higher
- Parameters:
  - `json`: An expression. Typically the name of a variable or a column that contains JSON text.
- Returns: Returns a rowset view over the elements of a JSON object or array.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.OpenJson

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.OpenJson(System.String,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: OPENJSON (Transact-SQL)A table-valued function that parses JSON text and returns objects and properties from the JSON input as rows and columns.
- Remarks: Only available on SQL Server 2016 or later, and compatibility mode for the database must be set to 130 or higher
- Parameters:
  - `json`: An expression. Typically the name of a variable or a column that contains JSON text.
  - `path`: A JSON path expression that specifies the property to query.
- Returns: Returns a rowset view over the elements of a JSON object or array.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.OpenJson

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.OpenJson(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: OPENJSON (Transact-SQL)A table-valued function that parses JSON text and returns objects and properties from the JSON input as rows and columns.
- Remarks: Only available on SQL Server 2016 or later, and compatibility mode for the database must be set to 130 or higher
- Parameters:
  - `json`: An expression. Typically the name of a variable or a column that contains JSON text.
- Returns: Returns a rowset view over the elements of a JSON object or array.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.OriginalDbName

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.OriginalDbName`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: ORIGINAL_DB_NAME (Transact-SQL)Returns the database name specified by the user in the database connection string. This database is specified by using the sqlcmd-d option (USE database). It can also be specified with the Open Database Connectivity (ODBC) data source expression (initial catalog = databasename). This database is different from the default user database.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Parse<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Parse``1(System.String,LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlType{``0},System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: PARSE (Transact-SQL)Returns the result of an expression, translated to the requested data type in SQL Server.
- Parameters:
  - `string_value`: nvarchar(4000) value representing the formatted value to parse into the specified data type.
  - `data_type`: Literal value representing the data type requested for the result.
  - `culture`: Optional string that identifies the culture in which string_value is formatted.
- Returns: Returns the result of the expression, translated to the requested data type.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Parse<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Parse``1(System.String,LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlType{``0})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: PARSE (Transact-SQL)Returns the result of an expression, translated to the requested data type in SQL Server.
- Parameters:
  - `string_value`: nvarchar(4000) value representing the formatted value to parse into the specified data type.
  - `data_type`: Literal value representing the data type requested for the result.
- Returns: Returns the result of the expression, translated to the requested data type.

- Kind: Method

- Parameters:

  - `data_type`: Literal value representing the data type requested for the result.
  - `culture`: Optional string that identifies the culture in which string_value is formatted.
- Returns: Returns the result of the expression, translated to the requested data type.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Parse<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Parse``1(System.String,System.Func{LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlType{``0}})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: PARSE (Transact-SQL)Returns the result of an expression, translated to the requested data type in SQL Server.
- Parameters:

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Parse``1(System.String,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: PARSE (Transact-SQL)Returns the result of an expression, translated to the requested data type in SQL Server.
- Parameters:

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Parse<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Parse``1(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: PARSE (Transact-SQL)Returns the result of an expression, translated to the requested data type in SQL Server.
- Parameters:
  - `string_value`: nvarchar(4000) value representing the formatted value to parse into the specified data type.
- Returns: Returns the result of the expression, translated to the requested data type.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ParseName

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ParseName(System.String,System.Int32)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: PARSENAME (Transact-SQL)Returns the database schema name for schema-scoped objects. For a list of schema-scoped objects, see sys.objects (Transact-SQL).
- Parameters:
  - `object_name`: Is the parameter that holds the name of the object for which to retrieve the specified object part. This parameter is an optionally-qualified object name. If all parts of the object name are qualified, this name can have four parts: the server name, the database name, the schema name, and the object name. Each part of the 'object_name' string is type sysname which is equivalent to nvarchar(128) or 256 bytes. If any part of the string exceeds 256 bytes, PARSENAME will return NULL for that part as it is not a valid sysname.
  - `object_piece`: Is the object part to return. object_piece is of type int, and can have these values: 1 = Object name2 = Schema name3 = Database name4 = Server name
- Returns: sysname

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.PatIndex

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.PatIndex(System.String,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: PATINDEX (Transact-SQL)Returns the starting position of the first occurrence of a pattern in a specified expression, or zeros if the pattern is not found, on all valid text and character data types.
- Parameters:
  - `pattern`: Is a character expression that contains the sequence to be found. Wildcard characters can be used; however, the % character must come before and follow pattern (except when you search for first or last characters). pattern is an expression of the character string data type category. pattern is limited to 8000 characters.
  - `expression`: Is an expression, typically a column that is searched for the specified pattern. expression is of the character string data type category.
- Returns: bigint if expression is of the varchar(max) or nvarchar(max) data types; otherwise int.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.PatIndexBig

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.PatIndexBig(System.String,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: PATINDEX (Transact-SQL)Returns the starting position of the first occurrence of a pattern in a specified expression, or zeros if the pattern is not found, on all valid text and character data types.
- Parameters:
  - `pattern`: Is a character expression that contains the sequence to be found. Wildcard characters can be used; however, the % character must come before and follow pattern (except when you search for first or last characters). pattern is an expression of the character string data type category. pattern is limited to 8000 characters.
  - `expression`: Is an expression, typically a column that is searched for the specified pattern. expression is of the character string data type category.
- Returns: bigint if expression is of the varchar(max) or nvarchar(max) data types; otherwise int.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.PI

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.PI`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: PI (Transact-SQL)Returns the constant value of PI.
- Returns: float

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Power<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Power``1(``0,``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: POWER (Transact-SQL)Returns the base-10 logarithm of the specified float expression.
- Parameters:
  - `float_expression`: Is an expression of type float or of a type that can be implicitly converted to float.
  - `y`: Is the power to which to raise float_expression. y can be an expression of the exact numeric or approximate numeric data type category, except for the bit data type.
- Returns: float

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.PublishingServerName

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.PublishingServerName`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: PUBLISHINGSERVERNAME (Transact-SQL)Returns the name of the originating Publisher for a published database participating in a database mirroring session. This function is executed at a Publisher instance of SQL Server on the publication database. Use it to determine the original Publisher of the published database.
- Returns: nvarchar

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.QuoteName

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.QuoteName(System.String,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: QUOTENAME (Transact-SQL)Returns a Unicode string with the delimiters added to make the input string a valid SQL Server delimited identifier.
- Parameters:
  - `character_string`: Is a string of Unicode character data. character_string is sysname and is limited to 128 characters. Inputs greater than 128 characters return NULL.
  - `quote_character`: Is a one-character string to use as the delimiter. Can be a single quotation mark ( ' ), a left or right bracket ( [] ), a double quotation mark ( " ), a left or right parenthesis ( () ), a greater than or less than sign ( >< ), a left or right brace ( {} ) or a backtick ( ` ). NULL returns if an unacceptable character is supplied. If quote_character is not specified, brackets are used.
- Returns: nvarchar(258)

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.QuoteName

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.QuoteName(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: QUOTENAME (Transact-SQL)Returns a Unicode string with the delimiters added to make the input string a valid SQL Server delimited identifier.
- Parameters:
  - `character_string`: Is a string of Unicode character data. character_string is sysname and is limited to 128 characters. Inputs greater than 128 characters return NULL.
- Returns: nvarchar(258)

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Radians<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Radians``1(``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: RADIANS (Transact-SQL)Returns radians when a numeric expression, in degrees, is entered.
- Parameters:
  - `numeric_expression`: An expression of the exact numeric or approximate numeric data type category, except for the bit data type.
- Returns: Return values have the same type as numeric_expression.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Random

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Random`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: RAND (Transact-SQL)Returns a pseudo-random float value from 0 through 1, exclusive.
- Returns: float

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Random

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Random(System.Int32)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: RAND (Transact-SQL)Returns a pseudo-random float value from 0 through 1, exclusive.
- Returns: float

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Replace

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Replace(System.String,System.String,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: REPLACE (Transact-SQL)Replaces all occurrences of a specified string value with another string value.
- Parameters:
  - `string_expression`: Is the string expression to be searched. string_expression can be of a character or binary data type.
  - `string_pattern`: Is the substring to be found. string_pattern can be of a character or binary data type. string_pattern must not exceed the maximum number of bytes that fits on a page. If string_pattern is an empty string (''), string_expression is returned unchanged.
  - `string_replacement`: Is the replacement string. string_replacement can be of a character or binary data type.
- Returns: Returns nvarchar if one of the input arguments is of the nvarchar data type; otherwise, REPLACE returns varchar. Returns NULL if any one of the arguments is NULL.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Replicate

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Replicate(System.Byte[],System.Nullable{System.Int32})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: REPLICATE (Transact-SQL)Repeats a string value a specified number of times.
- Parameters:
  - `string_expression`: Is an expression of a character string or binary data type.
  - `integer_expression`: Is an expression of any integer type, including bigint. If integer_expression is negative, NULL is returned.
- Returns: Returns the same type as string_expression.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Replicate

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Replicate(System.String,System.Nullable{System.Int32})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: REPLICATE (Transact-SQL)Repeats a string value a specified number of times.
- Parameters:
  - `string_expression`: Is an expression of a character string or binary data type.
  - `integer_expression`: Is an expression of any integer type, including bigint. If integer_expression is negative, NULL is returned.
- Returns: Returns the same type as string_expression.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Reverse

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Reverse(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: REVERSE (Transact-SQL)Returns the reverse order of a string value.
- Parameters:
  - `string_expression`: string_expression is an expression of a string or binary data type. string_expression can be a constant, variable, or column of either character or binary data.
- Returns: varchar or nvarchar

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Right

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Right(System.String,System.Nullable{System.Int32})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: RIGHT (Transact-SQL)Returns the right part of a character string with the specified number of characters.
- Parameters:
  - `character_expression`: Is an expression of character or binary data. character_expression can be a constant, variable, or column. character_expression can be of any data type, except text or ntext, that can be implicitly converted to varchar or nvarchar. Otherwise, use the CAST function to explicitly convert character_expression.
  - `integer_expression`: Is a positive integer that specifies how many characters of the character_expression will be returned. If integer_expression is negative, an error is returned. If integer_expression is type bigint and contains a large value, character_expression must be of a large data type such as varchar(max). The integer_expression parameter counts a UTF-16 surrogate character as one character.
- Returns: Returns varchar when character_expression is a non-Unicode character data type. Returns nvarchar when character_expression is a Unicode character data type.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.RightTrim

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.RightTrim(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: RTRIM (Transact-SQL)Returns a character string after truncating all trailing spaces.
- Parameters:
  - `character_expression`: Is an expression of character data. character_expression can be a constant, variable, or column of either character or binary data.
- Returns: varchar or nvarchar

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Round<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Round``1(``0,System.Int32,System.Int32)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: ROUND (Transact-SQL)Returns a numeric value, rounded to the specified length or precision.
- Parameters:
  - `numeric_expression`: An expression of the exact numeric or approximate numeric data type category, except for the bit data type.
  - `length`: Is the precision to which numeric_expression is to be rounded. length must be an expression of type tinyint, smallint, or int. When length is a positive number, numeric_expression is rounded to the number of decimal positions specified by length. When length is a negative number, numeric_expression is rounded on the left side of the decimal point, as specified by length.
  - `function`: Is the type of operation to perform. function must be tinyint, smallint, or int. When function is omitted or has a value of 0 (default), numeric_expression is rounded. When a value other than 0 is specified, numeric_expression is truncated
- Returns: Return values have the same type as numeric_expression.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Round<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Round``1(``0,System.Int32)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: ROUND (Transact-SQL)Returns a numeric value, rounded to the specified length or precision.
- Parameters:
  - `numeric_expression`: An expression of the exact numeric or approximate numeric data type category, except for the bit data type.
  - `length`: Is the precision to which numeric_expression is to be rounded. length must be an expression of type tinyint, smallint, or int. When length is a positive number, numeric_expression is rounded to the number of decimal positions specified by length. When length is a negative number, numeric_expression is rounded on the left side of the decimal point, as specified by length.
- Returns: Return values have the same type as numeric_expression.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.RowCountBig

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.RowCountBig`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: ROWCOUNT_BIG (Transact-SQL)Returns the number of rows affected by the last statement executed. This function operates like @@ROWCOUNT, except the return type of ROWCOUNT_BIG is bigint.
- Returns: bigint

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.SchemaID

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.SchemaID`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: SCHEMA_ID (Transact-SQL)Returns the schema ID associated with a schema name.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.SchemaID

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.SchemaID(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: SCHEMA_ID (Transact-SQL)Returns the schema ID associated with a schema name.
- Parameters:
  - `schema_name`: Is the name of the schema. schema_name is a sysname. If schema_name is not specified, SCHEMA_ID will return the ID of the default schema of the caller.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.SchemaName

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.SchemaName`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: SCHEMA_NAME (Transact-SQL)Returns the schema name associated with a schema ID.
- Returns: sysname

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.SchemaName

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.SchemaName(System.Nullable{System.Int32})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: SCHEMA_NAME (Transact-SQL)Returns the schema name associated with a schema ID.
- Parameters:
  - `schema_id`: The ID of the schema. schema_id is an int. If schema_id is not defined, SCHEMA_NAME will return the name of the default schema of the caller.
- Returns: sysname

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ScopeIdentity

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ScopeIdentity`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: SCOPE_IDENTITY (Transact-SQL)Returns the last identity value inserted into an identity column in the same scope. A scope is a module: a stored procedure, trigger, function, or batch. Therefore, if two statements are in the same stored procedure, function, or batch, they are in the same scope.
- Returns: numeric(38,0)

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ServerProperty

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ServerProperty(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ServerPropertyName)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: SERVERPROPERTY (Transact-SQL)Returns property information about the server instance.
- Parameters:
  - `property`: Is an expression that contains the property information to be returned for the server.
- Returns: sql_variant

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Sign<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Sign``1(``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: SIGN (Transact-SQL)Returns the positive (+1), zero (0), or negative (-1) sign of the specified expression.
- Parameters:
  - `numeric_expression`: An expression of the exact numeric or approximate numeric data type category, except for the bit data type.
- Returns: Return values have the same type as numeric_expression.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Sin<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Sin``1(``0,``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: SIN (Transact-SQL)Returns the trigonometric sine of the specified angle, in radians, and in an approximate numeric, float, expression.
- Parameters:
  - `float_expression`: Is an expression of type float or of a type that can be implicitly converted to float, in radians.
- Returns: float

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.SmallDateTimeFromParts

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.SmallDateTimeFromParts(System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: SMALLDATETIMEFROMPARTS (Transact-SQL)Returns a smalldatetime value for the specified date and time.
- Returns: datetime

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.SoundEx

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.SoundEx(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: SOUNDEX (Transact-SQL)Returns a four-character (SOUNDEX) code to evaluate the similarity of two strings.
- Parameters:
  - `character_expression`: Is an alphanumeric expression of character data. character_expression can be a constant, variable, or column.
- Returns: varchar

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Space

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Space(System.Nullable{System.Int32})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: SPACE (Transact-SQL)Returns a string of repeated spaces.
- Parameters:
  - `integer_expression`: Is a positive integer that indicates the number of spaces. If integer_expression is negative, a null string is returned.
- Returns: varchar

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Sqrt<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Sqrt``1(``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: SQRT (Transact-SQL)Returns the square root of the specified float value.
- Parameters:
  - `float_expression`: Is an expression of type float or of a type that can be implicitly converted to float.
- Returns: float

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Square<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Square``1(``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: SQUARE (Transact-SQL)Returns the square of the specified float value.
- Parameters:
  - `float_expression`: Is an expression of type float or of a type that can be implicitly converted to float.
- Returns: float

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.StatsDate

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.StatsDate(System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: STATS_DATE (Transact-SQL)Returns the date of the most recent update for statistics on a table or indexed view.
- Parameters:
  - `object_id`: ID of the table or indexed view with the statistics.
  - `stats_id`: ID of the statistics object.
- Returns: Returns datetime on success. Returns NULL if a statistics blob was not created.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Str<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Str``1(``0,System.Int32,System.Int32)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: STR (Transact-SQL)Returns character data converted from numeric data. The character data is right-justified, with a specified length and decimal precision.
- Parameters:
  - `float_expression`: Is an expression of approximate numeric (float) data type with a decimal point.
  - `length`: Is the total length. This includes decimal point, sign, digits, and spaces. The default is 10.
  - `decimal`: Is the number of places to the right of the decimal point. decimal must be less than or equal to 16. If decimal is more than 16 then the result is truncated to sixteen places to the right of the decimal point.
- Returns: varchar

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Str<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Str``1(``0,System.Int32)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: STR (Transact-SQL)Returns character data converted from numeric data. The character data is right-justified, with a specified length and decimal precision.
- Parameters:
  - `float_expression`: Is an expression of approximate numeric (float) data type with a decimal point.
  - `length`: Is the total length. This includes decimal point, sign, digits, and spaces. The default is 10.
- Returns: varchar

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Str<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Str``1(``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: STR (Transact-SQL)Returns character data converted from numeric data. The character data is right-justified, with a specified length and decimal precision.
- Parameters:

- Returns: varchar

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.StringEscape

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.StringEscape(System.String,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: STRING_ESCAPE (Transact-SQL)Returns character data converted from numeric data. The character data is right-justified, with a specified length and decimal precision.
- Parameters:
  - `text`: Is a nvarchar expression expression representing the object that should be escaped.
  - `type`: Escaping rules that will be applied. Currently the value supported is 'json'.
- Returns: varchar

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Stuff

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Stuff(System.String,System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: STUFF (Transact-SQL)The STUFF function inserts a string into another string. It deletes a specified length of characters in the first string at the start position and then inserts the second string into the first string at the start position.
- Parameters:
  - `character_expression`: Is an expression of character data. character_expression can be a constant, variable, or column of either character or binary data.
  - `start`: Is an integer value that specifies the location to start deletion and insertion. If start is negative or zero, a null string is returned. If start is longer than the first character_expression, a null string is returned. start can be of type bigint.
  - `length`: Is an integer that specifies the number of characters to delete. If length is negative, a null string is returned. If length is longer than the first character_expression, deletion occurs up to the last character in the last character_expression. If length is zero, insertion occurs at start location and no characters are deleted. length can be of type bigint.
  - `replaceWith_expression`: Is an expression of character data. character_expression can be a constant, variable, or column of either character or binary data. This expression replaces length characters of character_expression beginning at start. Providing NULL as the replaceWith_expression, removes characters without inserting anything.
- Returns: Returns character data if character_expression is one of the supported character data types. Returns binary data if character_expression is one of the supported binary data types.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Substring

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Substring(System.String,System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: SUBSTRING (Transact-SQL)Returns part of a character, binary, text, or image expression in SQL Server.
- Parameters:
  - `expression`: Is a character, binary, text, ntext, or image expression.
  - `start`: Is an integer or bigint expression that specifies where the returned characters start. (The numbering is 1 based, meaning that the first character in the expression is 1). If start is less than 1, the returned expression will begin at the first character that is specified in expression. In this case, the number of characters that are returned is the largest value of either the sum of start + length - 1 or 0. If start is greater than the number of characters in the value expression, a zero-length expression is returned.
  - `length`: Is a positive integer or bigint expression that specifies how many characters of the expression will be returned. If length is negative, an error is generated and the statement is terminated. If the sum of start and length is greater than the number of characters in expression, the whole value expression beginning at start is returned.
- Returns: Returns character data if expression is one of the supported character data types. Returns binary data if expression is one of the supported binary data types.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.SwitchOffset

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.SwitchOffset(System.Nullable{System.DateTimeOffset},System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: SWITCHOFFSET (Transact-SQL)Returns a datetimeoffset value that is changed from the stored time zone offset to a specified new time zone offset.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.SysDatetime

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.SysDatetime`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: SYSDATETIME (Transact-SQL)Returns a datetime2(7) value that contains the date and time of the computer on which the instance of SQL Server is running.
- Returns: datetime2(7)

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.SysDatetimeOffset

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.SysDatetimeOffset`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: SYSDATETIMEOFFSET (Transact-SQL)Returns a datetimeoffset(7) value that contains the date and time of the computer on which the instance of SQL Server is running. The time zone offset is included.
- Returns: datetimeoffset(7)

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.SysUtcDatetime

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.SysUtcDatetime`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: SYSUTCDATETIME (Transact-SQL)Returns a datetime2 value that contains the date and time of the computer on which the instance of SQL Server is running. The date and time is returned as UTC time (Coordinated Universal Time). The fractional second precision specification has a range from 1 to 7 digits. The default precision is 7 digits.
- Returns: datetime2

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Tan``1(``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: TAN (Transact-SQL)Returns the tangent of the input expression.
- Parameters:
  - `float_expression`: Is an expression of type float or of a type that can be implicitly converted to float.
- Returns: float

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.TimeFromParts

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.TimeFromParts(System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: TIMEFROMPARTS (Transact-SQL)This function returns a date value that maps to the specified year, month, and day values.
- Returns: date

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.TimeFromParts

- Kind: Method
- Summary: TODATETIMEOFFSET (Transact-SQL)Returns a datetimeoffset value that is translated from a datetime2 expression.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Translate

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Translate(System.String,System.String,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: TRANSLATE (Transact-SQL)Returns part of a character, binary, text, or image expression in SQL Server.
- Parameters:
  - `inputString`: inputString Is the string expression to be searched. inputString can be any character data type (nvarchar, varchar, nchar, char).
  - `characters `: Is a string expression containing characters that should be replaced. characters can be any character data type.
  - `translations`: Is a string expression containing the replacement characters. translations must be the same data type and length as characters.
- Returns: Returns a character expression of the same data type as inputString where characters from the second argument are replaced with the matching characters from third argument.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Trim

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Trim(System.String,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: TRIM (Transact-SQL)Removes the space character char(32) or other specified characters from the start and end of a string.
- Parameters:
  - `characters`: Is a literal, variable, or function call of any non-LOB character type (nvarchar, varchar, nchar, or char) containing characters that should be removed. nvarchar(max) and varchar(max) types aren't allowed.
  - `string`: Is an expression of any character type (nvarchar, varchar, nchar, or char) where characters should be removed.
- Returns: Returns a character expression with a type of string argument where the space character char(32) or other specified characters are removed from both sides. Returns NULL if input string is NULL.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Trim

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Trim(System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: TRIM (Transact-SQL)Removes the space character char(32) or other specified characters from the start and end of a string.
- Parameters:
  - `string`: Is an expression of any character type (nvarchar, varchar, nchar, or char) where characters should be removed.
- Returns: Returns a character expression with a type of string argument where the space character char(32) or other specified characters are removed from both sides. Returns NULL if input string is NULL.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.TryCast<T>

- Parameters:
  - `expression`: Any valid expression.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.VectorDistance

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.VectorDistance(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.DistanceMetric,System.Single[],System.Single[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: The VECTOR_DISTANCE function calculates the distance between two vectors using a specified distance metric.
- Parameters:
  - `distanceMetric`: A string with the name of the distance metric to use to calculate the distance between the two given vectors. The following distance metrics are supported: cosineCosine distanceeuclideanEuclidean distancedot(Negative) Dot product
  - `vector1`: An expression that evaluates to vector data type.
  - `vector2`: An expression that evaluates to vector data type.
- Returns: The function returns a scalar float value that represents the distance between the two vectors using the specified distance metric.

- Kind: Method

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.VectorDistance<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.VectorDistance``1(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.DistanceMetric,``0,``0)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: The VECTOR_DISTANCE function calculates the distance between two vectors using a specified distance metric.
- Parameters:
  - `distanceMetric`: A string with the name of the distance metric to use to calculate the distance between the two given vectors. The following distance metrics are supported: cosineCosine distanceeuclideanEuclidean distancedot(Negative) Dot product
  - `vector1`: An expression that evaluates to vector data type.
  - `vector2`: An expression that evaluates to vector data type.
- Returns: The function returns a scalar float value that represents the distance between the two vectors using the specified distance metric.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.XactState

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.XactState`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: XACT_STATE (Transact-SQL)Is a scalar function that reports the user transaction state of a current running request. XACT_STATE indicates whether the request has an active user transaction, and whether the transaction is capable of being committed.
- Returns: smallint

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Year

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Year(System.Nullable{System.DateTime})`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: YEAR (Transact-SQL)This function returns an integer that represents the day (day of the month) of the specified date.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerExtensions.Contains

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerExtensions.Contains(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.ISqlServerExtensions,System.String,System.Object[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Applies full-text search condition using CONTAINS predicate against specified full-text columns or all FTS columns in table. Example: "CONTAINS((col1, col2), N'search')".
- Parameters:
  - `ext`: Extension point.
  - `search`: Full-text search condition.
  - `tableOrColumns`: Full-text columns that should be queried or table, if all FST columns should be queried.
- Returns: Returns if full-text search found matching records.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerExtensions.ContainsProperty

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerExtensions.ContainsProperty(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.ISqlServerExtensions,System.Object,System.String,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Applies full-text search condition using CONTAINS(PROPERTY(...)) predicate against specified full-text column property. Example: "CONTAINS(PROPERTY(column, 'property'), N'search')".
- Parameters:
  - `ext`: Extension point.
  - `column`: Full-text column that should be queried.
  - `property`: Name of document property to search in.
  - `search`: Full-text search condition.
- Returns: Returns if full-text search found matching records.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerExtensions.ContainsPropertyWithLanguage

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerExtensions.ContainsPropertyWithLanguage(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.ISqlServerExtensions,System.Object,System.String,System.String,System.Int32)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Applies full-text search condition using CONTAINS(PROPERTY(...)) predicate against specified full-text column property. Example: "CONTAINS(PROPERTY(column, 'property'), N'search', LANGUAGE language_code)".
- Parameters:
  - `ext`: Extension point.
  - `column`: Full-text column that should be queried.
  - `property`: Name of document property to search in.
  - `search`: Full-text search condition.
  - `language`: Language LCID code (see syslanguages.lcid).
- Returns: Returns if full-text search found matching records.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerExtensions.ContainsPropertyWithLanguage

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerExtensions.ContainsPropertyWithLanguage(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.ISqlServerExtensions,System.Object,System.String,System.String,System.String)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Applies full-text search condition using CONTAINS(PROPERTY(...)) predicate against specified full-text column property. Example: "CONTAINS(PROPERTY(column, 'property'), N'search', LANGUAGE N'language')".
- Parameters:
  - `ext`: Extension point.
  - `column`: Full-text column that should be queried.
  - `property`: Name of document property to search in.
  - `search`: Full-text search condition.
  - `language`: Language name (see syslanguages.alias).
- Returns: Returns if full-text search found matching records.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerExtensions.ContainsTable<T1,T2>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerExtensions.ContainsTable``2(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.ISqlServerExtensions,LinqToDB.ITable{``0},System.Linq.Expressions.Expression{System.Func{``0,System.Object}},System.String,System.Int32)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Performs full-text search query using CONTAINSTABLE function against specified full-text columns. Example: "CONTAINSTABLE(table, (col1, col2), N'search', top)".
- Type parameters:
  - `TTable`: Queried table mapping class.
  - `TKey`: Full-text index key type.
- Parameters:
  - `ext`: Extension point.
  - `table`: Table to perform full-text search query against.
  - `columns`: Selector expression for full-text columns that should be queried.
  - `search`: Full-text search condition.
  - `top`: Top filter to return top N ranked results.
- Returns: Returns full-text search ranking table.

  - `table`: Table to perform full-text search query against.
  - `columns`: Selector expression for full-text columns that should be queried.
  - `term`: Full-text search term.
  - `language`: Language LCID code (see syslanguages.lcid).
  - `top`: Top filter to return top N ranked results.
- Returns: Returns full-text search ranking table.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerExtensions.FreeTextWithLanguage

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerExtensions.FreeTextWithLanguage(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.ISqlServerExtensions,System.String,System.Int32,System.Object[])`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Applies full-text search condition using FREETEXT predicate against specified full-text columns or all FTS columns in table. Example: "FREETEXT((col1, col2), N'search', LANGUAGE language_code)".
- Parameters:
  - `ext`: Extension point.
  - `term`: Full-text search term.
  - `language`: Language LCID code (see syslanguages.lcid).
  - `tableOrColumns`: Full-text columns that should be queried or table, if all FST columns should be queried.
- Returns: Returns if full-text search found matching records.

- Returns: Returns if full-text search found matching records.
