## LinqToDB.Tools.DataExtensions.RetrieveIdentity<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.Tools.DataExtensions.RetrieveIdentity``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},LinqToDB.IDataContext,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Initializes source columns, marked with or with identity values: if column had sequence name set using and set to , values from sequence used. Implemented for: Oracle, PostgreSQLif table has identity configured and set to , values from sequence used. Implemented for: SQL Server 2005+Otherwise column initialized with values, incremented by 1 starting with max value from database for this column plus 1.
- Type parameters:
  - `T`: Entity type.
- Parameters:
  - `source`: Ordered list of entities to initialize.
  - `context`: Data connection to use to retrieve sequence values of max used value for column.
  - `useSequenceName`: Enables identity values retrieval from sequence for columns with sequence name specified in mapping using . Implemented for Oracle and PostgreSQL.
  - `useIdentity`: Enables identity values retrieval from table with identity column. Implemented for SQL Server 2005+.
- Returns: Returns new collection of identity fields initialized or if entity had no identity columns.

## LinqToDB.Tools.DataExtensions.RetrieveIdentityAsync<T>

- XML member: `M:LinqToDB.Tools.DataExtensions.RetrieveIdentityAsync``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},LinqToDB.IDataContext,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Threading.CancellationToken)`
- Kind: Method
- Summary: Initializes source columns, marked with or with identity values: if column had sequence name set using and set to , values from sequence used. Implemented for: Oracle, PostgreSQLif table has identity configured and set to , values from sequence used. Implemented for: SQL Server 2005+Otherwise column initialized with values, incremented by 1 starting with max value from database for this column plus 1.
- Type parameters:
  - `T`: Entity type.
- Parameters:
  - `source`: Ordered list of entities to initialize.
  - `context`: Data connection to use to retrieve sequence values of max used value for column.
  - `useSequenceName`: Enables identity values retrieval from sequence for columns with sequence name specified in mapping using . Implemented for Oracle and PostgreSQL.
  - `useIdentity`: Enables identity values retrieval from table with identity column. Implemented for SQL Server 2005+.
  - `cancellationToken`: Asynchronous operation cancellation token.
- Returns: Returns new collection of identity fields initialized or if entity had no identity columns.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.BulkCopy.Options

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.BulkCopy.Options`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Default options.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Connection.Options

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Connection.Options`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Default options.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.DataContext.Options

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.DataContext.Options`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Default options.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.CacheSlidingExpiration

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.CacheSlidingExpiration`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Specifies timeout when query will be evicted from cache since last execution of query. Default value is 1 hour.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.CompareNulls

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.CompareNulls`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: If set to nullable fields would be checked for IS NULL in Equal/NotEqual comparisons. If set to comparisons are compiled straight to equivalent SQL operators, which consider nulls values as not equal. is a backward compatible option that works mostly as , but sniffs parameters value and changes = into IS NULL when parameters are null. Comparisons to literal null are always compiled into IS NULL. This affects: Equal, NotEqual, Not Contains Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.ConcatenateOrderBy

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.ConcatenateOrderBy`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Controls behavior, when LINQ query chain contains multiple or calls: if - non-first OrderBy* call will be treated as ThenBy* call;if - OrderBy* call will place sort specifications, added by previous OrderBy* and ThenBy* calls, after current call. Default value: . Example: query.OrderBy(r => r.FirstName).OrderBy(r => r.LastName) // =true - non-first OrderBy* calls treated as ordering continuation (non-standard) ORDER BY p.FIRST_NAME, p.LAST_NAME // =false - last OrderBy* calls have higher priority like they should per API documentation ORDER BY p.LAST_NAME, p.FIRST_NAME // to completely discard old sort specification, use API: query.OrderBy(r => r.FirstName).RemoveOrderBy().OrderBy(r => r.LastName) // for any value will produce ORDER BY p.LAST_NAME

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.DisableQueryCache

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.DisableQueryCache`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Used to disable LINQ expressions caching for queries. This cache reduces time, required for query parsing but have several side-effects: - cached LINQ expressions could contain references to external objects as parameters, which could lead to memory leaks if those objects are not used anymore by other code - cache access synchronization could lead to bigger latencies than it saves. Default value: . It is not recommended to enable this option as it could lead to severe slowdown. Better approach will be to use scope around queries, that produce severe memory leaks you need to fix. More details.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.DoNotClearOrderBys

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.DoNotClearOrderBys`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Controls behavior, when LINQ query chain contains multiple or calls: - if - non-first OrderBy* call will be treated as ThenBy* call; - if - OrderBy* call will discard sort specifications, added by previous OrderBy* and ThenBy* calls. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.EnableContextSchemaEdit

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.EnableContextSchemaEdit`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: If , user could add new mappings to context mapping schems (). Otherwise will be generated on locked mapping schema edit attempt. It is not recommended to enable this option as it has performance implications. Proper approach is to create single instance once, configure mappings for it and use this instance for all context instances. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.GenerateExpressionTest

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.GenerateExpressionTest`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Enables generation of test class for each LINQ query, executed while this option is enabled. This option could be useful for issue reporting, when you need to provide reproducible case. Test file will be placed to linq2db subfolder of temp folder and exact file path will be logged to data connection tracing infrastructure. See for more details. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.GuardGrouping

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.GuardGrouping`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Controls behavior of LINQ query, which ends with GroupBy call. - if - will be thrown for such queries; - if - eager loading used. Default value: .
- Remarks: More details.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.IgnoreEmptyUpdate

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.IgnoreEmptyUpdate`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Controls behavior of linq2db when there is no updateable fields in Update query: - if - query not executed and Update operation returns 0 as number of affected records; - if - will be thrown. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.KeepDistinctOrdered

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.KeepDistinctOrdered`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Allows SQL generation to automatically transform SELECT DISTINCT value FROM Table ORDER BY date Into GROUP BY equivalent if syntax is not supported Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.OptimizeJoins

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.OptimizeJoins`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: If enabled, linq2db will try to reduce number of generated SQL JOINs for LINQ query. Attempted optimizations: - removes duplicate joins by unique target table key; - removes self-joins by unique key; - removes left joins if joined table is not used in query. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.Options

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.Options`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Default options. Automatically synchronized with other settings in class.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.ParameterizeTakeSkip

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.ParameterizeTakeSkip`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Enables Take/Skip parameterization. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.PreferApply

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.PreferApply`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Used to generate CROSS APPLY or OUTER APPLY if possible. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.PreloadGroups

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.PreloadGroups`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Controls how group data for LINQ queries ended with GroupBy will be loaded: - if - group data will be loaded together with main query, resulting in 1 + N queries, where N - number of groups; - if - group data will be loaded when you call enumerator for specific group . Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.TraceMapperExpression

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.TraceMapperExpression`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Enables logging of generated mapping expression to data connection tracing infrastructure. See for more details. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.MaxArrayParameterLengthLogging

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.MaxArrayParameterLengthLogging`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Determines number of items after which logging of collection data in SQL will be truncated. This is to avoid Out-Of-Memory exceptions when getting SqlText from .
- Remarks: This value defaults to 8 elements. Use a value of -1 to disable and always log full collection. Set to 0 to truncate all data.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.MaxBinaryParameterLengthLogging

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.MaxBinaryParameterLengthLogging`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Determines the length after which logging of binary data in SQL will be truncated. This is to avoid Out-Of-Memory exceptions when getting SqlText from .
- Remarks: This value defaults to 100. Use a value of -1 to disable and always log full binary. Set to 0 to truncate all binary data.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.MaxStringParameterLengthLogging

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.MaxStringParameterLengthLogging`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Determines the length after which logging of string data in SQL will be truncated. This is to avoid Out-Of-Memory exceptions when getting SqlText from .
- Remarks: This value defaults to 200. Use a value of -1 to disable and always log full string. Set to 0 to truncate all string data.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.QueryTrace.Options

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.QueryTrace.Options`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Default options.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.DefaultCoefficient

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.DefaultCoefficient`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: The default coefficient for the exponential function used to compute the delay between retries, must be nonnegative. Default value: 1 second.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.DefaultExponentialBase

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.DefaultExponentialBase`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: The default base for the exponential function used to compute the delay between retries, must not be lesser than 1. Default value: 2.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.DefaultMaxDelay

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.DefaultMaxDelay`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: The default maximum time delay between retries, must be nonnegative. Default value: 30 seconds.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.DefaultMaxRetryCount

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.DefaultMaxRetryCount`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: The default number of retry attempts. Default value: 5.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.DefaultRandomFactor

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.DefaultRandomFactor`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: The default maximum random factor, must not be lesser than 1. Default value: 1.1.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.Factory

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.Factory`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Retry policy factory method, used to create retry policy for new instance. If factory method is not set, retry policy is not used. Not set by default.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.Options

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.Options`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Default options. Automatically synchronized with other settings in class.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.UseDefaultPolicy

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.UseDefaultPolicy`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Status of use of default retry policy. Getter returns if default retry policy used, and false if custom retry policy used or retry policy is not set. Setter sets to default retry policy factory if is , otherwise removes retry policy.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Sql.AssociationAlias

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Sql.AssociationAlias`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Format for association alias. Default value: "a_{0}". In the following query var query = from child in db.Child select new { child.ChildID, child.Parent.Value1 }; for association Parent will be generated association A_Parent in resulting SQL. SELECT [child].[ChildID], [a_Parent].[Value1] FROM [Child] [child] LEFT JOIN [Parent] [a_Parent] ON ([child].[ParentID] = [a_Parent].[ParentID]) Set this value to to disable special alias generation queries.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Sql.DefaultNullsPosition

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Sql.DefaultNullsPosition`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Default position of NULL values in an ORDER BY clause for ordering keys that do not specify a explicitly. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Sql.EnableConstantExpressionInOrderBy

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Sql.EnableConstantExpressionInOrderBy`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: If , linq2db will allow any constant expressions in ORDER BY clause. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Sql.GenerateFinalAliases

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Sql.GenerateFinalAliases`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates whether SQL Builder should generate aliases for final projection. It is not required for correct query processing but simplifies SQL analysis. Default value: . For the query var query = from child in db.Child select new { TrackId = child.ChildID, }; When property is SELECT [child].[ChildID] as [TrackId] FROM [Child] [child] Otherwise alias will be removed SELECT [child].[ChildID] FROM [Child] [child]

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Sql.Options

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Sql.Options`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Default options. Automatically synchronized with other settings in class.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.TraceMaterializationActivity

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.TraceMaterializationActivity`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Enables tracing of object materialization activity. It can significantly break performance if tracing consumer performs slow, so it is disabled by default.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.TranslationThreadMaxHopCount

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.TranslationThreadMaxHopCount`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Maximum number of hops to other threads during translation. When all threads used up, application will throw . To disable this feature and produce when stack is used up, set -1 as value. Default value: 5.

## LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.UseNullableTypesMetadata

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.UseNullableTypesMetadata`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Whether or not Nullable Reference Types annotations from C# are read and taken into consideration to determine if a column or association can be null. Nullable Types can be overriden with explicit CanBeNull annotations in [Column], [Association], or [Nullable].
- Remarks: Defaults to false.

## LinqToDB.Common.Convert<T1,T2>.Expression

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Convert`2.Expression`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets an expression that converts a value of TFrom type to TTo type. Setter updates both expression and delegate forms of converter. Assigning value will reset converter to default conversion logic. Assigning non-null value will also set converter as default converter.

## LinqToDB.Common.Convert<T1,T2>.From

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Convert`2.From`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets conversion function delegate.

## LinqToDB.Common.Convert<T1,T2>.Lambda

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Convert`2.Lambda`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets a function that converts a value of TFrom type to TTo type. Setter updates both expression and delegate forms of converter. Assigning value will reset converter to default conversion logic. Assigning non-null value will also set converter as default converter.

## LinqToDB.Common.LinqToDBConvertException.ColumnName

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.LinqToDBConvertException.ColumnName`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets name of misconfigured column, which caused exception.

## LinqToDB.Common.Option<T>.HasValue

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Option`1.HasValue`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Returns if current option stores some value instead of .

## LinqToDB.Common.Option<T>.Value

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Common.Option`1.Value`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets value, stored in option.

## LinqToDB.Configuration.IConnectionStringSettings.ConnectionString

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Configuration.IConnectionStringSettings.ConnectionString`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets connection string.

## LinqToDB.Configuration.IConnectionStringSettings.IsGlobal

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Configuration.IConnectionStringSettings.IsGlobal`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Is this connection configuration defined on global level (machine.config) or on application level.

## LinqToDB.Configuration.IConnectionStringSettings.Name

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Configuration.IConnectionStringSettings.Name`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets connection configuration name.

## LinqToDB.Configuration.IConnectionStringSettings.ProviderName

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Configuration.IConnectionStringSettings.ProviderName`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets data provider configuration name.

## LinqToDB.Configuration.IDataProviderSettings.Attributes

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Configuration.IDataProviderSettings.Attributes`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Extra provider-specific parameters. Sybase: assemblyName - Sybase provider assembly name. SAP HANA: assemblyName - SAP HANA provider assembly name. Oracle: assemblyName - Oracle provider assembly name. SQL Server: assemblyName - provider assembly name. Recognized values: "System.Data", "System.Data.SqlClient", "Microsoft.Data.SqlClient". version - T-SQL support level, recognized values: "2005", "2008", "2012", "2014", "2016", "2017", "2018". Default: "2008". DB2: version - DB2 platform, recognized values: "zOS" or "z/OS" - DB2 for z/OS. Default platform - DB2 LUW.

## LinqToDB.Configuration.IDataProviderSettings.Default

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Configuration.IDataProviderSettings.Default`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets a value indicating whether the provider is default.

## LinqToDB.Configuration.IDataProviderSettings.Name

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Configuration.IDataProviderSettings.Name`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets a name of this data provider configuration.

## LinqToDB.Configuration.IDataProviderSettings.TypeName

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Configuration.IDataProviderSettings.TypeName`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets an assembly qualified type name of this data provider.

## LinqToDB.Configuration.ILinqToDBSettings.ConnectionStrings

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Configuration.ILinqToDBSettings.ConnectionStrings`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets list of connection configurations.

## LinqToDB.Configuration.ILinqToDBSettings.DataProviders

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Configuration.ILinqToDBSettings.DataProviders`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets list of data provider settings.

## LinqToDB.Configuration.ILinqToDBSettings.DefaultConfiguration

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Configuration.ILinqToDBSettings.DefaultConfiguration`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets name of default connection configuration.

## LinqToDB.Configuration.ILinqToDBSettings.DefaultDataProvider

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Configuration.ILinqToDBSettings.DefaultDataProvider`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets name of default data provider configuration.

## LinqToDB.Configuration.NamedValue.Name

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Configuration.NamedValue.Name`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets name for value.

## LinqToDB.Configuration.NamedValue.Value

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Configuration.NamedValue.Value`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets ot sets value.

## LinqToDB.CreateTableOptions.DatabaseName

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.CreateTableOptions.DatabaseName`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Optional name of table's database. If not specified, value from mapping will be used.

## LinqToDB.CreateTableOptions.DefaultNullable

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.CreateTableOptions.DefaultNullable`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Defines how columns nullability flag should be generated: - - generate only NOT NULL for non-nullable fields. Missing nullability information treated as NULL by database. - - generate only NULL for nullable fields. Missing nullability information treated as NOT NULL by database. - - explicitly generate NULL and NOT NULL for all columns. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.CreateTableOptions.SchemaName

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.CreateTableOptions.SchemaName`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Optional name of table schema/owner. If not specified, value from mapping will be used.

## LinqToDB.CreateTableOptions.ServerName

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.CreateTableOptions.ServerName`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Optional name of linked server. If not specified, value from mapping will be used.

## LinqToDB.CreateTableOptions.StatementFooter

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.CreateTableOptions.StatementFooter`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: SQL appended to the generated create table statement.

## LinqToDB.CreateTableOptions.StatementHeader

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.CreateTableOptions.StatementHeader`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Replacement for "CREATE TABLE table_name" header. Header is a template with {0} parameter for table name.

## LinqToDB.CreateTableOptions.TableName

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.CreateTableOptions.TableName`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Optional name of table. If not specified, value from mapping will be used.

## LinqToDB.CreateTableOptions.TableOptions

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.CreateTableOptions.TableOptions`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Optional Table options. Default is .

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.BulkCopyTimeout

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.BulkCopyTimeout`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Number of seconds for the operation to complete before it times out. For native bulk copy this option works only for providers that support it: DB2InformixMySql/MariaDB using MySqlConnector providerOraclePostgreSQLSAP HANASQL ServerSAP/Sybase ASE

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.BulkCopyType

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.BulkCopyType`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Default bulk copy mode, used by methods, if mode is not specified explicitly.

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.CheckConstraints

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.CheckConstraints`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Enables database constrains enforcement during bulk copy operation. Supported with bulk copy mode for following databases: OracleSQL ServerSAP/Sybase ASE

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.ConflictAction

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.ConflictAction`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Specifies the action to take when conflicts occur during bulk copy operation. See for more details on supported databases and compatibility.

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.DatabaseName

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.DatabaseName`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets explicit name of target database instead of one, configured for copied entity in mapping schema. See method for support information per provider.

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.Default

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.Default`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets default instance.

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.FireTriggers

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.FireTriggers`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Enables insert triggers during bulk copy operation. Supported with bulk copy mode for following databases: OracleSQL ServerSAP/Sybase ASE

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.KeepIdentity

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.KeepIdentity`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: If this option set to true, bulk copy will use values of columns, marked with IsIdentity flag. SkipOnInsert flag in this case will be ignored. Otherwise those columns will be skipped and values will be generated by server. Not compatible with mode.

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.KeepNulls

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.KeepNulls`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Enables instert of NULL values instead of values from colum default constraint during bulk copy operation. Supported with bulk copy mode for following databases: SQL ServerSAP/Sybase ASE

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.LinqToDB#Internal#Options#IOptionSet#Default

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.LinqToDB#Internal#Options#IOptionSet#Default`
- Kind: Property

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.MaxBatchSize

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.MaxBatchSize`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Number of rows in each batch. At the end of each batch, the rows in the batch are sent to the server. Returns an integer value or zero if no value has been set.

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.MaxDegreeOfParallelism

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.MaxDegreeOfParallelism`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Implemented only by ClickHouse.Driver provider. Defines number of connections, used for parallel insert in mode.

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.MaxParametersForBatch

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.MaxParametersForBatch`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: If set, will set the maximum parameters per batch statement. Also see .

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.NotifyAfter

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.NotifyAfter`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets counter after how many copied records should be called. E.g. if you set it to 10, callback will be called after each 10 copied records. To disable callback, set this option to 0 (default value).

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.RowsCopiedCallback

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.RowsCopiedCallback`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets callback method that will be called by BulkCopy operation after each rows copied. This callback will not be used if set to 0.

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.SchemaName

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.SchemaName`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets explicit name of target schema/owner instead of one, configured for copied entity in mapping schema. See method for support information per provider.

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.ServerName

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.ServerName`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets explicit name of target server instead of one, configured for copied entity in mapping schema. See method for support information per provider. Also note that it is not supported by provider-specific insert method.

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.TableLock

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.TableLock`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Applies table lock during bulk copy operation. Supported with bulk copy mode for following databases: DB2Informix (using DB2 provider)SQL ServerSAP/Sybase ASE

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.TableName

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.TableName`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets explicit name of target table instead of one, configured for copied entity in mapping schema.

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.TableOptions

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.TableOptions`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets flags overrides instead of configured for copied entity in mapping schema. See method for support information per provider.

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.UseInternalTransaction

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.UseInternalTransaction`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Enables automatic transaction creation during bulk copy operation. Supported with bulk copy mode for following databases: OracleSQL ServerSAP/Sybase ASE

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.UseParameters

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.UseParameters`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets whether to always use Parameters for MultipleRowsCopy. Default is false. If True, provider-specific parameter limit per batch will be used to determine the maximum number of rows per insert, Unless overridden by .

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.WithoutSession

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.WithoutSession`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Implemented only by ClickHouse.Driver provider. When set, provider-specific bulk copy will use session-less connection even if called over connection with session. Note that session-less connections cannot be used with session-bound functionality like temporary tables.

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyRowsCopied.Abort

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyRowsCopied.Abort`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the bulk copy operation should be aborted.

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyRowsCopied.RowsCopied

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyRowsCopied.RowsCopied`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets a value that returns the number of rows copied during the current bulk copy operation.

## LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyRowsCopied.StartTime

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyRowsCopied.StartTime`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets operation execution start time.

## LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.ConfigurationString

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.ConfigurationString`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets configuration string name to use with instance.

## LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.ConnectionFactory

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.ConnectionFactory`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets connection factory to use with instance. Accepts current context settings.

## LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.ConnectionInterceptor

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.ConnectionInterceptor`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Connection interceptor to support connection configuration before or right after connection opened.

## LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.ConnectionString

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.ConnectionString`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: The connection string, or if a was used instead of a connection string.

## LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.DataProvider

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.DataProvider`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets optional implementation to use with connection.

## LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.DataProviderFactory

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.DataProviderFactory`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets factory to use with instance.

## LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.DbConnection

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.DbConnection`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets optional instance to use with instance.

## LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.DbTransaction

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.DbTransaction`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets optional instance to use with instance.

## LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.Default

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.Default`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets default instance.

## LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.DisposeConnection

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.DisposeConnection`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets ownership status for instance. If , will dispose provided/created connection on own dispose. If , will dispose connection on own dispose only if it created by instance.

## LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.LinqToDB#Internal#Options#IOptionSet#Default

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.LinqToDB#Internal#Options#IOptionSet#Default`
- Kind: Property

## LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.MappingSchema

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.MappingSchema`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets optional instance to use with instance.

## LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.OnEntityDescriptorCreated

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.OnEntityDescriptorCreated`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Action, called on entity descriptor creation. Allows descriptor modification. When not specified, application-wide callback called.

## LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.ProviderName

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions.ProviderName`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets optional provider name to use with instance.

## LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.CommandTimeout

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.CommandTimeout`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets command execution timeout in seconds. Supported values: 0 : infinite timeout > 0 : command timeout in seconds -1 on property get : default provider/connection command timeout value used (not controlled by Linq To DB) negative value on property set : throws exception. To reset timeout to provider/connection defaults use or methods

## LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.ConfigurationString

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.ConfigurationString`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Database configuration name (connection string name).

## LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.Connection

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.Connection`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets underlying database connection, used by current connection object, or opens new.

## LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.ConnectionString

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.ConnectionString`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Database connection string.

## LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.CurrentCommand

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.CurrentCommand`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets current command instance if it exists or otherwise.

## LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.DataProvider

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.DataProvider`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Database provider implementation for specific database engine.

## LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.DefaultConfiguration

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.DefaultConfiguration`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets default connection configuration name. Used by by default and could be set automatically from: - ; - first non-global connection string name from ; - first non-global connection string name passed to method.

## LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.DefaultDataProvider

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.DefaultDataProvider`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets name of default data provider, used by new connection if user didn't specified provider explicitly in constructor or in connection options. Initialized with value from ..

## LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.DefaultOnTraceConnection

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.DefaultOnTraceConnection`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets default trace handler.

## LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.DefaultSettings

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.DefaultSettings`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets default connection settings. By default, contains settings from linq2db configuration section from configuration file (not supported by .Net Core).

## LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.InlineParameters

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.InlineParameters`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets option to force inline parameter values as literals into command text. If parameter inlining not supported for specific value type, it will be used as parameter.

## LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.IsMarsEnabled

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.IsMarsEnabled`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets status of Multiple Active Result Sets (MARS) feature. This feature available only for SQL Azure and SQL Server 2005+.

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.OnTraceConnection`
- Kind: Property

## LinqToDB.Data.RetryPolicy.RetryPolicyBase.ExceptionsEncountered

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.RetryPolicy.RetryPolicyBase.ExponentialBase`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: The base for the exponential function used to compute the delay between retries, must not be lesser than 1.

## LinqToDB.Data.RetryPolicy.RetryPolicyBase.MaxRetryCount

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.RetryPolicy.RetryPolicyBase.MaxRetryCount`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: The maximum number of retry attempts.

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.RetryPolicy.RetryPolicyBase.MaxRetryDelay`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: The maximum delay in milliseconds between retries.

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.RetryPolicy.RetryPolicyBase.Random`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: A pseudo-random number generator that can be used to vary the delay between retries.

## LinqToDB.Data.RetryPolicy.RetryPolicyBase.RandomFactor

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.RetryPolicy.RetryPolicyBase.RandomFactor`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: The maximum random factor, must not be lesser than 1.

## LinqToDB.Data.RetryPolicy.RetryPolicyBase.Suspended

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.RetryPolicy.RetryPolicyBase.Suspended`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates whether the strategy is suspended. The strategy is typically suspending while executing to avoid recursive execution from nested operations.

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.RetryPolicy.RetryPolicyOptions.Coefficient`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: The coefficient for the exponential function used to compute the delay between retries, must be nonnegative.

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Data.RetryPolicy.RetryPolicyOptions.ExponentialBase`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: The base for the exponential function used to compute the delay between retries, must not be lesser than 1.

- Kind: Property

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.ClickHouse.ClickHouseOptions.UseStandardCompatibleAggregates`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Enables -OrNull combinator for Min, Max, Sum and Avg aggregation functions to support SQL standard-compatible behavior. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.DB2.DB2Options.IdentifierQuoteMode

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.DB2.DB2Options.IdentifierQuoteMode`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Identifier quotation logic for SQL generation. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdOptions.IdentifierQuoteMode

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdOptions.IdentifierQuoteMode`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Specifies how identifiers like table and field names should be quoted. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdOptions.IsLiteralEncodingSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdOptions.IsLiteralEncodingSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Specifies that Firebird supports literal encoding. Available from version 2.5 and could be used for Dialect 1 databases.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.Informix.InformixOptions.ExplicitFractionalSecondsSeparator

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Informix.InformixOptions.ExplicitFractionalSecondsSeparator`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Enables use of explicit fractional seconds separator in datetime values. Must be enabled for Informix starting from v11.70.xC8 and v12.10.xC2. More details at: https://www.ibm.com/support/knowledgecenter/SSGU8G_12.1.0/com.ibm.po.doc/new_features_ce.htm#newxc2__xc2_datetime

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.OracleOptions.AlternativeBulkCopy

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.OracleOptions.AlternativeBulkCopy`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Defines type of multi-row INSERT operation to generate for bulk copy mode.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.OracleOptions.DontEscapeLowercaseIdentifiers

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.OracleOptions.DontEscapeLowercaseIdentifiers`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets flag to tell LinqToDB to quote identifiers, if they contain lowercase letters. Default value: . This flag is added for backward compatibility and not recommended for use with new applications.

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLOptions.IdentifierQuoteMode

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLOptions.IdentifierQuoteMode`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Specify identifiers quotation logic for SQL generation. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLOptions.NormalizeTimestampData

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLOptions.NormalizeTimestampData`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Enables normalization of and data, passed to query as parameter or passed to APIs, to comform with Npgsql 6 requerements: convert value to UTC value with zero Use for timestamptz valuesUse for timestamp values with kind Default value: .

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlCe.SqlCeOptions.InlineFunctionParameters

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlCe.SqlCeOptions.InlineFunctionParameters`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Enables force inlining of function parameters to support SQL CE 3.0. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SQLite.SQLiteOptions.AlwaysCheckDbNull

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SQLite.SQLiteOptions.AlwaysCheckDbNull`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Enables null-value checks during database data mapping even if SQLite reports that column cannot be NULL to avoid on mapping when database reports nullability incorrectly. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Connections

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Connections`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: @@CONNECTIONS (Transact-SQL)This function returns the number of attempted connections - both successful and unsuccessful - since SQL Server was last started.
- Returns: integer

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.CpuBusy

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.CpuBusy`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: @@CPU_BUSY (Transact-SQL)This function returns the amount of time that SQL Server has spent in active operation since its latest start.
- Returns: integer

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.CurrentTimestamp

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.CurrentTimestamp`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: CURRENT_TIMESTAMP (Transact-SQL)This function returns the current database system timestamp as a datetime value, without the database time zone offset. CURRENT_TIMESTAMP derives this value from the operating system of the computer on which the instance of SQL Server runs.
- Returns: datetime

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.DateFirst

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.DateFirst`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: @@DATEFIRST (Transact-SQL)This function returns the current value of SET DATEFIRST, for a specific session.
- Returns: tinyint

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.DbTS

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.DbTS`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: @@DBTS (Transact-SQL)This function returns the value of the current timestamp data type for the current database. The current database will have a guaranteed unique timestamp value.
- Returns: varbinary

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Identity

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Identity`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: @@IDENTITY (Transact-SQL)Is a system function that returns the last-inserted identity value.
- Returns: numeric(38,0)

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Idle

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Idle`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: @@IDLE (Transact-SQL)Returns the time that SQL Server has been idle since it was last started.
- Returns: integer

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.IOBusy

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.IOBusy`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: @@IO_BUSY (Transact-SQL)Returns the time that SQL Server has spent performing input and output operations since SQL Server was last started.
- Returns: integer

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.LangID

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.LangID`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: @@LANGID (Transact-SQL)Returns the local language identifier (ID) of the language that is currently being used.
- Returns: smallint

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Language

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Language`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: @@LANGUAGE (Transact-SQL)Returns the name of the language currently being used.
- Returns: nvarchar

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.LockTimeout

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.LockTimeout`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: @@LOCK_TIMEOUT (Transact-SQL)Returns the current lock time-out setting in milliseconds for the current session.
- Returns: integer

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.MaxConnections

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.MaxConnections`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: @@MAX_CONNECTIONS (Transact-SQL)Returns the maximum number of simultaneous user connections allowed on an instance of SQL Server. The number returned is not necessarily the number currently configured.
- Returns: integer

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.MaxPrecision

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.MaxPrecision`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: @@MAX_PRECISION (Transact-SQL)Returns the precision level used by decimal and numeric data types as currently set in the server.
- Returns: tinyint

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.NestLevel

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.NestLevel`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: @@NESTLEVEL (Transact-SQL)Returns the nesting level of the current stored procedure execution (initially 0) on the local server.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Options

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.Options`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: @@OPTIONS (Transact-SQL)Returns information about the current SET options.
- Returns: integer

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.PacketErrors

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.PacketErrors`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: @@PACKET_ERRORS (Transact-SQL)Returns the number of network packet errors that have occurred on SQL Server connections since SQL Server was last started.
- Returns: integer

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.PackReceived

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.PackReceived`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: @@PACK_RECEIVED (Transact-SQL)Returns the number of input packets read from the network by SQL Server since it was last started.
- Returns: integer

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.PackSent

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.PackSent`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: @@PACK_SENT (Transact-SQL)Returns the number of output packets written to the network by SQL Server since it was last started.
- Returns: integer

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.RemServer

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.RemServer`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: @@REMSERVER (Transact-SQL)Returns the name of the remote SQL Server database server as it appears in the login record.
- Returns: nvarchar(128)

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.RowCount

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.RowCount`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: @@ROWCOUNT (Transact-SQL)Returns the number of rows affected by the last statement. If the number of rows is more than 2 billion, use ROWCOUNT_BIG.
- Returns: int

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ServerName

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ServerName`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: @@SERVERNAME (Transact-SQL)Returns the name of the local server that is running SQL Server.
- Returns: nvarchar

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ServiceName

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.ServiceName`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: @@SERVICENAME (Transact-SQL)Returns the name of the registry key under which SQL Server is running. @@SERVICENAME returns 'MSSQLSERVER' if the current instance is the default instance; this function returns the instance name if the current instance is a named instance.
- Returns: nvarchar

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.SpID

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.SpID`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: @@SPID (Transact-SQL)Returns the session ID of the current user process.
- Returns: smallint

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.TextSize

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.TextSize`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: @@TEXTSIZE (Transact-SQL)Returns the current value of the TEXTSIZE option.
- Returns: integer

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.TimeTicks

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.TimeTicks`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: @@TIMETICKS (Transact-SQL)Returns the number of microseconds per tick.
- Returns: integer

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.TotalErrors

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.TotalErrors`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: @@TOTAL_ERRORS (Transact-SQL)Returns the number of microseconds per tick.
- Returns: integer

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.TotalRead

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.TotalRead`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: @@TOTAL_READ (Transact-SQL)Returns the number of microseconds per tick.
- Returns: integer

## LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.TotalWrite

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.TotalWrite`
- Kind: Property

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.Access.AccessBulkCopy.MaxParameters

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.Access.AccessBulkCopy.MaxParameters`
- Kind: Property
- Remarks: Settings based on https://www.jooq.org/doc/3.12/manual/sql-building/dsl-context/custom-settings/settings-inline-threshold/ We subtract 1 here to be safe since some ADO providers use parameter for command itself.

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.Access.AccessBulkCopy.MaxSqlLength

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.Access.AccessBulkCopy.MaxSqlLength`
- Kind: Property
- Remarks: This max is based on https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/access-specifications-0cf3c66f-9cf2-4e32-9568-98c1025bb47c

- Kind: Property

- Kind: Property

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.DB2.DB2BulkCopy.MaxParameters

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.DB2.DB2BulkCopy.MaxParameters`
- Kind: Property
- Remarks: Settings based on https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/i/7.3?topic=reference-sql-limits We subtract 1 here to be safe since some ADO providers use parameter for command itself.

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.DB2.DB2BulkCopy.MaxSqlLength

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.DB2.DB2BulkCopy.MaxSqlLength`
- Kind: Property
- Remarks: Setting based on https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/i/7.3?topic=reference-sql-limits Max is actually 2MIB, but we keep a lower number here to avoid the cost of huge statements.

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.DB2.DB2ProviderAdapter.ConnectionWrapper

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.DB2.DB2ProviderAdapter.ConnectionWrapper`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: This is internal API and is not intended for use by Linq To DB applications. It may change or be removed without further notice.

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdBulkCopy.MaxParameters

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdBulkCopy.MaxParameters`
- Kind: Property
- Remarks: Based on https://github.com/FirebirdSQL/firebird/blob/799bca3ca5f9eb604433addc0f2b7cb3b6c07275/src/dsql/DsqlCompilerScratch.cpp#L528 Max is 65536/2. We subtract one from that in case ADO provider uses parameter for statement.

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdBulkCopy.MaxSqlLength

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdBulkCopy.MaxSqlLength`
- Kind: Property
- Remarks: Number based on http://www.firebirdfaq.org/faq197/ Firebird 2.5 has 64k limit, Firebird 3.0+ 10MB.

- Kind: Property

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.IDynamicProviderAdapter.DataReaderType`
- Kind: Property

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.IDynamicProviderAdapter.ParameterType`

- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets type, that implements for current ADO.NET provider. For providers/databases without transaction support contains .

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.Informix.InformixProviderAdapter.IsIDSProvider

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.Informix.InformixProviderAdapter.IsIDSProvider`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: IDS or SQLI provider.

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlBulkCopy.MaxParameters

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlBulkCopy.MaxParameters`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Settings based on https://www.jooq.org/doc/3.12/manual/sql-building/dsl-context/custom-settings/settings-inline-threshold/ MySQL supports more but realistically this might be too much already for practical cases.

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlBulkCopy.MaxSqlLength

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlBulkCopy.MaxSqlLength`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: MySQL can support much larger sizes, based on https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/8.0/en/server-system-variables.html#sysvar_max_allowed_packet But we keep a smaller number here to avoid choking the network.

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlProviderAdapter.GetDateTimeOffsetMethodName

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlProviderAdapter.GetDateTimeOffsetMethodName`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: MySqlConnector-only.

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlProviderAdapter.GetDbType

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlProviderAdapter.GetDbType`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Returns object, because both providers use different enums and we anyway don't need typed value.

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlProviderAdapter.GetMySqlDecimalMethodName

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlProviderAdapter.GetMySqlDecimalMethodName`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Not supported by MySqlConnector prior to 2.1.0.

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlProviderAdapter.MySqlDecimalGetter

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlProviderAdapter.MySqlDecimalGetter`

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlProviderAdapter.MySqlDecimalType

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.MySql.MySqlProviderAdapter.MySqlDecimalType`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Not supported by MySqlConnector prior to 2.1.0.

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLBulkCopy.MaxParameters

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLBulkCopy.MaxParameters`
- Kind: Property
- Remarks: Settings based on https://www.jooq.org/doc/3.12/manual/sql-building/dsl-context/custom-settings/settings-inline-threshold/ We subtract 1 based on possibility of provider using parameter for command.

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLBulkCopy.MaxSqlLength

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLBulkCopy.MaxSqlLength`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Setting based on https://stackoverflow.com/a/4937695/2937845 Max is actually 2GiB, but we keep a lower number here to avoid the cost of huge statements.

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.ReaderInfo.DataReaderType

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.ReaderInfo.DataReaderType`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Type of implementation. Could not match Type, implemented by ADO.NET provider if wrapper like MiniProfiler used without proper registration provided.

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.ReaderInfo.DataTypeName

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.ReaderInfo.DataTypeName`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Type name, returned by for column.

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.ReaderInfo.FieldType

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.ReaderInfo.FieldType`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Type, returned by for column.

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.ReaderInfo.ProviderFieldType

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.ReaderInfo.ProviderFieldType`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Type, returned by for column.

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.ReaderInfo.ToType

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.ReaderInfo.ToType`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Expected type (e.g. type of property in mapped entity class). For nullable value types doesn't include wrapper.

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.SQLite.SQLiteBulkCopy.MaxParameters

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.SQLite.SQLiteBulkCopy.MaxParameters`
- Kind: Property
- Remarks: Settings based on https://www.jooq.org/doc/3.12/manual/sql-building/dsl-context/custom-settings/settings-inline-threshold/ We subtract 1 based on possibility of ADO Provider using parameter for command.

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.SQLite.SQLiteBulkCopy.MaxSqlLength

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.SQLite.SQLiteBulkCopy.MaxSqlLength`
- Kind: Property
- Remarks: Based on https://www.sqlite.org/limits.html. Since SQLite is parsed locally by the lib, we aren't worried about network congestion and keep the max.

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerBulkCopy.MaxParameters

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerBulkCopy.MaxParameters`
- Kind: Property
- Remarks: Settings based on https://www.jooq.org/doc/3.12/manual/sql-building/dsl-context/custom-settings/settings-inline-threshold/ We subtract 1 since SQL Server ADO Provider uses one parameter for command.

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerBulkCopy.MaxSqlLength

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerBulkCopy.MaxSqlLength`
- Kind: Property
- Remarks: Based on https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/sql-server/maximum-capacity-specifications-for-sql-server?redirectedfrom=MSDN&view=sql-server-ver15 Default Max is actually (4096*65536) = 256MIB, but we keep a lower number here to avoid the cost of huge statements.

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerProviderAdapter.SqlServer2005BulkCopyUnsupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerProviderAdapter.SqlServer2005BulkCopyUnsupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: True when the loaded SqlClient driver dropped SqlBulkCopy support for SQL Server 2005 (Microsoft.Data.SqlClient 7.0+). The destination-table inspection statement SqlBulkCopy issues on 7.0+ uses T-SQL syntax 2005 doesn't accept, so callers should fall back to a non-SqlBulkCopy path for 2005 targets. False for System.Data.SqlClient regardless of version.

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.Sybase.SybaseBulkCopy.MaxParameters

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.Sybase.SybaseBulkCopy.MaxParameters`
- Kind: Property
- Remarks: Settings based on https://www.jooq.org/doc/3.12/manual/sql-building/dsl-context/custom-settings/settings-inline-threshold/ We subtract 1 based on possibility of provider using parameter for command.

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.Sybase.SybaseBulkCopy.MaxSqlLength

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.Sybase.SybaseBulkCopy.MaxSqlLength`
- Kind: Property
- Remarks: Setting is conservative based on https://maxdb.sap.com/doc/7_6/f6/069940ccd42a54e10000000a1550b0/content.htm Possible to be higher in other versions.

## LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.Ydb.YdbSqlExpressionConvertVisitor.SupportsNullInColumn

- Kind: Property

## LinqToDB.Internal.Expressions.Types.TypeWrapper.CompiledWrappers

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.Expressions.Types.TypeWrapper.CompiledWrappers`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Provides access to delegates, created from expressions, defined in wrapper class using following property and type mappings, configured for : private static IEumerable<T> Wrappers { get; } where T could be or Tuple<LambdaExpression, bool>. Boolean flag means that mapping expression compilation allowed to fail if it is set to . This could be used to map optional API, that present only in specific versions of provider. If wrapper doesn't need any wrapper delegates, this property could be ommited.

- Kind: Property

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.ConcatFunctionName

- Kind: Property
- Summary: Function name used when is . Defaults to CONCAT; override e.g. for ClickHouse's lowercase concat.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.ConcatStyle
