- Kind: Property
- Summary: SQL-emit shape for . Defaults to ; override per provider.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.CteFirst

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.CteFirst`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Identifies CTE clause location: (default): WITH clause goes first in query: WITH clause goes before SELECT

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.FakeTable

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.FakeTable`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: If set to false and provider doesn't support SELECTs without FROM clause, this property should contain name of table (or equivalent SQL) with single record. IMPORTANT: as this property could return SQL, we don't escape it, so it should contain only valid SQL/identifiers.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.FakeTableSchema

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.FakeTableSchema`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: If set to false and provider doesn't support SELECTs without FROM clause, this property could contain name of schema for table with single record. Returned name should be already escaped if escaping required.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.InlineComma

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.InlineComma`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Inline comma separator. Default value: ", "

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.IsEmptyValuesSourceSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.IsEmptyValuesSourceSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: If true, builder will generate command for empty enumerable source; Otherwise exception will be generated.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.IsOverRequiredWithinGroup

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.IsOverRequiredWithinGroup`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: if provider requires OVER() clause to be present in window function WITHIN GROUP. Currently only SQL Server

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.IsValuesSyntaxSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.IsValuesSyntaxSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: If true, provider supports list of VALUES as a source element of merge command.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.OpenParens

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.OpenParens`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: End-of-line open parentheses element. Default value: "("

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.RequiresConstantColumnAliases

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.RequiresConstantColumnAliases`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: If true, provider require column aliases for each column. E.g. as table_alias (column_alias1, column_alias2).

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.SupportsColumnAliasesInSource

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.SupportsColumnAliasesInSource`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: If true, provider supports column aliases specification after table alias. E.g. as table_alias (column_alias1, column_alias2).

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.SupportsMaterializedCteHint

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.SupportsMaterializedCteHint`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Providers that understand WITH cte AS MATERIALIZED (...) override this to . Currently: PostgreSQL 12+, SQLite 3.35+, ClickHouse 26.3+.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.SupportsNotMaterializedCteHint

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.SupportsNotMaterializedCteHint`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Providers that understand WITH cte AS NOT MATERIALIZED (...) override this to . Defaults to because most engines support both polarities; ClickHouse is an exception (no NOT MATERIALIZED).

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.WrapJoinCondition

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.WrapJoinCondition`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: if it is needed to wrap join condition with ()

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlExpressionConvertVisitor.ConcatRequiresExplicitStringCast

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlExpressionConvertVisitor.ConcatRequiresExplicitStringCast`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: When (default), wraps every non-string operand in an explicit CAST(... AS VARCHAR(N)) before adding it to the concat chain. Required for providers whose concat operator is + (SQL Server pre-2025, SqlCe, Access) — SQL-standard data-type precedence would otherwise try to coerce string operands to the non-string side's type. Most providers whose final concat operator is || (PostgreSQL / Oracle / SQLite / SAP HANA / DuckDB / Firebird / DB2 / Informix / SQL Server 2025+) or CONCAT(...) function (MySQL / ClickHouse) auto-coerce non-string operands and override this to for cleaner SQL. Sybase ASE is the exception: it emits || but keeps this , since ASE requires an explicit convert() for non-character operands under both + and ||.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlExpressionConvertVisitor.LikeCharactersToEscape

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlExpressionConvertVisitor.LikeCharactersToEscape`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Characters with special meaning in LIKE predicate (defined by ) that should be escaped to be used as matched character. Default: ["%", "_", "?", "*", "#", "[", "]"].

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlExpressionConvertVisitor.LikeEscapeCharacter

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlExpressionConvertVisitor.LikeEscapeCharacter`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Escape sequence/character to escape special characters in LIKE predicate (defined by ). Default: "~".

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlExpressionConvertVisitor.LikeIsEscapeSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlExpressionConvertVisitor.LikeIsEscapeSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Should be for provider with LIKE ... ESCAPE modifier support. Default: .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.AcceptsOuterExpressionInAggregate

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.AcceptsOuterExpressionInAggregate`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Provider supports aggregated expression with Outer reference SELECT ( SELECT SUM(inner.FieldX + outer.FieldOuter) FROM table2 inner ) AS Sum_Column FROM table1 outer Otherwise aggeragated expression will be wrapped in subquery and aggregate function will be applied to subquery column. SELECT ( SELECT SUM(sub.Column) FROM ( SELECT inner.FieldX + outer.FieldOuter AS Column FROM table2 inner ) sub ) AS Sum_Column FROM table1 outer Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.AcceptsTakeAsParameter

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.AcceptsTakeAsParameter`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates that TAKE/TOP/LIMIT could accept parameter. Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.AcceptsTakeAsParameterIfSkip

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.AcceptsTakeAsParameterIfSkip`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates that TAKE/LIMIT could accept parameter only if also SKIP/OFFSET specified. Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.CalculateSupportedCorrelatedLevelWithAggregateQueries

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.CalculateSupportedCorrelatedLevelWithAggregateQueries`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the calculation of deep level includes aggregation queries. Works in conjunction with .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.CanCombineParameters

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.CanCombineParameters`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates that provider could share parameter between statements in multi-statement batch. Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.CustomFlags

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.CustomFlags`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Flags for use by external providers.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.DefaultMultiQueryIsolationLevel

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.DefaultMultiQueryIsolationLevel`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Used when there is query which needs several additional database requests for completing query (e.g. eager load or client-side GroupBy). Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.DefaultNullsOrdering

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.DefaultNullsOrdering`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: The provider's natural placement of NULL values in an ORDER BY (the placement used when no NULLS FIRST/NULLS LAST is specified). (the default) means it is unknown, so a requested is always honored (emulated or rendered) and never elided. When set, a requested position that already equals the natural placement for the item's direction is dropped, avoiding a redundant emulation sort key or NULLS token.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.DoesProviderTreatsEmptyStringAsNull

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.DoesProviderTreatsEmptyStringAsNull`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Provider treats empty string as in queries. It is specific behaviour only for Oracle. Default

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsAccessBuggyLeftJoinConstantNullability

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsAccessBuggyLeftJoinConstantNullability`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates that provider has bug in LEFT join translator. In the following example can_be_null is always not null, which is wrong. SELECT can_be_null FROM Some LEFT JOIN 1 as can_be_null, * FROM Other ON ... As workaround translator is trying to check for nullability all projected fields. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsAllSetOperationsSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsAllSetOperationsSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Provider supports EXCEPT ALL, INTERSECT ALL set operators. Otherwise they will be emulated. Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsApplyJoinSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsApplyJoinSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates support for OUTER/CROSS APPLY. Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsColumnSubqueryShouldNotContainParentIsNotNull

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsColumnSubqueryShouldNotContainParentIsNotNull`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Workaround for an Oracle 11 bug: when set to , the validator rejects column-position scalar subqueries whose body contains an IS NOT NULL predicate resolving against a parent (outer) source, forcing the optimizer to pick a different shape (or fail upstream with a clearer error) instead of emitting SQL the server mishandles. The check is gated on being inside a SELECT-projection column expression — IS NOT NULL in WHERE / ON / HAVING positions is unaffected. Default value: .
- Remarks: Set to only for Oracle 11, where deep correlation through column-position scalar subqueries (UNION ALL, derived tables, IS NOT NULL projections) leaks alias scope and the server raises ORA-00904 at runtime. Oracle 12+ resolves these references correctly. The flag's "should not contain" naming reflects its role as a provider-bug constraint, not a feature toggle. See Issue3557Case1 test.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsColumnSubqueryWithParentReferenceAndTakeSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsColumnSubqueryWithParentReferenceAndTakeSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Provider supports column subqueries which references outer scope when nesting is more than 1
- Remarks: Used only for Oracle 11. linq2db emulates Take(n) via 'ROWNUM' and it causes additional nesting. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsCommonTableExpressionsSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsCommonTableExpressionsSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates support of CTE expressions. If provider does not support CTE, unsuported exception will be thrown when using CTE. Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsComplexJoinConditionSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsComplexJoinConditionSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: When disabled, all conditions from INNER JOIN ON moved to WHERE except conjunction of equality predicates. FROM T1 INNER JOIN T2 ON t1.field1 == t2.field1 AND t1.field2 == t2.field2 AND t1.field3 > 10 -- with flag: FROM T1 INNER JOIN T2 ON t1.field1 == t2.field1 AND t1.field2 == t2.field2 WHERE t1.field3 > 10 Default: .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsCorrelatedSubQueryTakeSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsCorrelatedSubQueryTakeSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates support for paging clause in correlated subquery. Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsCountSubQuerySupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsCountSubQuerySupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates that database supports count subquery as scalar in column. SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM some_table) FROM ... Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsCrossApplyJoinSupportsCondition

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsCrossApplyJoinSupportsCondition`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates support for CROSS APPLY supports condition LATERAL JOIN for example. Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsCrossJoinSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsCrossJoinSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates support for CROSS JOIN. Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsCrossJoinSyntaxRequired

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsCrossJoinSyntaxRequired`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: When enabled, always prefer "FROM T1 CROSS JOIN T2" over "FROM T1, T2" join syntax. Default: .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsCTESupportsOrdering

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsCTESupportsOrdering`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates that top level CTE query supports ORDER BY. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsDerivedTableOrderBySupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsDerivedTableOrderBySupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Provider supports SUM/AVG/MIN/MAX(DISTINCT column) function. Otherwise, it will be emulated. Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsDerivedTableTakeSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsDerivedTableTakeSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates support for paging clause in derived table. Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsDistinctFromSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsDistinctFromSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Provider supports correlated DISTINCT FROM directly or through db-specific operator/method (e.g. DECODE, IS, <=>). This doesn't include emulation using INTERSECT. Default

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsDistinctSetOperationsSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsDistinctSetOperationsSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Provider supports EXCEPT, INTERSECT set operators. Otherwise it will be emulated. Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsExistsPreferableForContains

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsExistsPreferableForContains`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsIdentityParameterRequired

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsIdentityParameterRequired`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates that provider requires explicit output parameter for insert with identity queries to get identity from database. Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsInsertOrUpdateSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsInsertOrUpdateSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates support for single-query insert-or-update operation support. Otherwise two separate queries used to emulate operation (update, then insert if nothing found to update). Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsJoinDerivedTableWithTakeInvalid

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsJoinDerivedTableWithTakeInvalid`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates that provider has issue with any JOIN to subquery which has TOP statement. Default .
- Remarks: Currently use as workaround over Sybase bug.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsMultiTablesSupportsJoins

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsMultiTablesSupportsJoins`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates that provider supports JOINS in FROM clause which have several tables SELECT ... FROM table1 INNER JOIN query ON ... , table2 Otherwise the following query will be generated: SELECT ... FROM ( SELECT ... FROM table1, table2 ) S INNER JOIN query ON ... , FROM table2 Default: . Currently not supported only by Access.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsNamingQueryBlockSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsNamingQueryBlockSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Provider supports Naming Query Blocks QB_NAME(qb) Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsNestedJoinsSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsNestedJoinsSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Provider supports nested joins A JOIN (B JOIN C ON ?) ON ? otherwise nested join replaced with sub-query A JOIN (SELECT ? FROM B JOIN C ON ?) ON ? . Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsNullsOrderingSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsNullsOrderingSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Provider renders NULLS FIRST / NULLS LAST natively in ORDER BY (and window OVER(ORDER BY …)). When (the default), is emulated via a CASE WHEN <expr> IS NULL THEN … sort key.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsOrderByAggregateFunctionSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsOrderByAggregateFunctionSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Provider supports Aggregate function in ORDER BY. Default (set by ): .
- Remarks: Applied only to SqlCe provider.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsOrderByAggregateSubquerySupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsOrderByAggregateSubquerySupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Provider supports aggregation subquery in ORDER BY Clause. Default: .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsOrderBySubQuerySupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsOrderBySubQuerySupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Provider supports subquery in ORDER BY Clause.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsOuterApplyJoinSupportsCondition

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsOuterApplyJoinSupportsCondition`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates support for OUTER APPLY supports condition LATERAL JOIN for example. Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsOuterJoinSupportsInnerJoin

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsOuterJoinSupportsInnerJoin`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Provider supports INNER JOIN inside OUTER JOIN. For example: LEFT JOIN table1 ON ... INNER JOIN query ON ... Otherwise the following query will be left: LEFT JOIN ( SELECT ... FROM table1 INNER JOIN query ON ... ) Default: . Currently not supported only by Access.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsParameterOrderDependent

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsParameterOrderDependent`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates that provider (not database!) uses positional parameters instead of named parameters (parameter values assigned in order they appear in query, not by parameter name). Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsRecursiveCTEJoinWithConditionSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsRecursiveCTEJoinWithConditionSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Provider supports INNER JOIN with condition inside Recursive CTE, currently not supported only by DB2 Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsRowNumberWithoutOrderBySupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsRowNumberWithoutOrderBySupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Provider supports ROW_NUMBER OVER () without ORDER BY Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsSimpleCoalesceSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsSimpleCoalesceSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates that provider supports simple COALESCE translation without complex translation.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsSkipSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsSkipSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates support for SKIP/OFFSET paging clause (parameter) without TAKE clause. Provider could set this flag even if database not support it if emulates missing functionality. E.g. : TAKE [MAX_ALLOWED_VALUE] SKIP skip_value Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsSkipSupportedIfTake

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsSkipSupportedIfTake`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates support for SKIP/OFFSET paging clause (parameter) only if also TAKE/LIMIT specified. Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsSubQueryColumnSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsSubQueryColumnSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates support for scalar subquery in select list. E.g. SELECT (SELECT TOP 1 value FROM some_table) AS MyColumn, ... Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsSubqueryExpressionInsidePredicateSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsSubqueryExpressionInsidePredicateSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates that provider supports subquery in predicate, like (SELECT column FROM ...) IS NULL, COALESCE((SELECT column FROM ...), 0).

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsSubqueryJoinOnOuterReferenceSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsSubqueryJoinOnOuterReferenceSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Provider supports JOIN to subquery which contains reference to parent table in join condition.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsSubQueryOrderBySupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsSubQueryOrderBySupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates support of ORDER BY clause in sub-queries. Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsSubQuerySkipSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsSubQuerySkipSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates support for skip clause in column expression subquery. Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsSubQueryTakeSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsSubQueryTakeSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates support for paging clause in subquery. Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsSubqueryWithParentReferenceInJoinConditionSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsSubqueryWithParentReferenceInJoinConditionSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Provider supports condition in subquery which references parent table Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsSupportedSimpleCorrelatedSubqueries

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsSupportedSimpleCorrelatedSubqueries`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Provider supports correlated subqueris, which can be easily converted to JOIN. Default (set by ): .
- Remarks: Applied only to ClickHouse provider.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsSupportsJoinWithoutCondition

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsSupportsJoinWithoutCondition`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates that provider supports JOIN without condition ON 1=1. Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsTakeWithInAllAnySomeSubquerySupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsTakeWithInAllAnySomeSubquerySupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: When disabled, SQL dialect doesn't support Take/LIMIT & IN/ALL/ANY/SOME subquery.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsUnionAllOrderBySupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsUnionAllOrderBySupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates support of ORDER BY clause in UNION ALL. Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsUpdateFromSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsUpdateFromSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates support for following UPDATE syntax: UPDATE A SET ... FROM B Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsUpdateSkipTakeSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsUpdateSkipTakeSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Provider supports SKIP+TAKE limit for UPDATE query. Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsUpdateTakeSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsUpdateTakeSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Provider supports TAKE limit for UPDATE query. Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsWindowFunctionsSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsWindowFunctionsSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates that provider supports window functions. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.MaxInListValuesCount

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.MaxInListValuesCount`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Specifies limit of number of values in single IN predicate without splitting it into several IN's. Default (set by ): int.MaxValue (basically means there is no limit).

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.OutputDeleteUseSpecialTable

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.OutputDeleteUseSpecialTable`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: If , removed record fields in OUTPUT clause of DELETE statement should be referenced using table with special name (e.g. DELETED or OLD). Otherwise fields should be referenced using target table. Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.OutputInsertUseSpecialTable

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.OutputInsertUseSpecialTable`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: If , added record fields in OUTPUT clause of INSERT statement should be referenced using table with special name (e.g. INSERTED or NEW). Otherwise fields should be referenced using target table. Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.OutputMergeUseSpecialTables

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.OutputMergeUseSpecialTables`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: If , OUTPUT clause supports both OLD and NEW data in MERGE statement using tables with special names. Otherwise only current record fields (after all changes) available using target table. Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.OutputUpdateUseSpecialTables

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.OutputUpdateUseSpecialTables`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: If , OUTPUT clause supports both OLD and NEW data in UPDATE statement using tables with special names. Otherwise only current record fields (after update) available using target table. Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.RowConstructorSupport

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.RowConstructorSupport`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Provider support Row Constructor `(1, 2, 3)` in various positions (flags) Default (set by ): .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.SupportedCorrelatedSubqueriesLevel

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.SupportedCorrelatedSubqueriesLevel`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Provider supports correlated subqueris, but limited how deep in subquery outer reference Default . If this value is 0c>, provider do not support correlated subqueries

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.SupportsBooleanType

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.SupportsBooleanType`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates that boolean type could be used as predicate without additional conversions. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.SupportsPredicatesComparison

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.SupportsPredicatesComparison`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates that provider supports direct comparison of predicates. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.TakeHintsSupported

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.TakeHintsSupported`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates supported TAKE/LIMIT hints. Default (set by ): (none).

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.CteClause.Annotations

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.CteClause.Annotations`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Open-ended metadata bag for provider-specific CTE hints (e.g. PostgreSQL MATERIALIZED). Providers that do not recognize an annotation name ignore it.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.IQueryElement.ElementType

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.IQueryElement.ElementType`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: AST node type.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.NullabilityContext.JoinSource

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.NullabilityContext.JoinSource`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Current Join table source. Used for excluding source from nullable sources check

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.NullabilityContext.NonQuery

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.NullabilityContext.NonQuery`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Context for non-select queries of places where we don't know select query.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.NullabilityContext.Queries

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.NullabilityContext.Queries`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Current context query.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQuery.DoNotRemove

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQuery.DoNotRemove`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets flag when sub-query can be removed during optimization.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQuery.UniqueKeys

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQuery.UniqueKeys`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Contains list of columns that build unique key for this sub-query. Used in JoinOptimizer for safely removing sub-query from resulting SQL.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$2D0526ADB551921D1D0FBD46170AA51F.HasGroupBy

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$2D0526ADB551921D1D0FBD46170AA51F.HasGroupBy`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Determines whether the specified query includes any GROUP BY clauses.
- Returns: if the query contains one or more GROUP BY clauses; otherwise, .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$2D0526ADB551921D1D0FBD46170AA51F.HasHaving

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$2D0526ADB551921D1D0FBD46170AA51F.HasHaving`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Determines whether the specified select query includes a HAVING clause with a non-trivial search condition.
- Returns: if the select query contains a HAVING clause with a condition other than always true; otherwise, .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$2D0526ADB551921D1D0FBD46170AA51F.HasNoColumns

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$2D0526ADB551921D1D0FBD46170AA51F.HasNoColumns`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Determines whether the specified select query contains no columns in its SELECT clause.
- Returns: if the select query has no columns defined in its SELECT clause; otherwise, .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$2D0526ADB551921D1D0FBD46170AA51F.HasNoTables

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$2D0526ADB551921D1D0FBD46170AA51F.HasNoTables`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Determines whether the specified query does not reference any tables in its FROM clause.
- Returns: if the query's FROM clause contains no tables; otherwise, .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$2D0526ADB551921D1D0FBD46170AA51F.HasOrderBy

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$2D0526ADB551921D1D0FBD46170AA51F.HasOrderBy`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Determines whether the specified query includes any ORDER BY clauses.
- Returns: if the query contains at least one ORDER BY clause; otherwise, .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$2D0526ADB551921D1D0FBD46170AA51F.HasWhere

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$2D0526ADB551921D1D0FBD46170AA51F.HasWhere`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Determines whether the specified query includes a WHERE clause with any conditions.
- Returns: if the query contains a WHERE clause with at least one condition; otherwise, .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$2D0526ADB551921D1D0FBD46170AA51F.IsDistinct

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$2D0526ADB551921D1D0FBD46170AA51F.IsDistinct`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Determines whether the SELECT statement in the specified query is marked as DISTINCT.
- Returns: if the SELECT statement is DISTINCT; otherwise, .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$2D0526ADB551921D1D0FBD46170AA51F.IsLimited

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$2D0526ADB551921D1D0FBD46170AA51F.IsLimited`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Determines whether the specified query includes a limit or offset clause.
- Returns: if the query specifies a limit or offset; otherwise, .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$2D0526ADB551921D1D0FBD46170AA51F.IsSimple

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$2D0526ADB551921D1D0FBD46170AA51F.IsSimple`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Determines whether the specified select query is a simple query without any set operators.
- Remarks: A simple select query is one that does not include set operations such as UNION, INTERSECT, or EXCEPT. Use this method to check if a query can be treated as a basic select statement without additional set logic.
- Returns: if the select query is simple and does not contain set operators; otherwise, .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$2D0526ADB551921D1D0FBD46170AA51F.IsSimpleOrSet

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$2D0526ADB551921D1D0FBD46170AA51F.IsSimpleOrSet`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Determines whether the specified query is a simple SELECT or a set operation without WHERE, GROUP BY, HAVING, or ORDER BY clauses, and with no table joins.
- Remarks: A simple query in this context is defined as one that does not use query modifiers, filtering, grouping, aggregation, ordering, or table joins. This method can be used to optimize processing or to apply special handling for basic queries.
- Returns: if the query is a simple SELECT or set operation with no WHERE, GROUP BY, HAVING, or ORDER BY clauses and no joins; otherwise, .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$2D0526ADB551921D1D0FBD46170AA51F.IsSingleColumn

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$2D0526ADB551921D1D0FBD46170AA51F.IsSingleColumn`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Determines whether the specified query selects exactly one column.
- Returns: if the query selects exactly one column; otherwise, .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$2D0526ADB551921D1D0FBD46170AA51F.IsSingleTableQueryWithoutJoins

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$2D0526ADB551921D1D0FBD46170AA51F.IsSingleTableQueryWithoutJoins`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Determine whether the specified query references a single table without a join.
- Returns: if the query's FROM clause contains exactly one table with no join; otherwise, .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$2D0526ADB551921D1D0FBD46170AA51F.IsTrivialFromWrapper

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$2D0526ADB551921D1D0FBD46170AA51F.IsTrivialFromWrapper`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Determines whether this query is a trivial pass-through wrapper around a single inner SELECT (i.e. SELECT * FROM (innerSelect) AS alias with no WHERE/GROUP BY/HAVING/ORDER BY/ DISTINCT/TAKE/SKIP/set operators, no joins, and no explicit projection columns). Such wrappers are inlined into innerSelect by the optimizer.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$585274A585B15432B89BA7E3C7366EFC.HasJoins

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$585274A585B15432B89BA7E3C7366EFC.HasJoins`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Determines whether the specified joined table includes any join clauses.
- Returns: if the joined table contains one or more join clauses; otherwise, .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$E67CEC5686BF77135195475981D92303.HasJoins

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQueryExtensions.<G>$E67CEC5686BF77135195475981D92303.HasJoins`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Determines whether the specified table source includes any join clauses.
- Returns: if the table source contains one or more joins; otherwise, .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SqlConcatExpression.PreserveNull

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SqlConcatExpression.PreserveNull`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: When false, null values replaced with empty string.

- Kind: Property
- Summary: This is internal API and is not intended for use by Linq To DB applications. It may change or be removed without further notice.

- Kind: Property
- Summary: This is internal API and is not intended for use by Linq To DB applications. It may change or be removed without further notice.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SqlParameter.NeedsCast

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SqlParameter.NeedsCast`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Enable parameter wrapping with type-cast. Ignored when is .

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SqlPredicate.ExprExpr.UnknownAsValue

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SqlPredicate.ExprExpr.UnknownAsValue`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Specify value, used as UNKNOWN value replacement on reduced predicate with UNKNOWN value erased. Replacement only applied when this property is not null.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SqlPredicate.IsTrue.WithNull

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SqlPredicate.IsTrue.WithNull`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: : evaluate predicate as is and preserve UNKNOWN (null) values if they produced : UNKNOWN values should be converted to FALSE : UNKNOWN values should be converted to TRUE

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SqlQueryExtension.Arguments

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SqlQueryExtension.Arguments`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets extension arguments.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SqlQueryExtension.BuilderType

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SqlQueryExtension.BuilderType`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets optional extension builder type. Must implement or interface.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SqlQueryExtension.Configuration

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SqlQueryExtension.Configuration`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets optional configuration, to which extension should be applied.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SqlQueryExtension.Scope

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SqlQueryExtension.Scope`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets extension apply scope/location.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SqlStatement.ParentStatement

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SqlStatement.ParentStatement`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Used internally for SQL Builder

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SqlTable.Expression

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SqlTable.Expression`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Custom SQL expression format string (used together with ) to transform to custom table expression. Arguments: {0}: {1}: {2+}: arguments from (with index adjusted by 2)

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SqlTableSource.UniqueKeys

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SqlTableSource.UniqueKeys`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Contains list of columns that build unique key for . Used in JoinOptimizer for safely removing sub-query from resulting SQL.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SqlValue.Value

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SqlValue.Value`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Provider specific value

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SqlValuesTable.Source

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SqlValuesTable.Source`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Source value expression.

## LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.Visitors.QueryElementVisitor.VisitMode

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.Visitors.QueryElementVisitor.VisitMode`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets default visitor inspection mode.

## LinqToDB.ITable<T>.DatabaseName

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.ITable`1.DatabaseName`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Logical database name component of the mapped table source, if specified.
- Remarks: May originate from mapping configuration. Interpreted by the active provider during SQL generation.

## LinqToDB.ITable<T>.SchemaName

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.ITable`1.SchemaName`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Logical schema name component of the mapped table source, if specified.
- Remarks: May originate from mapping configuration. Interpreted by the active provider during SQL generation.

## LinqToDB.ITable<T>.ServerName

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.ITable`1.ServerName`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Logical server name component of the mapped table source, if specified.
- Remarks: May originate from mapping configuration. Interpreted by the active provider during SQL generation.

## LinqToDB.ITable<T>.TableID

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.ITable`1.TableID`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Optional logical identifier for this table source within a query.
- Remarks: Used with TableID(...) to reference a table in SQL generation via Sql.TableAlias, Sql.TableName, or Sql.TableSpec (e.g., in provider-specific hints). Those helpers (Sql.TableAlias, Sql.TableName, and Sql.TableSpec) resolve generated SQL identifiers; TableID itself is only the logical lookup key. This identifier exists only within the translation scope of the query and is not a database object identifier.

## LinqToDB.ITable<T>.TableName

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.ITable`1.TableName`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Logical table name used during SQL generation.
- Remarks: May originate from mapping configuration and is interpreted by the active provider. It does not necessarily correspond to a physical database object name without provider-specific context.

## LinqToDB.ITable<T>.TableOptions

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.ITable`1.TableOptions`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Table-level translation flags affecting SQL generation and DDL behavior.
- Remarks: Interpreted by the configured provider. Translation-time only.

## LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.ModeConfig.IsServerSideOnly

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.ModeConfig.IsServerSideOnly`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: When (default), allows to bail out gracefully (return ) on a translation failure of any argument / item / value expression — but only when the caller passed isExpression: true to Build. Set to for strict server-side-only aggregates that must error rather than fall through to a partial-translation cascade.

## LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.ModeConfig.ItemTransform

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.ModeConfig.ItemTransform`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Plain-mode hook: rewrite each unpacked item LINQ-expression before SQL translation.

## LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.ModeConfig.ValueTransform

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.ModeConfig.ValueTransform`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Aggregate-mode hook: rewrite the per-row value LINQ-expression before SQL translation.

## LinqToDB.LinqExtensions.ProcessSourceQueryable

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.LinqExtensions.ProcessSourceQueryable`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets callback for preprocessing query before execution. Useful for intercepting queries.

## LinqToDB.LinqOptions.CacheSlidingExpiration

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.CacheSlidingExpiration`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Specifies timeout when query will be evicted from cache since last execution of query. Default value is 1 hour.

## LinqToDB.LinqOptions.CompareNulls

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.CompareNulls`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: If set to nullable fields would be checked for IS NULL in Equal/NotEqual comparisons. If set to comparisons are compiled straight to equivalent SQL operators, which consider nulls values as not equal. is a backward compatible option that works mostly as , but sniffs parameters value and changes = into IS NULL when parameters are null. Comparisons to literal null are always compiled into IS NULL. This affects: Equal, NotEqual, Not Contains Default value: . public class MyEntity { public int? Value; } db.MyEntity.Where(e => e.Value != 10) from e1 in db.MyEntity join e2 in db.MyEntity on e1.Value equals e2.Value select e1 var filter = new [] {1, 2, 3}; db.MyEntity.Where(e => ! filter.Contains(e.Value)) Would be converted to next queries under : SELECT Value FROM MyEntity WHERE Value IS NULL OR Value != 10 SELECT e1.Value FROM MyEntity e1 INNER JOIN MyEntity e2 ON e1.Value = e2.Value OR (e1.Value IS NULL AND e2.Value IS NULL) SELECT Value FROM MyEntity WHERE Value IS NULL OR NOT Value IN (1, 2, 3)

## LinqToDB.LinqOptions.ConcatenateOrderBy

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.ConcatenateOrderBy`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Controls behavior, when LINQ query chain contains multiple or calls: - if - non-first OrderBy* call will be treated as ThenBy* call; - if - OrderBy* call will discard sort specifications, added by previous OrderBy* and ThenBy* calls. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.LinqOptions.Default

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.Default`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets default instance.

## LinqToDB.LinqOptions.DisableQueryCache

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.DisableQueryCache`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Used to disable LINQ expressions caching for queries. This cache reduces time, required for query parsing but have several side-effects: - cached LINQ expressions could contain references to external objects as parameters, which could lead to memory leaks if those objects are not used anymore by other code - cache access synchronization could lead to bigger latencies than it saves. Default value: . It is not recommended to enable this option as it could lead to severe slowdown. Better approach will be to use scope around queries, that produce severe memory leaks you need to fix. More details.

## LinqToDB.LinqOptions.EnableContextSchemaEdit

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.EnableContextSchemaEdit`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: If , user could add new mappings to context mapping schemas (). Otherwise, will be generated on locked mapping schema edit attempt. It is not recommended to enable this option as it has performance implications. Proper approach is to create single instance once, configure mappings for it and use this instance for all context instances. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.LinqOptions.GenerateExpressionTest

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.GenerateExpressionTest`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Enables generation of test class for each LINQ query, executed while this option is enabled. This option could be useful for issue reporting, when you need to provide reproducible case. Test file will be placed to linq2db subfolder of temp folder and exact file path will be logged to data connection tracing infrastructure. See for more details. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.LinqOptions.GuardGrouping

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.GuardGrouping`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Controls behavior of LINQ query, which ends with GroupBy call. - if - will be thrown for such queries; - if - eager loading used. Default value: . More details.

## LinqToDB.LinqOptions.IgnoreEmptyUpdate

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.IgnoreEmptyUpdate`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Controls behavior of linq2db when there is no updateable fields in Update query: - if - query not executed and Update operation returns 0 as number of affected records; - if - will be thrown. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.LinqOptions.KeepDistinctOrdered

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.KeepDistinctOrdered`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Allows SQL generation to automatically transform SELECT DISTINCT value FROM Table ORDER BY date Into GROUP BY equivalent if syntax is not supported Default value: .

## LinqToDB.LinqOptions.LinqToDB#Internal#Options#IOptionSet#Default

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.LinqToDB#Internal#Options#IOptionSet#Default`
- Kind: Property

## LinqToDB.LinqOptions.OptimizeJoins

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.OptimizeJoins`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: If enabled, linq2db will try to reduce number of generated SQL JOINs for LINQ query. Attempted optimizations: - removes duplicate joins by unique target table key; - removes self-joins by unique key; - removes left joins if joined table is not used in query. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.LinqOptions.ParameterizeTakeSkip

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.ParameterizeTakeSkip`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Enables Take/Skip parameterization. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.LinqOptions.PreferApply

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.PreferApply`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Used to generate CROSS APPLY or OUTER APPLY if possible. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.LinqOptions.PreferExistsForScalar

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.PreferExistsForScalar`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: If , EXISTS operator will be generated instead of IN operator for scalar values. SELECT Value FROM MyEntity e WHERE EXISTS(SELECT * FROM MyEntity2 e2 WHERE e2.Value = e.Value) vs SELECT Value FROM MyEntity e WHERE Value IN (SELECT Value FROM MyEntity2 e2) Default value: .

## LinqToDB.LinqOptions.PreloadGroups

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.PreloadGroups`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Controls how group data for LINQ queries ended with GroupBy will be loaded: - if - group data will be loaded together with main query, resulting in 1 + N queries, where N - number of groups; - if - group data will be loaded when you call enumerator for specific group . Default value: .

## LinqToDB.LinqOptions.TraceMapperExpression

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.TraceMapperExpression`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Enables logging of generated mapping expression to data connection tracing infrastructure. See for more details. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.AliasName

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.AliasName`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets alias for association. Used in SQL generation process.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.AssociationSetterExpression

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.AssociationSetterExpression`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Specifies a setter expression. If is set, it will be used to set the storage member when using LoadWith(). Action takes two parameters: the storage member and the value to assign to it. Expression<Action<SomeContainerType,SomeOtherEntity>> setContainerValue; setContainerValue = (container, value) => container.Value = value;

## LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.AssociationSetterExpressionMethod

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.AssociationSetterExpressionMethod`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Specifies static property or method without parameters, that returns a setter expression. If is set, it will be used to set the storage member when using LoadWith(). Result of method should be Action which takes two parameters: the storage member and the value to assign to it. public class SomeEntity { [Association(SetExpressionMethod = nameof(OtherImpl), CanBeNull = true)] public SomeOtherEntity Other { get; set; } public static Expression<Action<SomeContainerType,SomeOtherEntity>> OtherImpl() { return (container, value) => container.Value = value; } }

## LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.CanBeNull

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.CanBeNull`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Defines type of join: - inner join for CanBeNull = false; - outer join for CanBeNull = true. When using Configuration.UseNullableTypesMetadata, the default value for associations (cardinality 1) is derived from nullability. Otherwise the default value is (for collections and when option is disabled).

## LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.ExpressionPredicate

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.ExpressionPredicate`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Specifies static property or method without parameters, that returns join predicate expression. This predicate will be used together with / join keys, if they are specified. Predicate expression lambda function takes two parameters: this record and other record and returns boolean result.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.OtherKey

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.OtherKey`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets comma-separated list of association key members on another side of association. Those keys will be used for join predicate generation and must be compatible with keys. You must specify keys it you do not use custom predicate (see ).

## LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.Predicate

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.Predicate`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Specifies predicate expression. This predicate will be used together with / join keys, if they are specified. Predicate expression lambda function takes two parameters: this record and other record and returns boolean result.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.QueryExpression

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.QueryExpression`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Specifies query expression. If is set, other association keys are ignored. Lambda function takes two parameters: this record, IDataContext and returns IQueryable result. Expression<Func<SomeEntity, IDataContext, IQueryable<SomeOtherEntity>>> associationQuery; associationQuery = (e, db) => db.GetTable<SomeOtherEntity>().Where(se => se.Id == e.Id);

## LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.QueryExpressionMethod

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.QueryExpressionMethod`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Specifies static property or method without parameters, that returns IQueryable expression. If is set, other association keys are ignored. Result of query method should be lambda which takes two parameters: this record, IDataContext and returns IQueryable result. public class SomeEntity { [Association(ExpressionQueryMethod = nameof(OtherImpl), CanBeNull = true)] public SomeOtherEntity Other { get; set; } public static Expression<Func<SomeEntity, IDataContext, IQueryable<SomeOtherEntity>>> OtherImpl() { return (e, db) => db.GetTable<SomeOtherEntity>().Where(se => se.Id == e.Id); } }

## LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.Storage

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.Storage`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Specify name of property or field to store association value, loaded using method. When not specified, current association member will be used.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.ThisKey

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.ThisKey`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets comma-separated list of association key members on this side of association. Those keys will be used for join predicate generation and must be compatible with keys. You must specify keys it you do not use custom predicate (see ).

## LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationDescriptor.AliasName

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationDescriptor.AliasName`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets alias for association. Used in SQL generation process.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationDescriptor.AssociationSetterExpression

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationDescriptor.AssociationSetterExpression`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets optional setter expression.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationDescriptor.AssociationSetterExpressionMethod

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationDescriptor.AssociationSetterExpressionMethod`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets optional setter method source property or method.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationDescriptor.CanBeNull

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationDescriptor.CanBeNull`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets join type, generated for current association. If , association will generate outer join, otherwise - inner join.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationDescriptor.ExpressionPredicate

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationDescriptor.ExpressionPredicate`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets optional predicate expression source property or method.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationDescriptor.ExpressionQuery

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationDescriptor.ExpressionQuery`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets optional query expression.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationDescriptor.ExpressionQueryMethod

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationDescriptor.ExpressionQueryMethod`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets optional query method source property or method.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationDescriptor.MappingSchema

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationDescriptor.MappingSchema`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets MappingSchema for current descriptor.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationDescriptor.MemberInfo

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationDescriptor.MemberInfo`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets association member (field, property or method).

## LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationDescriptor.OtherKey

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationDescriptor.OtherKey`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets list of names of to (other) key members. Could be empty, if association has predicate expression.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationDescriptor.Predicate

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationDescriptor.Predicate`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets optional predicate expression.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationDescriptor.Storage

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationDescriptor.Storage`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets optional association value storage field or property name. Used with LoadWith.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationDescriptor.ThisKey

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationDescriptor.ThisKey`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets list of names of from (this) key members. Could be empty, if association has predicate expression.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAliasAttribute.MemberName

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAliasAttribute.MemberName`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets the name of target property or field.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.CanBeNull

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.CanBeNull`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets whether a column can contain NULL values.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.CreateFormat

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.CreateFormat`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Custom template for column definition in create table SQL expression, generated using methods. Template accepts following string parameters: {0} - column name;{1} - column type;{2} - NULL specifier;{3} - identity specification.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.DataType

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.DataType`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets linq2db type for column. Default value: default type, defined for member type in mapping schema.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.DbType

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.DbType`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets the name of the database column type. Default value: default type, defined for member type in mapping schema.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.IsColumn

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.IsColumn`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets flag that tells that current member should be included into mapping. Use NonColumnAttribute instead as a shorthand. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.IsDiscriminator

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.IsDiscriminator`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets whether a column contains a discriminator value for a LINQ to DB inheritance hierarchy. for more details. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.IsIdentity

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.IsIdentity`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets whether a column contains values that the database auto-generates. Also see .

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.IsPrimaryKey

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.IsPrimaryKey`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets whether this class member represents a column that is part or all of the primary key of the table. Also see .

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.Length

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.Length`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets the length of the database column. Default value: value, defined for member type in mapping schema.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.MemberName

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.MemberName`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets the name of mapped member. When applied to class or interface, should contain name of property of field. If column mapped to a property or field of composite object, should contain a path to that member using dot as separator. public class Address { public string City { get; set; } public string Street { get; set; } public int Building { get; set; } } [Column("city", "Residence.City")] [Column("user_name", "Name")] public class User { public string Name; [Column("street", ".Street")] [Column("building_number", MemberName = ".Building")] public Address Residence { get; set; } }

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.Name

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.Name`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets the name of a column in database. If not specified, member name will be used.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.Order

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.Order`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Specifies the order of the field in table creation. Positive values first (ascending), then unspecified (arbitrary), then negative values (ascending).

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.Precision

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.Precision`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets the precision of the database column. Default value: value, defined for member type in mapping schema.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.PrimaryKeyOrder

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.PrimaryKeyOrder`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets the Primary Key order. See for more details.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.Scale

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.Scale`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets the Scale of the database column. Default value: value, defined for member type in mapping schema.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.SkipOnEntityFetch

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.SkipOnEntityFetch`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets whether a column must be explicitly defined in a Select statement to be fetched. If , a "SELECT *"-ish statement won't retrieve this column. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.SkipOnInsert

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.SkipOnInsert`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets whether a column is insertable. This flag will affect only insert operations with implicit columns specification like method and will be ignored when user explicitly specifies value for this column.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.SkipOnUpdate

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.SkipOnUpdate`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets whether a column is updatable. This flag will affect only update operations with implicit columns specification like method and will be ignored when user explicitly specifies value for this column.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.Storage

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.Storage`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets or sets a storage property or field to hold the value from a column. Could be usefull e.g. in combination of private storage field and getter-only mapping property.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.CanBeNull

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.CanBeNull`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets whether a column can contain null values.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.ColumnName

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.ColumnName`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets the name of a column in database. If not specified, value will be used.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.CreateFormat

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.CreateFormat`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Custom template for column definition in create table SQL expression, generated using methods. Template accepts following string parameters: - {0} - column name; - {1} - column type; - {2} - NULL specifier; - {3} - identity specification.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.DataType

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.DataType`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets LINQ to DB type for column.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.DbType

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.DbType`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets the name of the database column type.

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.EntityDescriptor`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets Entity descriptor.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.HasInheritanceMapping

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.HasInheritanceMapping`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates that owning entity included in inheritance mapping.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.HasValuesToSkipOnInsert

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.HasValuesToSkipOnInsert`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets whether the column has specific values that should be skipped on insert.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.HasValuesToSkipOnUpdate

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.HasValuesToSkipOnUpdate`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets whether the column has specific values that should be skipped on update.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.IsDiscriminator

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.IsDiscriminator`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets whether a column contains a discriminator value for a LINQ to DB inheritance hierarchy. for more details. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.IsIdentity

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.IsIdentity`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets whether a column contains values that the database auto-generates.

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.IsPrimaryKey

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.IsPrimaryKey`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets whether this member represents a column that is part or all of the primary key of the table. Also see .

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.Length`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets the length of the database column.

- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets MappingSchema for current ColumnDescriptor.

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.MemberName`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets the name of mapped member. When applied to class or interface, should contain name of property of field. If column is mapped to a property or field of composite object, should contain a path to that member using dot as separator. public class Address { public string City { get; set; } public string Street { get; set; } public int Building { get; set; } } [Column("city", "Residence.Street")] [Column("user_name", "Name")] public class User { public string Name; [Column("street", ".Street")] [Column("building_number", MemberName = ".Building")] public Address Residence { get; set; } }

## LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.MemberType

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.MemberType`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets type of column mapping member (field or property).

- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets the precision of the database column.

- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets the Scale of the database column.

- Summary: Gets whether a column must be explicitly defined in a Select statement to be fetched. If , a "SELECT *"-ish statement won't retrieve this column. Default value: .

- Kind: Property
- Summary: Gets whether a column is insertable. This flag will affect only insert operations with implicit columns specification like method and will be ignored when user explicitly specifies value for this column.

Otherwise, all supported members of scalar type will be used: - public instance fields and properties; - explicit interface implementation properties. Also see and .

If column is mapped to a property or field of composite object, should contain a path to that member using dot as separator. public class Address { public string City { get; set; } public string Street { get; set; } public int Building { get; set; } } [Column("city", "Residence.Street")] [Column("user_name", "Name")] public class User { public string Name; [Column("street", ".Street")] [Column("building_number", MemberName = ".Building")] public Address Residence { get; set; } }

- Summary: Gets or sets column mapping rules for current class or interface. If , properties and fields should be marked with one of those attributes to be used for mapping: - ; - ; - ; - . Otherwise all supported members of scalar type will be used: - public instance fields and properties; - explicit interface implementation properties. Also see and . Default value: .

## LinqToDB.Sql.CurrentTimestamp

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Sql.CurrentTimestamp`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Server-side-only equivalent of the ANSI SQL CURRENT_TIMESTAMP keyword. Produces the same SQL as on every provider — see that member for the per-provider mapping. Use for a dual-mode variant evaluable on the client, or for a UTC-specific variant.

## LinqToDB.Sql.CurrentTimestamp2

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Sql.CurrentTimestamp2`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Dual-mode counterpart of : emits the same server-side SQL but is also evaluable in-process. When evaluated client-side, returns .
- Returns: Current date and time as reported by the server (or client-side).

- Kind: Property
- Summary: If , this expression represents an aggregate result Examples would be SUM(),COUNT().

## LinqToDB.Sql.ExpressionAttribute.IsNullable

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Sql.ExpressionAttribute.IsNullable`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Used to determine whether the return type should be treated as something that can be null If CanBeNull is not explicitly set. Default is , which will be treated as

## LinqToDB.Sql.ExpressionAttribute.IsPredicate

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Sql.ExpressionAttribute.IsPredicate`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: If the expression is treated as a Predicate And when used in a Where clause will not have an added comparison to 'true' in the database.

## LinqToDB.Sql.ExpressionAttribute.IsPure

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Sql.ExpressionAttribute.IsPure`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: If , it notifies SQL Optimizer that expression returns same result if the same values/parameters are used. It gives optimizer additional information how to simplify query. For example ORDER BY PureFunction("Str") can be removed because PureFunction function uses constant value. For example Random function is NOT Pure function because it returns different result all time. But expression is Pure in case of executed query. is also Pure function because it returns the same result with the same parameters.

## LinqToDB.Sql.ExpressionAttribute.IsWindowFunction

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Sql.ExpressionAttribute.IsWindowFunction`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: If , this expression represents a Window Function Examples would be SUM() OVER(), COUNT() OVER().

## LinqToDB.Sql.ExpressionAttribute.Precedence

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Sql.ExpressionAttribute.Precedence`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Determines the priority of the expression in evaluation. Refer to .

## LinqToDB.Sql.ExpressionAttribute.PreferServerSide

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.Sql.ExpressionAttribute.PreferServerSide`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: If a greater effort will be made to execute the expression on the DB server instead of in .NET.

- Summary: Gets default instance.

## LinqToDB.SqlOptions.DefaultNullsPosition

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.SqlOptions.DefaultNullsPosition`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Default position of NULL values in an ORDER BY clause for ordering keys that do not specify a explicitly. When set to or , it is applied to every such key (and emulated for providers without native NULLS FIRST/NULLS LAST support). A position specified explicitly on a key always takes precedence — including an explicit , which opts that key out of the default and uses the provider's natural null ordering. Default value: (use the provider's default null ordering).

## LinqToDB.SqlOptions.EnableConstantExpressionInOrderBy

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.SqlOptions.EnableConstantExpressionInOrderBy`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: If , linq2db will allow any constant expressions in ORDER BY clause. Default value: .

## LinqToDB.SqlOptions.GenerateFinalAliases

- XML member: `P:LinqToDB.SqlOptions.GenerateFinalAliases`
- Kind: Property
- Summary: Indicates whether SQL Builder should generate aliases for final projection. It is not required for correct query processing but simplifies SQL analysis. Default value: . For the query var query = from child in db.Child select new { TrackId = child.ChildID, }; When property is SELECT [child].[ChildID] as [TrackId] FROM [Child] [child] Otherwise alias will be removed SELECT [child].[ChildID] FROM [Child] [child]

## LinqToDB.CreateTableOptions

- XML member: `T:LinqToDB.CreateTableOptions`
- Kind: Type
- Summary: Options for creating a table.
- Parameters:
  - `TableName`: Optional name of table. If not specified, value from mapping will be used.
  - `DatabaseName`: Optional name of table's database. If not specified, value from mapping will be used.
  - `SchemaName`: Optional name of table schema/owner. If not specified, value from mapping will be used.
  - `ServerName`: Optional name of linked server. If not specified, value from mapping will be used.
  - `StatementHeader`: Replacement for "CREATE TABLE table_name" header. Header is a template with {0} parameter for table name.
  - `StatementFooter`: SQL appended to the generated create table statement.
  - `TableOptions`: Optional Table options. Default is .
  - `DefaultNullable`: Defines how columns nullability flag should be generated: - - generate only NOT NULL for non-nullable fields. Missing nullability information treated as NULL by database. - - generate only NULL for nullable fields. Missing nullability information treated as NOT NULL by database. - - explicitly generate NULL and NOT NULL for all columns. Default value: .

  - `StatementFooter`: SQL appended to the generated create table statement.
  - `TableOptions`: Optional Table options. Default is .
  - `DefaultNullable`: Defines how columns nullability flag should be generated: - - generate only NOT NULL for non-nullable fields. Missing nullability information treated as NULL by database. - - generate only NULL for nullable fields. Missing nullability information treated as NOT NULL by database. - - explicitly generate NULL and NOT NULL for all columns. Default value: .
